The optical and X-ray emission of NGC 3077 and NGC 4449 is surrounded by an extended \ envelope which can be ascribed to tidal debris due to a current interaction see, e.g., yun94,hun98 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4214 as a nearby starbursting dwarf, separating HII/PDR and diffuse dust emission and finding standard dust properties without submillimeter excess.

quotes.quote: We have carried out a detailed modelling of the dust heating and emission in the nearby, starbursting dwarf galaxy NGC 4214. Due to its proximity and the great wealth of data from the UV to the millimeter range (from GALEX, HST, Spitzer , Herschel, Planck and IRAM) it is possible to separately model the emission from HII regions and their associated photodissociation regions (PDRs) and the emission from diffuse dust. Furthermore, most model parameters can be directly determined from the data leaving very few free parameters. We can fit both the emission from HII+PDR regions and the diffuse emission in NGC 4214 with these models with "normal" dust properties and realistic parameters.

In the present work we model the dust emission of the nearby ( D=2.9 Mpc) starbursting dwarf galaxy NGC 4214. Due to its proximity and the large amount of ancillary data it is possible to apply physical models that take into account the full radiation transfer and constrain their input parameters. In the present contribution we describe the general outline of our work and the results, whereas a more detailed description of the available data, the data reduction and the determination of the model input parameters is presented in the contribution of Hermelo et al. in this volume. NGC 4214 is an irregular galaxy, dominated by two bright, young star-forming (SF) regions in its center (called NW and SE in this work).

sources.summary: NGC 4214 is described as a nearby dwarf starburst with recent central star formation, two HII complexes containing young massive clusters, and a young stellar population embedded in an older stellar disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 4214 is a nearby dwarf IAB(s)m galaxy (de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991) in the low-redshift CVn I Cloud (Sandage Bedke 1994) located at a distance of 3.04 0.05 Mpc (Dalcanton et al. 2009). It has a mass similar to that of the Large Magellanic Cloud (LMC; M=5 10^ 9 M_ ; Karachentsev et al. 2004) and a moderately low metal-content ( 1.6< [ M/H ] <0 ; Williams et al. 2011 hereafter W11). NGC 4214 is characterized by an intense recent star-formation activity, as shown by the presence of two HII star forming complexes located in its central region where several Young Massive Clusters (YMCs) are evident as H emitting peaks (Fanelli et al. 1997). The young stellar population is embedded in a smooth disk of old stars which account for a significant fraction (up to 75 ) of the total stellar mass (W11).

The largest star forming complex is located in the north-western region of NGC 4214 (referred as 4214-I). It is formed by a number of star clusters and has a complex H structure dominated by the presence of two cavities. Its ultraviolet flux is dominated by an extended YMC (I-As) extending for 70 pc located in a heart-shaped H cavity.

sources.summary: The study models NGC 4214 as a face-on, strongly warped disk with multiple spiral arms and an uncertain rotation curve affected by dynamical-center choice.

quotes.quote: Nine galaxies in our sample (50 ) have a regularly rotating disk: NGC 1705, NGC 2366, NGC 4068, NGC 4214, NGC 6789, UGC 4483, I Zw 18, I Zw 36, and SBS 1415+437. For these objects, we derived rotation curves following a procedure similar to Swaters2009 . As a first step, we obtained initial estimates of the geometrical parameters and of the rotation curve by fitting a tilted-ring model to the VF Begeman1987 .

NGC 4214 has a disk with multiple spiral arms. Intriguingly, the optical and spiral arms wind in opposite directions (clockwise and counter-clockwise, respectively). The disk is close to face-on and strongly warped, thus the rotation curve is uncertain. In the inner parts, our rotation curve rises more steeply than the one derived by Swaters2009 ; the difference seems to be due to a different choice of the dynamical center (see the PV-diagram in Swaters2009 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4214 THINGS/VLA data to study imaging-parameter effects, finding central flux growth followed by decreasing cumulative flux at larger radii.

quotes.quote: NGC 4214 and NGC 5055 observations and Data In the following, we present different data sets of NGC 4214 and NGC 5055 observations. The reason for this selection is that these galaxies are located at different distances and they have substantial differences in their morphology, extent, and physical parameters 2009AJ....137.4670L . Table tb:n4214_n5055_obs_par presents the physical and observational parameters of these two galaxies.

For the NGC 4214 data sets the measured total flux increases in the central regions. The maximum value is measured at a radius of about 8 arcminutes for all 4 data sets. At this radius the bulk of the emission from the galaxy is measured. For the larger radii the cumulative flux decreases.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC4214 radio continuum data to constrain dark-matter annihilation, adopting its distance, magnetic field, angular size, radiation field, and diffusion assumptions.

quotes.quote: We have examined some archival galactic radio continuum spectral data and we have found a very good candidate - NGC 4214 galaxy - to do the analysis. The radio continuum data can be found in . The thermal contribution can be modeled by S_ th =20( /0.1 GHz )^ 0.1 mJy . Therefore, we can obtain the non-thermal radio flux data S_ nth and their uncertainties (see Table 1). The non-thermal radio spectral index is very close to a constant so that it is very good for analysis.

The distance to the galaxy is D=2.94 Mpc and the average uniform magnetic field strength is B 8 G . The angular size of the galaxy is smaller than 0.2^ . Note that the radio flux data we considered are integrated flux which represent the total emissions of the galaxy.

sources.summary: NGC 4214 is presented as a slower-rotating, weakly barred irregular with little polarized emission, a mostly random 4 microgauss total field, and no strong large-scale field.

quotes.quote: NGC 4214 has a slower rotation rate and weaker bar than NGC 4449 and a higher star formation rate per unit area. From the 6 cm VLA image of the continuum emission from this galaxy, we determined the amount of synchrotron emission using images supplied by Deidre Hunter to estimate the thermal contribution to the 6 cm emission. There is very little polarized emission associated this galaxy. The total field in this galaxy is about 4 G and it is mostly random.

The mostly random magnetic field structure of NGC 4214 closely resembles the magnetic field structure of NGC 6822 and IC 10. This galaxy probably does not have a strong enough rotation rate or bar to sustain a large-scale field.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4214's central star-forming region, describing it as a nearby dwarf starburst galaxy with intense recent star formation, young massive clusters, two H II complexes, and resolved stellar populations.

quotes.quote: We present the results of deep high resolution imaging performed with ACS/HRC@HST in the most active region of the nearby starburst galaxy NGC 4214. We resolved the stellar populations of five Young Massive Clusters and their surrounding galactic field. The star formation history of this region is characterized by two main bursts occurred within the last 500 Myr, with the oldest episode spread out across an area larger than that covered by the most recent one.

NGC 4214 is a nearby dwarf IAB(s)m galaxy (de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991) in the low-redshift CVn I Cloud (Sandage Bedke 1994) located at a distance of 3.04 0.05 Mpc (Dalcanton et al. 2009). It has a mass of M 5 10^ 9 M_ (Karachentsev et al. 2004) and a low metal-content ( 1.6< [ M/H ] <0 ; Williams et al. 2011 hereafter W11). NGC 4214 is characterized by an intense recent star-formation activity, as shown by the presence of two HII star forming complexes located in its central region (Fanelli et al. 1997).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4214 as a THINGS profile example, describing its narrow linewidth, unresolved spectral wiggles, limited model smoothing, and below-average residual factor.

quotes.quote: A good example is the global spectrum of NGC 4214, which is shown together with a model fit in Fig. fig_H . At first inspection it is not clear why the model has not better fitted the seemingly regular double-horned profile. However, the linewidth of this galaxy is, at 56 km s ^ 1 , relatively small: less than 5 times the `sigma' width of the turbulent broadening, which here is 12 km s ^ 1 . Further consultation of Table tab_O shows that the fraction of solid rotation fitted (which tends to reduce the depth of the valley between the horns) is small - consistent with zero. In fact the fitting procedure has chosen the sharpest and deepest possible double-horned profile which, after smoothing by the turbulent-broadening Gaussian, is consistent with the line slopes.

With this amount of smoothing it is not possible for the model to follow deviations over velocity scales less than 10 km s ^ 1 , as occurs with NGC 4214. These fluctuations only fool the eye into assuming a regularity which isn't in fact there, because NGC 4214 is too narrow to make obvious the actually random nature of its wiggles; in contrast to the spectrum of NGC 925, for example (shown in the same figure). It is worth observing too that, although the wiggles in NGC 4214 do rather offend the eye, the J factor for this galaxy is only 2.3 , well below the average seen in Fig. fig_T .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4214 as a lopsided dwarf with a warped spiral-patterned disk, northwest extension, and a nearby gas-poor companion DDO 113.

quotes.quote: Other galaxies, instead, show lopsided morphologies, characterized by minor asymmetries and/or extensions in the outer parts (NGC 2366, NGC 4068, NGC 4214, UGC 6456, UGC 9128, UGC 4483, and SBS 1415+437).

NGC 4214 has a disk with a well-defined spiral pattern. The disk is strongly warped (see LVF14) and slightly more extended to the North-West. NGC 4214 is in the CVn I cloud and has a small companion galaxy (DDO 113) at a projected distance of 8 kpc. DDO 113 likely is a gas-poor spheroidal Kaisin2008 . This object, indeed, is within the field-of-view of the VLA but no emission is detected within the covered velocity range.

sources.summary: The paper uses resolved HST stellar photometry in NGC 4214 to infer ionizing fluxes and measure a spatially varying escape fraction with a global value above previous zero estimates.

quotes.quote: We demonstrate a new method for measuring the escape fraction of ionizing photons using Hubble Space Telescope imaging of resolved stars in NGC 4214, a local analog of high-redshift starburst galaxies that are thought to be responsible for cosmic reionization. Specifically, we forward model the UV through near-IR spectral energy distributions of 83,000 resolved stars to infer their individual ionizing flux outputs. We constrain the local escape fraction by comparing the number of ionizing photons produced by stars to the number that are either absorbed by dust or consumed by ionizing the surrounding neutral hydrogen in individual star-forming regions.

We find substantial spatial variation in the escape fraction (0--40 ). Integrating over the entire galaxy yields a global escape fraction of 25 16 15 . This value is much higher than previous escape fractions of zero reported for this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents a Spitzer IRAC mid-infrared point-source catalog for NGC 4214, reporting 1185 detected sources and common completeness limits corresponding to sources brighter than M_ 3.6 = -10.

quotes.quote: We present Spitzer IRAC mid-infrared point source catalogs for mosaics covering the fields of the nearby ( 4 Mpc) galaxies NGC 55, NGC 253, NGC 2366, NGC 4214, and NGC 5253. We detect a total of 20159 sources in these five fields. The individual galaxy point source breakdown is the following: NGC 55, 8746 sources; NGC 253, 9001 sources; NGC 2366, 505 sources; NGC 4214, 1185 sources; NGC 5253, 722 sources. The completeness limits of the full catalog vary with bandpass and were found to be m_ 3.6 =18.0 , m_ 4.5 =17.5 , m_ 5.8 =17.0 , and m_ 8.0 =16.5 mag. For all galaxies, this corresponds to detection of point sources brighter than M_ 3.6 =-10 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4214 ionized-gas kinematics, mapping its star-forming complexes, rotation curve, superbubbles, and supernova remnants in this nearby dwarf starburst galaxy.

quotes.quote: We analysed the ionised gas kinematics of the dwarf galaxy NGC 4214 using high resolution Fabry-Perot interferometry observations and present a set of narrowband images in the H , [ S ii ] 6717 , [ N ii ] 6584 \ and [ O iii ] 5007 \ emission lines. The high-resolution Fabry-Perot observations of the H emission line, allowed us to derive the velocity field, the velocity dispersion , and the rotation curve of the galaxy. We also present for the first time, three-dimensional kinematic maps of the complexes NGC 4214-I and NGC 4214-II and analysed the kinematics of the ionised gas of two new superbubbles, as well as the supernova remnants previously detected in this galaxy by other authors, in radio, optical and X-ray emission.

NGC 4214 is a nearby 2.7 Mpc, derived from the galaxy distance from the tip of the red giant branch (TRGB) Drozdovsky2002, Lelli2014 irregular dwarf starburst galaxy also known as UGC 7278 and also classified as Magellanic type (NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED)). NGC 4214 has two main regions of star formation located in the central part of the galaxy; the northwest (NW) complex, also called NGC 4214-I, is a large complex of H II regions displaying a shell morphology and the southeast (SE) complex known as NGC 4214-II, formed mainly of compact knots see for example MacKenty2000,Fahrion2017,Choi2020 .

sources.summary: The paper presents HST UV-optical photometry and astrometry for the nearby dwarf starburst NGC 4214, deriving stellar and cluster properties and resolving young and old populations.

quotes.quote: We present the data and methods that we have used to perform a detailed UV--optical study of the nearby dwarf starburst galaxy NGC 4214 using multifilter HST/WFPC2+STIS photometry. We explain the process followed to obtain high--quality photometry and astrometry of the stellar and cluster populations of this galaxy. We describe the procedure used to transform magnitudes and colors into physical parameters using spectral energy distributions. The data show the existence of both young and old stellar populations that can be resolved at the distance of NGC 4214 (2.94 Mpc) and we perform a general description of those populations.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4214 as a low-metallicity starbursting dwarf, finding shell-like HII-region extinction, extended 850 micron emission, and spatial variation in its 24-to-850 ratio.

quotes.quote: We present a study of the extinction, traced by the Balmer decrement, in HII regions in the dwarf galaxies NGC 1569 and NGC 4214. We find that the large-scale extinction around the most prominent HII regions in both galaxies forms a shell in which locally the intrinsic extinction can adopt relatively high values ( A_V = 0.8 - 0.9 mag) despite the low metallicity and thus the low overall dust content.

We find in general a good agreement between all tracers, expect for the 850 \ emission in NGC 4214 which is more extended than the extinction and the other emissions. Whereas in NGC 1569 the dust emission at all wavelengths is very similar, NGC 4214 shows spatial variations in the 24-to-850 \ ratio.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4214 among low-velocity dwarf starbursts but concludes its larger gravitational potential prevents clouds from reaching the observed 40-50 km/s speeds.

quotes.quote: In particular, the Na D absorbers in the sample of (NGC 1569, NGC 4214, NGC 4449) have low velocities V_ 40-50 km s ^ 1 , which may be explained by the CC85 model combined with the RPA scenario and additional observational constraints on the diffuse X-ray emission from these systems. We search for cool cloud wind solutions in these systems over a wide range of and , and assuming cool cloud properties as follows: r_ 0 =2R , T_ =10^ 3 K, N_ H ^ =10^ 21 cm ^ 2 . We find that clouds in the three dwarf starbursts can never be accelerated to 40-50 km s ^ 1 if we use the fiducial destruction timescale in equation ( shtime1 ).

Finally, due to the large gravitational potential in NGC 4214 with a value of 100 km s ^ 1 ( Thronson88 ; Schwartz04 ; DOnghia08 ), clouds in NGC 4214 cannot be accelerated to the observed V_ 40-50 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4214 has central high-surface-brightness H-alpha emission tracing H I arms, expanding bubbles, and diffuse ionized gas extending outward from star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 4214 From Figure fig:ngc4214 , NGC 4214 has numerous high surface brightness \ emission in the central region and extending along a Northwest to Southeast axis. The high surface brightness \ emission in NGC 4214 presents a unique view of how well the \ distribution traces the \ in the spiral arm features of the galaxy. Many of the \ high surface brightness regions in the top right panel clearly follow the peak \ density structures, which are also the sites of recent star formation. Spectroscopically derived radial \ velocities using a Fabry-Per o t interferometer range for the central 4 4 field range from 260 310 km s ^ 1 and may be receding through the disk Garrido2004 , compared to a circular velocity of 79 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4214 as a dwarf barred irregular whose low inclination makes geometry uncertain and whose velocity maps show rough bar-aligned anisotropies.

quotes.quote: NGC 4214 NGC 4214 is a dwarf barred irregular galaxy . The TRM measurement of the inclination angle is highly fluctuating at small radii and becomes uncertain at large radii, resulting in large error bars (see Fig. NGC4214 ). This uncertainty also highly affects the kinemetry measurement of v_c(R) : the errors are too large to be meaningful.

sources.summary: Chandra and XMM-Newton observations of the dwarf starburst NGC 4214 reveal diffuse X-ray emission tied to central star formation, differing X-ray output from two complexes, two-temperature gas, and likely future blow-out.

quotes.quote: We present results from Chandra and XMM-Newton X-ray observations of NGC 4214, a nearby dwarf starburst galaxy containing several young regions of very active star-formation. Starburst regions are known to be associated with diffuse X-ray emission, and in this case the X-ray emission from the galaxy shows an interesting morphological structure within the galaxy, clearly associated with the central regions of active star-formation. Of the two main regions of star formation in this galaxy, X-ray emission associated with the older is identified whereas little is detected from the younger, providing an insight into the evolutionary process of the formation of superbubbles around young stellar clusters. The spectra of the diffuse emission from the galaxy can be fitted with a two temperature component thermal model with kT=0.14 keV and 0.52 keV, and analysis of this emission suggests that NGC 4214 will suffer a blow-out in the future.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4214's diffuse X-ray emission centred and slightly elongated north-south, smaller than the H distribution, with four point sources coincident with diffuse X-ray and H emission.

quotes.quote: Chandra observations revisited The extent of the diffuse X-ray emission centred on NGC 4214 is about 1 3 (corresponding to 1 kpc) and slightly elongated along the north-south axis (see Fig. fig:olay4214 a ). A second, smaller X-ray emitting region is found close to an \ region at the northern tip of this galaxy (Fig. fig:olay4214 a and [ b ] ), which, however, is not significant and might be an artefact from the adaptive smoothing process (note that this feature cannot be seen when smoothed with a Gaussian kernel of fixed size). The X-ray emitting region is much smaller than the \ distribution ( 6 8 3 2 ). Four point sources are detected coinciding with the diffuse X-ray and \ emission of NGC 4214.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4214 as a dwarf starburst galaxy hosting a 3.62-hour eclipsing X-ray binary, likely with luminosity up to 7 10^38.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of an eclipsing X-ray binary with a 3.62-hr period within 24 \ of the center of the dwarf starburst galaxy NGC 4214. The orbital period places interesting constraints on the nature of the binary, and allows for a few very different interpretations. The most likely possibility is that the source lies within NGC 4214 and has an X-ray luminosity, L_x , of up to 7 10^ 38 . In this case the binary may well be comprised of a naked He-burning donor star with a neutron-star accretor, though a stellar-mass black-hole accretor cannot be completely excluded.

sources.summary: The paper presents 7278 as strongly warped, with uncertain low-inclination rotation amplitudes and central H I that may counterrotate relative to the outer parts.

quotes.quote: 7278 has a strong kinematic warp, with the inclination changing from about 30^ in the central parts to about 10^ in the outer parts, and the position angle changing from 74^ to 90^ . Because of the low inclination, the actual values of the inclination, and hence the amplitude of the rotation curve, are uncertain. The \ in the central parts has a high velocity dispersion and seems to counterrotate with respect to the outer parts.

sources.summary: NGC4214 is characterized as a large late-type system with low star formation and dwarf-like properties; its outer disk has elevated dynamical surface density and a non-declining dynamical mass curve.

quotes.quote: The galaxies NGC628, NGC6946 and NGC3184 are grand design spirals with moderate star formation rates (SFRs) of values 1 to 5, but NGC4214 has a much lower SFR of 0.05 walter.etal.2008 .
The size, gas rich nature and SFR of NGC4214 is more akin to that of late type dwarf galaxies das.etal.2019 than large spirals.
From Figure 3 it is clear that _ dyn as traced by the H i velocity dispersion is at least 1.5 to 2 times the value of _ H i in the region R>R_ 25 for the galaxies NGC628, NGC3184 and NGC4214.

sources.summary: The model gives NGC 4214 a high inner gas Toomre parameter, viscous times exceeding depletion times beyond two disk scale lengths, and radial gas accretion able to maintain star formation.

quotes.quote: A high Toomre parameter Q_ gas 8 is found in the inner disks of NGC 2403, NGC 2967, NGC 4214, and NGC 7793 decreasing to Q_ gas 1.5 2 in the outer disks.
The viscous timescales exceed the gas depletion time in NGC 2403, NGC 2967, NGC 4214, and NGC 7793 beyond R 2 R _ d , the radius within which half of the galaxy's luminosity is contained.
For the low-mass galaxies, with M_* < 10^ 10 M _ , the model yields up to R 2 R_ d for NGC 4214, NGC 2403, and NGC 7793 and up to R R_ d for NGC 2976.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4214 as an exception to the massive-galaxy q trend, emphasizing that it is relatively small and has been classified as a dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: Figure 2 and Table 2 show that galaxies with more massive stellar masses (M(*) > 10 ^9 M _ ) have q values close to q 1 at R_ 25 and beyond (e.g. NGC 628 and NGC 6946). The exceptions are NGC 4214 and NGC 5236. Of these two galaxies, NGC 4214 is a relatively small galaxy and close to being a dwarf olling.1996 . In fact, based on its rotation velocity, stellar mass, and absolute B-band magnitude values, NGC 4214 has been classified as a dwarf galaxy by 2008AJ....136.2782L . But NGC 5236 is quite massive ( M(*) 6.9 10^ 10 M_ and so its low q value is quite surprising. For the less massive galaxies, a large fraction of which are LSB dwarfs, q is much smaller (Figure 2 and table 2). Six of them have q=0.2-0.3 (Holmberg1, M81dwfA, IC10, DDO75, DDO187 and UGC8833) and 3 of them have q=0.4-0.5 (Holmberg2, F564-V3, DDO46). The exception is IC1613 which has a relatively large q 1 value at R_ 25 .

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 4214 as a UV-slope outlier: its COS spectrum appears redder than its IUE spectrum, though the two spectra visually differ while remaining consistent within uncertainties.

quotes.quote: There are a few strong outliers from the _ IUE vs _ COS trend in Figure fig3 , namely (5) IRAS 08339+6517, (8) NGC 3125, (15) NGC 3690, (18) NGC 4214, and (23) NGC 5236.
On the other hand, the COS spectra for (8) NGC 3125 and (18) NGC 4214 seem to have redder slopes than their corresponding IUE spectra.
The COS and IUE spectra for NGC 4214 are somewhat different visually but agree within the uncertainties of the two spectra.

gmw_00000436.name: NGC 708
gmw_00000436.overview: NGC 708 is a cD brightest cluster galaxy in Abell 262 [arXiv:2403.12144] that hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:2403.12144] and a low-power radio source [arXiv:2111.02430]. The galaxy features a rotating circumnuclear molecular gas disk [arXiv:1902.09227] and a nearly edge-on central dust lane [arXiv:1206.5768, arXiv:0908.4391].

sources.summary: The paper reports a roughly 10^10 M_ black hole in NGC 708, a cD brightest cluster galaxy in Abell 262, finding it an outlier in most black-hole scaling relations with radius-dependent triaxial structure.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a (1.0 0.28) 10^ 10 M _ Supermassive Black Hole (BH) at the centre of NGC 708, the Brightest Cluster Galaxy of Abell 262.

NGC 708 is found to be an outlier in all the canonical scaling relations except for those linking the BH mass to the core properties. The galaxy mass-to-light ratio points to a Kroupa IMF rather than Salpeter, with this finding confirmed using photometry in two different bands. We perform this analysis using our novel triaxial Schwarzschild code to integrate orbits in a 5-dimensional space, using a semi-parametric deprojected light density to build the potential and non-parametric Line-of-Sight Velocity Distributions (LOSVDs) derived from long-slit spectra recently acquired at Large Binocular Telescope (LBT) to exploit the full information in the kinematic. We find that the galaxy geometry changes as a function of the radius going from prolate, nearly spherical in the central regions to triaxial at large radii, highlighting the need to go beyond constant shape profiles.

NGC 708 is the Brightest Cluster Galaxy (BCG) of the Abell 262 cluster. It is located at a distance of 68.48 Mpc and has an absolute magnitude M_ g' = -22.89 Matthias20 . The galaxy is commonly classified as cD (see e.g. Wegner12 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 708 as mildly triaxial, with a nearly edge-on central dust disk, twisted isophotes, and models that do not need a distinct dark halo.

quotes.quote: NGC 708. The strong dark stripe of NGC 708 appears to be consistent with a dust disk seen nearly edge-on as it runs roughly North-South through the center of the galaxy image. It extends out to 3 2 (1.1 kpc) on its South side and 5 8 (2.0 kpc) on the North side. Individual dark blobs can be seen along the edge of the band, particularly to the West side.

NGC 708. The inner and outer isophotes ( 2.5 ) have different orientations (Fig. fig:photometry ). The dynamical model was adjusted towards the galaxy center, where the stellar kinematics was measured (Fig. fig:kinematics ) and the largest stellar rotation is at =130^ . It vanishes at =40^ , indicating that the position angle of the galaxy's line of nodes is close to =130^ adopted for the dynamical model.
However, as is consistent with the stellar orientation at the corresponding radii we infer that NGC 708 is probably only mildly triaxial and that its dynamical mass is reliable.

sources.summary: The paper treats the Abell 262 central galaxy as an ALMA-observed massive elliptical and identifies it as a gas-rich outlier with low jet and continuum power.

quotes.quote: The list of the 9 remaining galaxies are shown in table tab:obs . We call the central galaxies by the names of their host clusters. The galaxy redshifts z are those determined by optical observations.

Abell 262 appears to be an outlier. While it has a large amount of circumnuclear molecular gas ( M_ mol ), both the jet power ( P_ cav ) and the mm/submm continuum luminosities ( L_ con and L_ 1.4 ) are small. This might be explained if the AGN activity has been gentle for a long time. Thus, there may be an additional factor that affects the activity other than the mass of circumnuclear molecular gas.

sources.summary: The paper examines the central galaxy in Abell 262, detecting compact stellar light and extended [OII] gas whose rotating gas is disturbed by jets.

quotes.quote: Abell 262 has a substantially lower redshift than our other targets, such that the KCWI FOV only covers the inner part of the central galaxy. The KCWI spectra cover rest-frame wavelengths between 3445 and 5118 . Fig. fig:flux:A262 shows the stellar flux map and the continuum-subtracted [ OII ] flux map for Abell 262 in the left panel and middle and right panels, respectively.

Much of the nebular gas and all of the molecular gas in Abell 262 is in rotation about the nucleus of the central galaxy along an E-W axis. Radio jets, oriented 25^ north of east, roughly align with the angular momentum axis of the disk. The velocity dispersion of the nebular gas sharply rises along the jets, as seen in Fig. fig:sig_radio:A262 , indicating that the jets are interacting with and disturbing the nebula. A more complete analysis of the gas and stellar velocity fields in Abell 262 will be presented in a future paper.

sources.summary: The paper presents multiwavelength imaging of radio-loud elliptical NGC 708, linking dust, ionized gas, infrared, radio, and X-ray structures and identifying cavity pairs near the nucleus.

quotes.quote: We present a multi-wavelength study of a nearby radio loud elliptical galaxy NGC 708, selected from the Bologna B2 sample of radio galaxies. We obtained optical broad band and narrow images from IGO 2m telescope (Pune, India). We supplement the multi-wavelength coverage of the observation by using X-ray data from Chandra, infrared data from 2MASS, Spitzer and WISE and optical image from DSS and HST.

From the derived maps it is evident that cool gas, dust, warm ionized H and hot X-ray gas are spatially associated with each other. We investigate the inner and outer photometric and kinematic properties of the galaxy using surface brightness profiles. From X-ray 2d beta model, unsharp masking, surface brightness profiles techniques, it is evident that pair of X-ray cavities are present in this system and which are 5.6 Kpc away from the central X-ray source.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 708 as a radio-luminous early-type galaxy whose jet may heat or expel hot gas, with its inner X-ray emission requiring two temperature components.

quotes.quote: We note that of the two sources that exhibit temperatures in excess of that required for the gas to escape the system (i.e., _ spec < 0.25 ), one galaxy (NGC 708) is among the most radio-luminous considered here with P_ 1.4 GHz 4 10^ 22  W/Hz. In NGC 708 the mechanical heating from the jet may be sufficient to expel the hot gas from the galaxy entirely. Indeed, in-depth studies of the cluster host, Abell 262, show a complex structure and cooling flow towards the central region surrounding NGC 708 with X-ray cavities inflated by radio lobes, suggesting a once powerful outflow (see Blanton:2004aa ).

In line with these results, we find that the X-ray emitting gas within R_e is better fit by two separate temperature components with T_1 2.0  keV and T_2 1.1  keV. The cooler component has a substantially lower luminosity by a factor 5-7 compared with the hot component.

sources.summary: The paper treats A262 as a rare disk-dominated central galaxy, with most molecular gas in a rotating circumnuclear disk plus a possible additional bipolar or non-circular component.

quotes.quote: By comparison, in A262 and Hydra A, the majority of the molecular gas is resolved in a rotating circumnuclear gas disk (eg. Prandoni07,Hatch07 ) and only 10 extends to larger radii. Disk-dominated sources are rare in our sample ( 15 ) with only these two clear examples.

ALMA observations of A262 show a similarly ordered circumnuclear disk from 200 to +200 centred on the AGN and oriented perpendicular to the radio jet axis (Fig. fig:A262maps ). The disk's velocity structure matches that observed in the ionized gas ( Hatch07 ) and is consistent with the IRAM 30 CO line profile ( Prandoni07 ).

Although the additional velocity component at the centre of A262 may similarly indicate a bipolar gas flow, this structure is aligned in projection with the rotating gas disk and is oriented perpendicular to the larger scale radio lobe axis and extended filament (Fig. fig:A262maps ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies B2 0149+35 with NGC 708 in Abell 262, linking excess central X-rays to a cooling flow and treating unresolved emission as an upper limit.

quotes.quote: B2 0149+35. In the longer, and more reliable, of the two OBIs (12.7ks), 207 counts were detected. The point-like contribution to the net emission from this source however, is not significant at the 95 per cent level when an F-test is performed. The detected counts are therefore taken as an upper limit on the point model emission. The shorter OBI was not used.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0149+35 as the brightest cluster galaxy in A262, noting dust in the nucleus, feedback with thermal gas, and unresolved VLBA emission.

quotes.quote: 0149+35 (NGC 708) -- This galaxy is the BCG of the cooling cluster A262. At optical wavelengths, it is a low brightness galaxy whose nuclear regions are crossed by an irregular dust lane and patches of dust ( ). Feedback between the radio activity and the surrounding thermal gas is discussed by . In our VLBA image, this source appears unresolved with a total flux density S_5 = 3.2 mJy. Some of the arcsecond core flux is lost on the parsec scale ( S_ VLBA /S_ 64 ).

sources.summary: NGC 708 is presented as Abell 262's brightest galaxy hosting B2 0149+35, with a central radio point source, east-west lobes, and known radio/X-ray interaction.

quotes.quote: NGC 708 is the brightest galaxy of the Abell 262 cluster and hosts the radio source B2 0149+35. The VLA data reveal a central point source and two lobes in an east-west configuration. The X-ray emission from the cluster is described by Blanton04 . A combined study of low frequency radio and X-ray data is presented by Clarke09 , showing the interaction of the radio source with its surroundings.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 708 as an Abell 262 CDG and low-power FR I source with confined two-sided radio lobes, X-ray cavities, and possible low-power activity.

quotes.quote: NGC 708 The giant elliptical galaxy, NGC 708 is a CDG of the cooling flow cluster Abell 262. and defined this galaxy as a low-power FR I radio source. \ VLA archival data in the A and C configuration (Figure fig:chandra_vla_a f), also studied by , , and , reveal a diffuse two-sided radio lobe-like emission with a total extent of 40 kpc from the central radio source B2 0149+35.

gmw_00000437.name: PG1247+267
gmw_00000437.overview: PG1247+267 is a highly luminous quasar located at a redshift of approximately 2 [arXiv:1604.02462]. It is a candidate for super-Eddington accretion onto a supermassive black hole [arXiv:1604.02462] and exhibits a strong X-ray reflection component [arXiv:astro-ph/0405458, arXiv:1604.02462].

sources.summary: The paper presents reverberation mapping of PG 1247+267, deriving a comparatively small black-hole mass for its extreme luminosity and discussing orientation or gravitational lensing as explanations.

quotes.quote: In this work we present the C IV , C III ] and continuum light curves obtained for PG 1247+267, and we estimate the relevant time lags based on a method proposed by Zu11 . We also analyze the shape of C IV and C III ] lines, and discuss the determination of the virial mass of the central BH, the corresponding value of the Eddington ratio an possible explanations of the anomalously high value found.

Adopting the same definition of V , we obtain for PG 1247+267 M_ BH 6.7 10^8 M_ , i.e. about 5 times smaller, in spite of its higher luminosity. Hereinafter we will use as our best estimate of the virial mass M_ BH ^ cent =8.3 ^ +3.4 _ 2.7 10^ 8 M_ . From this comparison it appears that the new point, we are adding at the highest luminosity, corresponds to a mass which is roughly a factor 20 smaller with respect to the extrapolation of the general trend, which would predict a mass of the order of 2 10^ 10 M _ .

With respect to this relation PG 1247+267, with _ ox =-1.69 and L_ (2500 )=2.5 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 Shem14 deviates from the general trend by an amount which appears significant, despite the relatively large spread around the average relation. With respect to the average negative correlation between the C IV equivalent width (EW) and \ L_ (1350 ) Bian12 , again PG 1247+267, with EW=39.5 Shen11 , is deviant and would be brought to full consistency by allowing for gravitational lensing. An amplification of about 10 would at the same time: i) account for both these effects; ii) bring the Eddington ratio to 1 and iii) make this object less luminous than S5 0836+071 and consistent with the _l L relation.

sources.summary: The paper presents NuSTAR and archival X-ray analysis of luminous quasar PG1247+267, interpreting its spectrum as evidence for super-Eddington accretion with competing reflection or absorption scenarios.

quotes.quote: PG1247+267 is one of the most luminous known quasars at z 2 and is a strongly super-Eddington accreting SMBH candidate. We obtained \ data of this intriguing source in December 2014 with the aim of studying its high-energy emission, leveraging the broad band covered by the new \ and the archival \ data. Several measurements are in agreement with the super-Eddington scenario for PG1247+267: the soft power law ( =2.3 0.1 ); the weak ionized Fe emission line; and a hint of the presence of outflowing ionized gas surrounding the SMBH. The presence of an extreme reflection component is instead at odds with the high accretion rate proposed for this quasar. This can be explained with three different scenarios; all of them are in good agreement with the existing data, but imply very different conclusions: i) a variable primary power law observed in a low state, superimposed on a reflection component echoing a past, higher flux state; ii) a power law continuum obscured by an ionized, Compton thick, partial covering absorber; and iii) a relativistic disk reflector in a lamp-post geometry, with low coronal height and high BH spin.

PG1247+267 (hereafter PG1247) at z=2.048 is candidate to be an extreme example of super-Eddington accretion, and in this paper we present new \ observations obtained with the aim of characterizing its high-energy emission. The existing \ data (Sec. 3) are combined with the hard X-ray data from \ (Sec. 4) to constrain the high-energy properties of this extreme QSO (Sec. 5).

sources.summary: PG 1247+267 is described as the QSO with significant reflection and broad iron emission, with spectral hardening attributed mainly to reflection rather than a soft excess.

quotes.quote: Of these, PG 1247+267 seems to have a broad line (EW 250 eV), with a strong, associated reflection component (R 2), measured out to 30 keV in the rest frame of the QSO.
The results of this model, given in Table reftab , show that only in PG 1247+267 was a reflection component significantly detected; upper limits are given for the remaining four objects.
The component in PG 1247+267 is strong, with R 2, and is required in addition to the broad line; R is defined to be /2 , where is the solid angle subtended by the scattering medium.

gmw_00000438.name: NGC 4696
gmw_00000438.overview: NGC 4696 is a giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0607113] and the brightest cluster galaxy of the Centaurus cluster [arXiv:1009.2581, arXiv:1305.7046]. It features a large dust lane [arXiv:1009.2581, arXiv:1806.08436], extensive optical filaments [arXiv:astro-ph/0507627, arXiv:1311.2831], and a central black hole that suppresses star formation [arXiv:1806.08436].

sources.summary: The paper studies coronal-line emission from NGC 4696, describing hot interstellar gas, radio bubbles, soft X-ray and H-alpha filaments, and dust depletion affecting central calcium emission.

quotes.quote: We report a detection ( 3.5 10^ 37 5.6 10^ 36 ) of the optical coronal emission line [ Fe X ] 6374 and upper limits of four other coronal lines using high resolution VIMOS spectra centred on NGC 4696, the brightest cluster galaxy in the Centaurus cluster. Emission from these lines is indicative of gas at temperatures between 1 and 5 million K so traces the interstellar gas in NGC 4696. The rate of cooling derived from the upper limits is consistent with the cooling rate from X-ray observations ( 10 ) however we detect twice the luminosity expected for [ Fe X ] 6374 emission, at 1 million K, our lowest temperature probe.

NGC 4696 houses a radio source, and multiple bubbles. These are accompanied by soft X-ray filaments and a sharp rise in the metal abundance in the central 30 , among the highest seen in any cluster, twice solar sanders2002, fabian2005b, sanders2006 . Centaurus also has the broadest range of X-ray temperatures seen, containing gas from 0.35 to 3.7 , over a factor of 10 in temperature sanders2008b . presented images showing the extensive, filamentary H nebulosity surrounding NGC 4696. These share the morphology of the soft X-ray filaments, and of a prominent dust lane sparks1989 .
NGC 4696 hosts a huge dust lane which almost completely encircles the core and spirals out to the north east, tracing the morphology of the H filaments.

sources.summary: The paper observes NGC 4696 with Herschel, detecting extended FIR cooling lines and dust, fitting cold and warm dust components, and finding a low star-formation rate plus extra gas-heating source.

quotes.quote: We present observations of NGC 4696, the BCG of the Centaurus galaxy cluster, at far-infrared (FIR) wavelengths with the space telescope. Using the PACS spectrometer, we detect the two strongest coolants of the interstellar medium, at 157.74 and at 63.18 , and in addition at 121.90 . The emission is extended over a region of 7 kpc with a similar spatial morphology and kinematics to the optical emission. This has the profound implication that the optical hydrogen recombination line, , the optical forbidden lines, , the soft X-ray filaments and the far-infrared line all have the same energy source.

We also detect dust emission using the PACS and SPIRE photometers at all six wavebands. We perform a detailed spectral energy distribution fitting using a two-component modified black-body function and find a cold 19 K dust component with mass 1.6 10^6  and a warm 46 K dust component with mass 4.0 10^3  . The total FIR luminosity between 8 and 1000 is 7.5 10^8  , which using Kennicutt relation yields a low star formation rate of 0.13  . This value is consistent with values derived from other tracers, such as ultraviolet emission.

sources.summary: HST imaging resolves dusty molecular filaments and a central gas swirl in NGC4696, whose low star formation and AGN feedback shape the Centaurus brightest cluster galaxy.

quotes.quote: The Brightest Cluster Galaxy (BCG), NGC4696, in the nearby Centaurus cluster, Abell 3526, has a system of dusty H filaments (Fabian et al. 1982; Sparks et al. 1989, Crawford et al 2005) connecting the hot intracluster medium (ICM) with its Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN). The low star formation rate ( 0.1 ) means that energy feedback into the ICM via the AGN-powered radio source has stifled almost all complete radiative cooling in the hot gas for billions of years, despite a central cooling time (Sanders et al. 2008, 2016) of only 3 10^7 . The details of how feedback is so successful and the operation of key ingredients such as the heating of the hot gas, the behaviour of the cold gas, the lack of star formation, and the fuelling of the central back hole are unclear (Fabian 2012).

Unlike the filaments in NGC1275, those in NGC4696 contain dust lanes, first reported by Shobbrook (1963), which contrasts with the NGC1275 filaments for which no dust lanes have been detected. Dust has also been detected in emission with Spitzer (Kaneda et al 2007) and Herschel (Mittal et al 2011) with a total cold dust mass of 1.6 10^6 . In comparison with the total molecular mass, the dust-to- gas ratio appears to be normal. Comparison of the dust extinction in the R and V bands shows (Sparks et al 1989) that to be normal also. The redshift of NGC 4696 is 0.0104 and we adopt H_0=71 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4696 as the Centaurus cD galaxy whose motion, dust lane, radio source, and surrounding cool gas shape interpretations of the cluster-core plume.

quotes.quote: The results of Dickens, Currie Lucey (1986) show that NGC 4696 lies close to the mean velocity of Cen 30. However, the radio maps of Taylor et al. (2001) show that the radio lobes bend to the south of the core. This indicates that NGC 4696 is moving north relative to the local intracluster medium.

Observations of the core of the cluster at optical wavelengths show a dust-lane in NGC 4696 (Shobbrook 1963). It has been noted that the X-ray emission has a similar morphology to the dust lane (Sparks et al. 1993). Using four HST archived observations (datasets U3560101T, U3560102T, U62G8401R and U62G8402R) of the galaxy at 5407 and 7940 , we have made a ratio image of the optical emission in two bands highlighting the dust-lane. This is shown in Fig. fig:imageHST , overlayed with a contour map of the centre of the adaptively smoothed image, Fig. fig:imageASmooth . The swirling morphology of the dust-lane is similar to, but smaller than, the X-ray plume.

Sparks, Macchetto Golombek (1989) and de Jong et al. (1990) have proposed that the cooler central gas around NGC 4696 originates from an infallen dust-rich dwarf galaxy, which also accounts for the dust lane.

sources.summary: The paper examines COS spectra of NGC 4696, describing it as a bright cool-core central galaxy with filamentary emission, detected lower-temperature lines, lower continuum, and smaller black-hole aperture sensitivity.

quotes.quote: We also examine a sightline towards the center of NGC 4696. This is the brightest central galaxy of the Centaurus cluster, which is a classic cool-core system: the temperature of the intracluster medium decreases from a baseline of 3-4 keV down to a bit below 1 keV in the center ( Fukazawa1994 , Allen1994 , Allen2001 , Molendi2002a , Fabian2005 , Graham2006 , Sanders2006 , Sanders2008 , Panagoulia2013 ). As with M87, the X-ray spectra in the cool core prefer multi-temperature fits, and NGC 4696 also hosts extensive filamentary structure which radiates in optical emission lines ( Fabian1982 , Crawford2005 ). More recently, [ Fe x ] 6374 emission has also been detected from this galaxy Canning2011 , which traces gas at 10^6 K. Mittal2011 have also detected [ C ii ] 158 m, [ O i ] 63 m, and [ N ii ] 122 m emission which is largely coincident with the filamentary structure, suggesting the presence of 10^2 K gas as well.
This spectrum has a much lower continuum than the spectrum of the nucleus of M87; this is partially due to the higher distance of NGC 4696, and partially intrinsic.

We are somewhat sensitive to an accretion flow in this galaxy as well, but NGC 4696 has a less massive black hole than M87 and its Bondi radius fills a much smaller fraction of our aperture than in the M87 observation.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC4696 as a nearby Centaurus-cluster BCG with a hidden cooling flow, dust lanes, very weak star formation, and FIR emission likely reradiated from absorbed soft X-rays.

quotes.quote: A good example of a well-studied nearby galaxy with a hidden cooling flow of 12 2 (HCFI) but very little star formation is NGC4696, the BCG in the Centaurus cluster (A3526) at a distance of 45 Mpc. In the optical band it shows clear dust lanes and we obtain a total intrinsic column density for the HCR of 1.5 10^ 22 . No blue light in excess of an old stellar population is seen Farage2010, Canning2011 . It was observed with the Herschel FIR Observatory from which deduced a SFR of 0.13 if it accounts for all the FIR emission.

Much lower values of 0.002-0.08 were deduced from FUV emission. The HCF solution requires that the FIR emission be due to the soft X-ray energy absorbed and reradiated by dust in the FIR. Any normal star formation in NGC4696 is then very small ( SFR 0.05 HCR ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4696 as Centaurus' central galaxy, with cool H-emitting filaments, a dust lane, soft X-ray plumes, and radio-bubble activity shaping its intracluster environment.

quotes.quote: We present images of NGC 4696, the central galaxy in the Centaurus cluster, showing the large extent of cool filaments which are bright in \ line emission. These filaments share the detailed structure of both the central dust lane and the inner regions of the arc-like plumes seen in soft X-ray emission. The X-ray gas is at its coolest, and most absorbed in this same region. The smoothness of the features implies that the local environment is not strongly turbulent.

We suggest that these filaments are either shaped by confinement due to a strong magnetic field, or by bulk flows within the intracluster medium. We propose that like similar filamentary systems in the core of clusters, these cooler components have been drawn out of the central galaxy behind buoyant gas bubbles from previous episodes of radio activity. We find a spur of low-frequency radio emission leading to a region of low X-ray pressure within the intracluster medium supporting this interpretation.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4696 as a central cluster galaxy with optical filaments, a strong dust lane, PAH and [NeII] emission, and Spitzer-detected warm molecular gas.

quotes.quote: We present Spitzer high-resolution spectra of off-nuclear regions in the central cluster galaxies NGC 1275 and NGC 4696 in the Perseus and Centaurus clusters, respectively. Both objects are surrounded by extensive optical emission-line filamentary nebulae, bright outer parts of which are the targets of our observations. The 10--37 m spectra show strong pure rotational lines from molecular hydrogen revealing a molecular component to the filaments which has an excitation temperature of 300-400 K. The flux in the 0-0S(1) molecular hydrogen line correlates well with the strength of the optical lines, having about 3 per cent of the H + [ NII ] emission. The 11.3 m PAH feature is seen in some spectra.

NGC 4696 at the centre of the Centaurus cluster has H filaments discovered by Fabian82 and a strong dust lane ( Shobbrook66 ). Sparks89 and Crawford05 have mapped the nebulosity which appears to surround the dust lane.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4696's Chandra temperature and metallicity profiles, confirming a central iron abundance drop and arguing that some missing iron may reside in cold dust.

quotes.quote: Received ; in original form firstpage - lastpage 2002 We re-analyse a combined 198 ks Chandra observation of NGC 4696, the brightest galaxy of the Centaurus cluster. We extract temperature and metallicity profiles from the data, and we confirm the presence of a sharp drop in iron abundance, from 1.8 Z to 0.4 Z , within the central 5 kpc of the cluster. We estimate that this central abundance drop corresponds to a total ``missing'' iron mass of 1.4 10 ^ 6 M _ . We propose that part of this missing iron is locked up in cool ( 19 K), far-IR emitting dust, as found by Spitzer and Herschel observations.

In this paper, we study NGC 4696, the BCG of the Centaurus galaxy cluster. NGC 4696 has a redshift of 0.0114 Struble99 , and has been studied extensively, in all wavelengths, because of its proximity Fabian82, Lucey86, deJong90, Allen94, Sanders02, Sanders06a, Sanders08, Crawford05b, Johnstone07, Canning11b, Mittal11 .

sources.summary: NGC 4696 is treated as the Centaurus brightest cluster galaxy with X-ray cavities, a plume, central missing iron, optical filaments, infrared dust emission, and multi-temperature spectral fitting.

quotes.quote: NGC 4696 NGC 4696 is the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) of the Centaurus cluster, and is the second closest galaxy cluster after the Virgo cluster (NGC 4696 has a redshift of 0.01). It has a pair of well-defined X-ray cavities, and a plume of X-ray emitting material that stretches to the north east of the cluster e.g. Fabian05 . We find that the ``missing'' iron mass with respect to unit Solar abundance is 1.4 10 ^ 6 M _ , in the central 5 kpc of this cluster Fig. fig:abundprofscertain ; see also Panagoulia13 .

The central 5 kpc of NGC 4696 contain most of the optical filamentary structure present in the galaxy Crawford05a . Herschel images, created by , show that the vast majority of the infrared emission comes from the inner 5 kpc of the cluster. In addition, the same authors detected emission from cold (19 K) and warm (46 K) dust, with respective masses of 1.6 0.3 10 ^ 6 M _ and 4.0 2.8 10 ^ 3 M _ .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4696 as the massive elliptical host of SN 2017ejb, with deep pre-explosion Chandra coverage, a captured dust lane, suppressed star formation, and little local host extinction.

quotes.quote: SN 2017ejb was discovered in NGC 4696, a well-studied elliptical galaxy in the Centaurus cluster with 894 ks of Chandra imaging between 14 and 3 years before SN 2017ejb was discovered.

NGC 4696 does have an extended, relatively massive dust lane that was likely captured 10^ 8 yr ago dejong+90,sparks+89 . However, SN 2017ejb is 158 (28 kpc at the distance of NGC 4696) in projection from the center of its host galaxy. It is unlikely that it would be enshrouded by significant host extinction.

In particular, we note that SN 2017ejb was discovered in the massive elliptical galaxy NGC 4696 shobbrook+63,mitchell+75 . While this galaxy exhibits tendrils of dust that likely originate from material captured 10^ 8 yr ago, most of the star formation in NGC 4696 is suppressed by its central black hole sanders+16 .

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes NGC 4696 HST globular-cluster photometry, arguing that aperture choices can reverse or erase the apparent mass-metallicity trend.

quotes.quote: At a distance of 42 Mpc NGC 4696 is among the most distant galaxies in the Harris et al. (2006) study. It is also the galaxy with the richest globular cluster system and shows the most obvious signature of an apparent mass-metallicity trend of all the bright ellipticals in their sample according to Harris et al. (2006). We have re-analyzed the globular clusters in the 5440s F435W (B band) and 2320s (I band) GO-9427 images of NGC 4696 studied by Harris et al (2006).

The lack of objects in the top left part of the diagram and the apparent tendency of clusters brighter than I 23 to trend redder at higher luminosity is consistent with the Harris et al. (2006) analysis. This has been interpreted as evidence of metallicity enrichment by Harris et al. (2006).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4696 as the central dominant galaxy of the Centaurus cool core, with dusty optical filaments, extended X-ray structure, and compact radio emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 4696: is the central dominant galaxy in the classical cool-core Centaurus Cluster. This well-studied system formed the basis of the suggestion by Sparks et al. (1989) that mergers in the presence of thermal conduction can be the cause of the X-ray excess and line emission, and not cooling flows. The optical filaments are dusty with normal extinction characteristics indicative of a merger origin. The X-ray morphology is similar to the optical though extended over a much larger scale, and the radio emission is compact and has a steep spectrum, on similar scale to the optical. Fig. 2 shows the two slit positions used.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4696 as the Centaurus central galaxy, comparing its globular-cluster radial coverage, median GC distance, and effective radius with Hydra.

quotes.quote: To investigate this in more detail, we show in Fig. cumrad the cumulative radial distribution of GC candidates in both Hydra and Centaurus, centered on NGC 3311 and NGC 4696 respectively. It is clear from this figure that the GC distribution is more centrally concentrated for Hydra than for Centaurus. We note that this is imposed mainly by the observational setup (Fig. map ), which was focused on NGC 3311 in Hydra, whereas the Centaurus observations focused on the area between the central galaxy NGC 4696 and NGC 4709. The median projected distance to NGC 3311 for the Hydra GCs is about 25 kpc, while the median distance to NGC 4696 for the Centaurus GCs is about 42 kpc. The effective radius of the Centaurus cluster central galaxy NGC 4696 is 82 " or 17 kpc, while for the Hydra central galaxy NGC 3311 it is 150 " and thus 35 kpc.

sources.summary: NGC 4696 is described as the Centaurus central galaxy whose dust lane, optical filaments, cool X-ray filaments, and metal-rich gas are linked to buoyant bubbles and AGN uplift.

quotes.quote: The central galaxy, NGC 4696, contains a dust lane Shobbrook63 and has bright filamentary central H emission Fabian82 , both of which have similar morphologies Sparks89 . These components are themselves correlated with the cool X-ray emitting filaments Crawford05 , and appear to be drawn up by buoyant bubbles rising in the ICM Crawford05 . This model is supported by deep integral-field spectroscopy CanningIFU11 , who find no evidence for fast shock excitation and whose spectra support the particle heating excitation model of .

If the AGN bubbles drag dusty clouds of high metallicity gas produced by stellar mass loss from the core of NGC 4696 to larger radius, this will naturally flatten out the central peak and give the observed effective diffusion coefficient which rapidly declines with radius Panagoulia13 . In addition, this intriguingly raises the possibility that the high metallicity regions are metal-rich materials uplifted from the galactic centre by AGN bubbles.

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe NGC 4696 as having extensive fan-shaped, dynamically rich LINER emission with split lines, narrow outer filaments, and shock-heated minor-merger remnants.

quotes.quote: NGC 4696: A very extensive and dynamically rich fan-shaped LINER. The opening angle is 90^ , and the axis is at PA = 180^ . Emission can be traced to the edge of the WiFeS field. In some regions the \ and \ lines are split into two velocity components, while the outer regions are characterised by narrow FWHM. This object has been previously studied in detail with WiFeS by Farage10 , which showed that the filaments are shock heated remnants of a minor merger.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4696 as the Centaurus core galaxy hosting PKS 1246-410, with optical filaments, a dust lane, cavities, lobes, and AGN feedback linked to core structures.

quotes.quote: As NGC 4696 hosts a low-power radio source, PKS 1246-410 , radio data were also employed Taylor_2002, Taylor_2006 . The multi-wavelength studies using X-ray observations and VLA and VLBA radio observations reveal a wealth of structures in the nucleus of the Centaurus cluster. For example, it was found that the plume is the most prominent below 1 keV and it consists of three soft filaments. The nucleus, where the plume seems to originate, coincides with optical filaments and dust lane from NGC 4696 Fabian_1982, Sparks_1989, Hamer_2019 . On the sides of the nucleus are cavities that anti-correlate with radio lobes. These authors concluded that the plume and other features in the core of the Centaurus cluster (cavities, edges, shocks, bay, etc.), are the result of AGN feedback from the NGC 4696, as well as sloshing motion.

sources.summary: The paper links the metal-rich Centaurus core around NGC 4696 to past star formation and stellar mass loss, while noting low current star formation constraints.

quotes.quote: The central galaxy, NCG 4696, is a giant elliptical, with a de Vaucouleurs profile and no evidence for an extended halo in near-infrared H band observations (Arnalte Mur et al 2006). The optical spectral indicator D_ 4000 of the galaxy light is slightly smaller than seen in field ellipticals, which may indicate some excess blue light and thus ongoing star formation (Johnstone, Fabian Nulsen 1987). The total mass of iron in the high abundance peak at radii less than 40 kpc is about 1.4 10^8 (Fig. fig:cumlmass ).

In order to obtain a limit on the current star formation rate in NGC 4696 (scenario i), we use the Mg _2 index, which is reduced by the presence of a young stellar population. (The H index, Trager et al (2005), would be better but is contaminated here by line emission.) It has been employed by Cardiel et al (1998) to reveal and measure the star formation rate in other Brightest Cluster Galaxies. Mg _2 has been measured in NGC 4696 by Gorgas, Efstathiou Arag on Salamanca (1990) and by Annibali et al (2006).

sources.summary: The paper links NGC 4696 to extended mid-infrared emission and nitrogen-rich optical filaments, arguing that its filaments may share an origin with cooling hot gas.

quotes.quote: There is, indeed, extended mid-IR emission observed from NGC 4696 Kaneda07 , which could in part be powered by such a mixing process.

The nitrogen emission lines in the optical spectrum of the emission-line filamentary nebula in NGC 4696, the central galaxy in the Centaurus cluster also indicate a high nitrogen abundance. Specifically, the [ NII ] /H line ratio found by of 3.2 is higher than the ratio of less than two which can be accounted for by photoionization or shock models for a solar abundance plasma Heckman89,VoitDonahue90,CrawfordFabian92 . A high nitrogen abundance in both the optical filaments and the hot gas supports the hypothesis that they have a common origin, with the filaments representing gas which has cooled from the hotter phase.

sources.summary: NGC 4696 is presented as the Centaurus Cluster's dominant galaxy, hosting PKS 1245-41 with two radio lobes, bent high-frequency jets, and clear ICM interaction.

quotes.quote: NGC 4696 NGC 4696 is the dominant galaxy of the Centaurus Cluster (Abell 3526) and hosts the radio source PKS 1245-41. The radio emission has two clear lobes. High frequency radio emission shows that the jets initially travel east-west before both turning south Taylor02 . The interaction of the radio source with the surrounding intra-cluster medium is strikingly clear and is discussed in detail by Sanders02,Fabian05,Taylor06a .

sources.summary: The paper presents deep VIMOS spectroscopy of NGC 4696, describing its luminous H emission nebula and filaments as material drawn out by rising radio bubbles.

quotes.quote: We present results of deep integral field spectroscopy observations using high resolution optical (4150-7200 ) VIMOS VLT spectra, of NGC 4696, the dominant galaxy in the Centaurus cluster (Abell 3526). After the Virgo cluster, this is the second nearest (z=0.0104) example of a cool core cluster. NGC 4696 is surrounded by a vast, luminous H emission line nebula (L _ H =2.2 10^ 40 ). We explore the origin and excitation of the emission-line filaments and find their origin consistent with being drawn out, under rising radio bubbles, into the intracluster medium as in other similar systems.

sources.summary: The paper finds that 1246--41 shows diffuse cluster-scale X-ray emission, lacks a required point-source PSF, and needs no power-law spectral component, implying a weak or heavily absorbed nucleus.

quotes.quote: 1246--41 (NGC 4696), where only diffuse emission is detected on the scale of the cluster, and 0334--01 (3C 15), where only a point source is detected. The nuclear X--ray spectra are well fitted at hard energies by an absorbed power law, with a wide range of photon indices, =1.5-2.7 . Excess absorption over the Galactic value is detected in 6/9 sources, with column densities N _H 10^ 21-22 . A thermal component is required at softer energies, in agreement with the results of the spatial analysis.

We found that 1246--41 does not require a PSF, and while it too appears to require an additional -model past 25 arcseconds, we could not get good statistics on a second -model. For 1333--33 it was required to fix the value for the core radius in order to arrive at physically meaningful results---several different values were tried, with the quoted value yielding the best fit. Timing Analysis We produced background subtracted light curves for each of the objects in our sample. For 1216+06 we refer to the detailed study by Gliozzi, Sambruna, Brandt (2003). We chose to produce light curves based on \ data when possible, but when only \ or \ data were available, those data were used. According to a ^2 test for constancy, we found there to be no statistically significant nuclear variability in any of the objects on the time scales probed by our observations, few hours. This is likely due to (1) the intrinsic weakness of the sources, which translates into a low count--rate especially for \ and \ observations, and (2) the short exposures. Spectral Analysis Previous X--ray spectral studies of low power radio galaxies suggest that at least two components are required to fit a spectrum: one, a thermal component associated with the extended emission; two, a power--law component associated with the unresolved nuclear point source wor94 . This is consistent with the results of our spatial analysis, in which four out of the six sources required a point--like source in diffuse emission. With these considerations in mind we began our spectral modeling with a power law, which dominates at hard energies, and a soft component modeled as emission from a collisionally--ionized plasma apec in XSPEC ; smi01 . All of the spectral models also assume a column density fixed to the Galactic level appropriate to the source dic90 . Additional components, such as intrinsic absorption, covering fraction, or additional thermal components, were added to the model when the data required them. The results of the spectral analysis are summarized in Table tbl-4 . When a source was observed with both \ and we quote the spectral model derived from the \ data, since it was more statistically sound. Seven out of the nine objects require a power law with between 1.5 and 2.7 , with the average value being 1.9 . The extremely flat value of for 1251--12 might be related to the poor photon statistics. Six out of the nine objects required at least one thermal component. The temperatures show a bimodal distribution around 0.6 keV and >1.5 keV, which probably reflects the different environments---poor and rich clusters respectively. Five out of the nine objects required intrinsic absorption with values ranging from 10^ 21 to 10^ 23 , with the average value being 4 10^ 23 . We tried to add a \ line at 6.4 keV, but this was not statistically required for any of the objects except 1216+06 see sam03 . The spectral results are in good agreement with the findings of the spatial analysis. The surface brightness profile of 0034--01 was found to not require a -model, indicating that little to no extended emission is present; indeed, we see that no thermal component is required to fit the object's spectrum. We also found that the surface brightness profile of 1246--41 did not require a PSF, indicating a heavily absorbed and/or very weak point source; this too conforms to the spectral results, in that no power law was required to fit the object's spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4696 as the central Cen30 galaxy, the brightest cluster galaxy with low Sersic index, and a giant galaxy whose light was subtracted to model nearby C-1-10.

quotes.quote: The main cluster component is Cen30 with NGC 4696 at its dynamical centre, whereas Cen45 is the in-falling sub-group with NGC 4709 at its centre 1986MNRAS.221..453L, 1997A&A...327..952S .
Only the brightest cluster galaxy, NGC 4696, has an exceptionally low S ersic index ( n=2.5 ).
Due to the projected proximity of C-1-10 to the giant galaxy NGC 4696, we created and subtracted a model of the latter before modelling the dwarf galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents WiFeS spectroscopy of NGC 4696, interpreting its emission-line filaments as gas from a minor merger shock-excited by passage through the galaxy halo.

quotes.quote: We present deep optical integral-field spectroscopic observations of the nearby ( z 0.01 ) brightest cluster galaxy NGC 4696 in the core of the Centaurus Cluster, made with the Wide Field Spectrograph (WiFeS) on the ANU 2.3m telescope at Siding Spring Observatory. We investigate the morphology, kinematics, and excitation of the emission-line filaments and discuss these in the context of a model of a minor merger. We suggest that the emission-line filaments in this object have their origin in the accretion of a gas-rich galaxy and that they are excited by v 100-200 shocks driven into the cool filament gas by the ram pressure of the transonic passage of the merging system through the hot halo gas of NGC 4696.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes deep Chandra and XMM-Newton data for NGC 4696, linking its central metal abundance drops, dust, filaments, and cluster-core position to dust depletion.

quotes.quote: The central BCG (NGC 4696) of this bright and nearby cool-core system exhibits one of the most prominent Fe drops known to date (with an abrupt inwards decrease from 2 to 0.5 solar), which was the second ever discovered Sanders2002 .

In addition, NGC 4696 is well known to contain significant amounts of dust, observed in far-infrared with Spitzer Kaneda2005,Kaneda2007 and Herschel Mittal2011 , as well as extended H and optical (in particular [ NII ] ) filaments see also Lakhchaura et al. Lakhchaura2018 Fabian2016,Hamer2018 .

In this paper, we take advantage of the extremely deep Chandra observations of NGC 4696 (residing in the core of the Centaurus cluster), obtained in successive pointings over 14 years since the beginning of the mission, combined with the unique spatial resolution of the telescope, in order to infer unprecedented constraints on its metal abundance profiles.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4696 as the brightest cluster galaxy in Centaurus and links its nucleus to nearby X-ray edges and asymmetric gas structure indicating dynamical change.

quotes.quote: Our earlier 31.7 ks Chandra image of the Centaurus cluster revealed a complex structure in the innermost few arcmin of the core, centred on the brightest cluster galaxy NGC 4696 (Sanders Fabian 2002).

While the edge to the E is centred on the nucleus of NGC 4696, that to the W is centred 52 arcsec (10.9 kpc) to the W of the nucleus, suggesting that the gas there is in motion with respect to the nucleus.

Given that in equilibrium temperature contours follow equipotentials, the asymmetric temperature structure around the central galaxy NGC 4696 means that some major part of the mass or gas distribution there is dynamically changing.

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 4696 as an optically peculiar Centaurus central galaxy with radio emission, dusty neutral gas, and disturbed hot gas, excluding it from normal-elliptical FIR interpretation.

quotes.quote: Of these six galaxies, only three -- NGC 4636, 4696 and 5044 -- have X-ray luminosities ( L_x 41.5 ) large enough for an accurate determination of the gas density and temperature profiles. However, the optically peculiar elliptical NGC 4696 is the central galaxy in the Centaurus Cluster with powerful FRI-type radio emission, a large mass of dusty neutral gas and highly disturbed hot cluster gas (Fabian et al. 2006). In view of these complexities, NGC 4696 is an inappropriate galaxy to learn about the nature of FIR emission from normal elliptical galaxies.

gmw_00000439.name: GMP2808
gmw_00000439.overview: GMP2808 is a dwarf galaxy located in the Coma Cluster [arXiv:1110.2649]. It is distinguished as the bluest galaxy within its local dwarf sample, requiring the use of blue stellar templates to accurately measure its velocity dispersion [arXiv:1110.2649].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GMP2808 as the bluest galaxy in the Coma dwarf sample, with template-dependent velocity-dispersion behavior requiring blue stellar templates.

quotes.quote: Of six faintest galaxies from Deimos sample, GMP2808, GMP2655, GMP3119, GMP2780 and GMP3141 are bluer than all other sample galaxies (see Figure fig:csigma ) and have larger velocity dispersion with respect to the L- relation of brighter galaxies (see Figure tab:faber_jackson ). In order to test the effect of stellar population on measured velocity dispersions, we have repeated the measurement using two sets of stellar templates. First set consists of 10 F-stars with different spectral types and metallicities (hereafter F-lib). Second set includes both F-lib and the G/K-stars of Table tab:ftemplates (hereafter FGK-lib). The results of measurements based on Set80, FGK-lib and F-lib are compared in Table tab:sig-lib . The analysis based on FGK-lib shows no changes in results except for GMP2808 which is the bluest galaxy in our sample.

The measured of GMP3119 and GMP2808 in F-lib run is smaller than those of Set80 and FGK-lib runs, however the resulting ^2 is higher for F-lib run. The maximum change in measured for all other galaxies in our sample in FGK-lib run is less than 2 km s^ 1 . As a results, the measured values in Table tab:results are independent of the stellar population except for the bluest galaxy, for which blue stellar templates should be used for measurement.

gmw_00000440.name: NGC 1387
gmw_00000440.overview: NGC 1387 is a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:0806.2658, arXiv:astro-ph/0511770] featuring an inner bar, a boxy bulge, and a nuclear dust ring [arXiv:1906.08187]. It is tidally interacting with the nearby galaxy NGC 1399 [arXiv:1906.08187, arXiv:1803.03276], which has stripped its outer stellar envelope [arXiv:1906.08187, arXiv:1803.03276] and globular clusters [arXiv:astro-ph/0605064, arXiv:1910.01884].

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 1387 as an outlier with limited literature data, notes it follows another SBF-color relation, and identifies it as a lenticular candidate for further study.

quotes.quote: Note the outlying cases of NGC 1387, NGC 4552, and NGC 1389 with an infrared effective magnitude that is 0.7 mag too bright for their UV excess. The relevant case of the merger radio-galaxy NGC 1316 (Fornax A) is also singled out in the plot, where the arrow indicates that a ``bluer'' (1550- V ) color might be more appropriate for this galaxy (with a more negligible impact on K _s , though), as a consequence of a strong internal absorption due to the observed presence of dust lanes. Excluding these controversial objects (discussed in more detail in Sec. 4), the data sample correlates fairly well ( = -0.75 , see arrow), indicating a less deployed AGB for UV-enhanced galaxies.

Concerning NGC 1387, the dearth of data for this galaxy in the literature (see Table 2) makes it hard to propose an origin for its departure from the K_s vs. \ ( 1500 - V ) sequence. Note that NGC 1387 complies with the K _s vs. \ ( V - I ) correlation determined by liu02 . On the other hand, NGC 1387 and NGC 1389 are lenticular galaxies, like the merger remnant NGC 1316, but so are NGC 3384 and NGC 4406, both of which do not deviate from the correlation. The other galaxies in our sample are all genuine ellipticals rc3 , except for NGC 224 ( alias M 31), which is so close, however, that bulge SBFs can be measured without any significant contamination from the disk. At any rate, NGC 1387 and NGC 1389 constitute privileged candidates for any future ``in-depth'' investigation.

sources.summary: FCC 184 is described as a bright elliptical tidally interacting with FCC 213, with an inner bar, boxy bulge, small decoupled component, and nuclear dust ring.

quotes.quote: FCC 184 (NGC 1387) -- Fig. fig:FCC184map FCC 184 is the bright elliptical galaxy located at R_ proj =0.3^ west of FCC 213 (NGC 1399). Their distance differs by 2 Mpc according to Blakeslee2009 . Photometric and spectroscopic studies proved that FCC 184 is tidally interacting with FCC 213 with an ongoing stripping of the eastern part of the outer stellar envelope Dabrusco2016, Spiniello2018, Iodice2018 . FCC 184 is one of the ancient infallers located in the north-south galaxy clump. The MUSE pointings were aligned along PA_ phot 100^ , while the stellar kinematics suggests that the rotation axis has PA_ kin 60^ .

FCC 184 has an inner bar, a prominent boxy bulge, and a small kinematically decoupled component. The stellar velocity dispersion is large inside the bulge ( 200 km s ^ 1 ) with isocontours consistent with the bulge boxy isophotes. A nuclear dust-ring of 6 arcsec was found from near-infrared Laurikainen2006 and optical deep imaging Iodice2018 .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1387 has rotation-related PN velocity asymmetries and is connected to NGC 1399 by a high-velocity PN stream interpreted as stripped outer-envelope material.

quotes.quote: We believe that part of this difference could be caused by the different image quality between FIELD2 and FIELD3 combined with the fact that this galaxy (and the PNe bound to it) shows clear sign of rotation. In fact, FIELD3 points to the receding part of the galaxy and is the field with the best image quality we have (in this pointing we found a total of 143 PNe). On the contrary, FIELD2, on the approaching side of the galaxy rotation, has lower image quality (in total 95 PNe). Consequently, the number of PNe with receding velocity with respect to the systemic velocity of NGC 1387 is larger than the number of PNe with approaching velocity.

Finally, we will show in the following sections that hints for gravitational interaction between NGC 1399 and NGC 1387 are suggested by the presence of a stream of high-velocity PNe connecting the two galaxies. Some of these high-velocity PNe might overlap spatially with the PNe gravitationally bound to NGC 1387, thus broadening the PN LOSVD at the location of this galaxy. It is worth noticing that this kinematical stream is in the same spatial region where FDS-I found a 5 arcmin long faint stellar bridge ( _ g 29-30 mag arcsec ^ 2 ) and and reported an over density of the distribution of blue GCs. We argue that the stream is the result of the stripping of the outer envelope of NGC 1387 on its east side.

sources.summary: The paper applies MUSE/DELF planetary-nebula analysis to NGC 1387, showing its PNLF, deriving its luminosity-specific PN number, and presenting inner stellar-population and metallicity-relation results.

quotes.quote: We show in these proceedings the results process applied to one of the galaxies in this sample, NGC 1387.
In Figure fig:pnlf we show the PNLF for NGC 1387.
