For NGC 1387, we obtain a value of _ 2.5 =4.39 10^ 8 N_ PNe L_ bol ^ 1 . We use the GIST pipeline to analyse the stellar population in the cubes. GIST utilizes Penalized PiXel-Fitting Cappellari2023 in order to obtain kinematics and stellar population properties (i.e. age and metallicity). In Figs. fig:kin and fig:sfh we show the stellar population properties of NGC 1387 obtained with this method. In Figure fig:alpha we show the relation between and the mean metallicity of the host galaxy for the sample studied by , covering a wide range of morphologies, and indicate the position that the inner region of NGC 1387 has in this relation.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1387 as a low-luminosity lenticular in Fornax, finding its globular cluster system has a distinct red-blue separation and many red clusters.

quotes.quote: Received ; in original form firstpage - lastpage 2005 We present a photometric study of the globular cluster systems of the Fornax cluster galaxies NGC 1374, NGC 1379, and NGC 1387. The data consists of images from the wide-field MOSAIC Imager of the CTIO 4-m telescope, obtained with Washington C and Kron--Cousins R filters. The images cover a field of 36 36 arcmin, corresponding to 200 200 kpc at the Fornax distance. Two of the galaxies, NGC 1374 and NGC 1379, are low-luminosity ellipticals while NGC 1387 is a low-luminosity lenticular. Their cluster systems are still embedded in the cluster system of NGC 1399.
NGC 1387 presents a particularly distinct separation between red and blue clusters and a overproportionally large population of red clusters.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1387 as a nearby giant galaxy with a GC population difficult to separate from NGC 1399, then estimates eight associated GCs using velocity criteria.

quotes.quote: It is not trivial to disentangle the GC population of NGC 1387 and NGC 1404 because of their proximity in phase-space to NGC 1399. Here we adopt a conservative approach to extract the GC system of these galaxies, but we acknowledge that more refined methodologies can return more accurate results, as we will discuss in forthcoming papers.

Here we used = 247 Vanderbeke11 , v_ sys = 1947 for NGC 1404 and = 170 Wegner03 , v_ sys = 1302 for NGC 1387, respectively. After applying the above selection criteria, we found 17 and 8 GCs associated with NGC 1404 and NGC 1387, respectively. The larger number of GCs associated with NGC 1404 is due to the galaxy being more massive than NGC 1387, but also to its proximity to NGC 1399 which increases the fraction of contaminants.

sources.summary: For NGC1387, the paper describes an almost spherical GC system affected by interaction with NGC1399, with extended structures near D25 and a blue-GC bridge alignment.

quotes.quote: The GCS of NGC1387 is characterized by an almost spherical spatial distribution and the lack of significant azimuthal anisotropy bassino2006 ; the GC color subclasses display different radial profiles, with red GCs more centrally concentrated and of a shallower but more radially more extended blue GCs profile. suggested that the current properties of the NGC1387 GCS have been determined by the hierarchical merging evolution history of the host beasley2002 and the tidal interaction with the nearby giant elliptical NGC1399 which has stripped NGC1387 of the outermost GCs. Figure fig:ngc1387 shows the residual maps based on the spatial distribution of the ACSFCS GCs observed in NGC1387. In the upper right panel, the map for all GCs features three extended structures straddling or located just outside the D _ 25 isophote of the host galaxy (G1, G2 and G4). G1 and G4 have complex, elongated morphology suggestive of the presence of substructures, partially confirmed by the different shapes of these structures in the residual maps for different color GCs classes, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1387 as an evolved, vertically thick bar case despite low _z values, attributing the apparent contradiction to early-type bulge contamination.

quotes.quote: The behavior of _z in NGC 1387 and NGC 1440 shows a relatively steep fall from the center outward in both axes, and their _z values are low, presumably indicating young bars. However, their values for _ z, bar are too high to be the case of young bars. These are certainly vertically thick and evolved bars, resembling the schema of Fig. 6(). These are typical cases where the early--type bulge presents a very high velocity dispersion. The low values of _z may likely be explained by light contamination in the bar minor axis from the bulge and the bar even at the outermost spectra.

sources.summary: The review highlights NGC 1387 as a low-luminosity Fornax S0 whose globular-cluster colours show strong bimodality, likely tied to S0 disk/halo structure and a past starburst transformation.

quotes.quote: Figure bimod shows the colour distributions in the Washington system of the GCSs of NGC 1399, the central giant elliptical in the Fornax cluster, and NGC 1387, a Fornax S0 galaxy near NGC 1399 (Dirsch et al. 2003, Bassino et al. 2005). To my knowledge this is the clearest bimodality seen in any galaxy. Being of quite low luminosity and an S0-galaxy, NGC 1387 is not a good candidate for supporting the merger scenario. However, the processes which produce S0s have, according to common wisdom to do, with interactions of galaxies, and the predominance of red clusters might have its explanation in the star burst which took place while NGC 1387 was transformed into an S0 galaxy.

However, a colour distribution like that seen for NGC 1387 cannot be the result of non-linearity or second parameters, but probably indicates the disk and halo structure of an S0-galaxy, as has been found also for NGC 1380 (Kissler-Patig et al. 1997).

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1387 has an unusually red globular-cluster color distribution, a low blue-cluster fraction, a small GCS, and likely tidal loss of blue clusters.

quotes.quote: In fact, the fraction of blue clusters, with respect to the total GC population is low for the three systems, but even lower for NGC 1387 (43 , 45 , and 24 for NGC 1374, NGC 1379 and NGC 1387, respectively). By means of the luminosity functions we estimate the total GC populations in NGC 1374, NGC 1379 and NGC 1387 between 200 and 400 clusters, and obtain specific frequencies S_ N = 2.4, 1.4 and 1.8, respectively.

The other tail, towards NGC 1387 cannot be just an overlapping of GCSs due to the small size of the GCS of NGC 1387 ( = 3 arcmin ) as compared to the projected distance to NGC 1399 (19 arcmin). Such overdensity of blue GCs may be understood as evidence that blue clusters, the less bound ones, are being lost by NGC 1387 due to some interaction process with the central cluster galaxy, like tidal stripping.

sources.summary: The paper links NGC1387 to a bridge-like stellar structure and globular-cluster contour connection with NGC1399, suggesting ongoing interaction and possible stripping of NGC1387 clusters.

quotes.quote: In this figure is shown the connection between NGC1399 and NGC1387 which reflects the ``bridge-like'' stellar steam between the two galaxies made by several filamentary structures, detected by , whose existence had initially been proposed by and confirmed by . This low surface brightness structure seems to be confirmed by the GCs distribution, whose iso-density contour forms a connection between the two galaxies. This suggests an ongoing interaction between the two galaxies where a fraction of GCs, originally belonging to NGC1387 , may have been stripped by the more massive NGC1399 .

sources.summary: The paper finds FCC 184 has anomalously low globular-cluster velocity dispersion and many red, metal-rich clusters whose low rotation suggests a dynamically cold disk-related component.

quotes.quote: FCC 184 is an outlier from this relation with a low velocity dispersion in its GCS. FCC 184 also stands out with having a large number of red, very metal-rich GCs (see Fig. fig:maps6 ) that seem to follow the (low) rotation amplitude of the galaxy as the left panel of Fig. fig:V_and_sig shows. This could indicate that these GCs have a common origin possibly in the disc of FCC 184 and constitute a dynamically cold component in the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 1387 as a bright early-type Fornax-core galaxy west of NGC 1399 and adjusts its simulated tracer velocities to match reported PN kinematics.

quotes.quote: In this area, there are two other bright early-type galaxies: NGC 1404, located just below the cD, in the S-E direction at about 9 arcmin; and NGC 1387, at a distance of 19 arcmin in the West direction from NGC 1399.

For NGC 1387, we take into account the velocity offset of PNe reported by S+18 (i.e. a mean velocity higher by 100 kms ^ 1 than the systemic velocity of the galaxy reported in literature). Indeed in this area, we have a larger number of PNe than GCs, respectively 117 (88 ) and 16 (12 ) within three effective radii from NGC 1387; thus the offset of the PN velocities might generate an overall velocity excess of 100 kms ^ 1 , that we thus artificially add to all simulated points around NGC 1387 in order to match the real objects, see also Table  tab:parameters of the simulation .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1387 as a WISDOM CO example whose exponential intensity profile is easily recovered by the neural-network architecture.

quotes.quote: As seen in Figure fig:NGC1387 , this effect is clearly visible, highlighting the predictable behavioural nature of the network. It is also clear in Figure fig:NGC1387 , that NGC 1387 (FCC184, alfocs , Boyce et al., in prep), an exemplar galaxy from the WISDOM sample, exhibits an exponential black intensity profile which the network can easily recover. Such an example demonstrates the transferable nature of this network architecture and training style but without the difficulties often associated with traditional transfer learning tasks.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 1387 among the Fornax ellipticals, uses its light profile in nova-placement tests, and notes Nova 4 lies at a radius enclosing more than 80 percent of its light.

quotes.quote: This area includes the central dominant (cD) galaxy NGC 1399 and three other elliptical galaxies: NGC 1404, NGC 1387, and NGC 1389.
To answer these questions, we required the light profiles of the four galaxies in our survey region: NGC 1399, NGC 1404, NGC 1389, and NGC 1387.
Nova 4 appears at a radius that encloses over 80 of the light of NGC 1387, while Nova 5 and Nova 6 are even farther outside of NGC 1404.

gmw_00000441.name: NGC 4293
gmw_00000441.overview: NGC 4293 is a highly inclined barred galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161] classified as an anemic or truncated Virgo Cluster member [arXiv:0803.3638]. It hosts a low-mass active galactic nucleus associated with a LINER [arXiv:2211.00048], and its disturbed dust distribution suggests past gravitational interactions [arXiv:0803.3638].

sources.summary: The paper observes NGC 4293's low-mass LINER AGN with VLBA, finding parsec-scale peaked-spectrum radio blobs, jet-medium interaction, and a black-hole mass near 10^6 solar masses.

quotes.quote: We conducted a Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) observation of the low mass active galactic nucleus (AGN) in galaxy NGC 4293 ( z=0.003 ). The object is associated with a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region (LINER). Its black hole mass is estimated as 10^5 or 10^7 M_ . The VLBA 1.5 GHz image shows an inverse symmetric structure with two discrete radio blobs separated by an angular distance of 120 mas, corresponding to 7 parsec. Furthermore, its integrated radio spectrum has a turnover at the frequency of 0.44 GHz. Based on the compactness and spectrum, the nuclear radio source in NGC 4293 belongs to a sample of (megahertz) peaked spectrum (PS/MPS) radio sources with compact symmetric morphologies.

One of the two blobs has a steep radio spectrum =-0.62 0.08 ( S_ ^ + ), while the other one has an inverted spectrum =0.32 0.10 . The VLBA 1.5 GHz luminosity ratio of the two blobs is 3.23 and both blobs show lateral-flowing structures where the hotspots reside at the edge of each radio lobe. This can be explained as jet interactions with dense circumnuclear medium. We estimate the black hole mass of \ through the fundamental plane of black hole activity, which constrains the black hole mass to be 10^6 M_ . It supports that the object is a low-mass AGN and a potential candidate for accreting and ejecting IMBHs.

sources.summary: NGC 4293 is described as a highly inclined barred galaxy with strong dust lanes, no corresponding ionised-gas features, and no signs of ongoing star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4293 is a relatively highly-inclined galaxy with a large-scale bar ls02 . The unsharp-masked image displays a strong dust lane passing through the nucleus and another dust lane about 7 \ south of the centre. No correspondence between the dust lanes and features in the ionised-gas maps is found. We find no signs of ongoing star formation in the / \ map, in agreement with kk04 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4293 as highly inclined, with strong dust lanes near the nucleus and young stars in the innermost dust lane, plus available driftscan spectra.

quotes.quote: NGC 4293 is a relatively highly-inclined galaxy with a strong dust lane passing close to the nucleus and another dust lane about 7 \ south of the centre. Young stars are seen in the dust lane closest to the nucleus. As for several of the galaxies of this sample, driftscan spectra are presented by Gavazzi et al. (2003).

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 4293 as an anemic/truncated Virgo galaxy whose SKB and HI distances agree, while its HI deficiency is linked to interaction signatures and disturbed dust.

quotes.quote: Following the use of the star formation classes introduced by Koopmann Kenney (2004), we can group our sample galaxies as follow Normal galaxies: NGC 4651. Truncated/Normal galaxies: NGC 4351, NGC 4569, NGC 4580, and NGC 4694. Truncated/Compact galaxies: NGC 4064, NGC 4424, and NGC 4606. Anemic or Truncated/Anemic galaxies: NGC 4293, NGC 4429, and NGC 4450.
From inspection of this figure, we conclude that NGC 4293, NGC 4450, NGC 4569, NGC 4580, and NGC 4651 have SKB distances that agree within the errors with those of Solanes.

Others likely owe their HI-deficiency to gravitational interactions, such as NGC 4064 (Cort es 2006) and NGC 4293, which exhibit kinematical peculiarities and disturbed dust distributions, both signatures of gravitational interactions. NGC 4450, NGC 4569, NGC 4580, and NGC 4651 are galaxies further out from the maximum rebound radius, according the SKB distances. The first three galaxies are HI deficient.

gmw_00000442.name: 2MASX J15400795-0318162
gmw_00000442.overview: 2MASX J15400795-0318162 is a cD galaxy identified as the brightest cluster galaxy of Abell 2104 [arXiv:1802.07745]. It is classified as an early-type galaxy with a steeply negative velocity-dispersion slope and hosts active galactic nuclei [arXiv:1802.07745].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J15400795-0318162 as the Abell 2104 cD galaxy and an unusual BCG with a steeply negative velocity-dispersion slope and AGN-bearing X-ray sources.

quotes.quote: From Figures fig:Sigma , fig:Sigmaslope , and fig:variety , it can be seen that one BCG is a clear exception in the BCG sample. The BCG in Abell 2104 has been unambiguously identified as the cD galaxy 2MASX J15400795-0318162 Crawford1999, Liang2000, Bildfell2008, Hoffer2012 . However, we find that the BCG is very unusual in that it has a very negative velocity dispersion slope of --0.2 (see Figure fig:kin8 ), similar to a typical isolated early-type galaxy. Liang2000 construct a galaxy velocity distribution for 47 cluster galaxies within 3000 km s ^ 1 of this central galaxy and show that this galaxy is at rest at the bottom of the cluster potential. Their X-ray imaging shows significant substructure in the centre of the cluster and an overall elliptical appearance, and it appears that the cluster has not yet reached dynamical equilibrium.

Neither the cluster nor the galaxy 2MASX J15400795-0318162 seem unusual in any other property, with the exception that Martini2002 presented deep Chandra observations that revealed a significant X-ray point-source excess over the expectations of blank fields. Their spectroscopy show that all six X-ray sources associated with red counterparts are cluster members and their X-ray properties are consistent with all of them being AGNs. The presence of these AGNs indicate that supermassive black holes have somehow retained a fuel source.

gmw_00000443.name: NGC2768
gmw_00000443.overview: NGC 2768 is a brightest group galaxy [arXiv:0908.3705, arXiv:1802.07745] classified as an early-type E6 or S0 system [arXiv:1209.4351, arXiv:1806.11242]. It features a low-luminosity LINER nucleus [arXiv:1611.00857] and a polar ring structure where the gas and dust rotate orthogonally to its stars [arXiv:astro-ph/0311204, arXiv:astro-ph/0502075].

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 2768's uncertain early-type classification, rotation, group-central role, and extended metallicity profile, including a steep inner gradient.

quotes.quote: NGC 2768 The literature is not unanimous with regards to the morphological classification of NGC 2768, which ranges from elliptical (de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991) to lenticular (Sandage, Tammann van den Bergh 1981; Sandage Bedke 1994). Evidence of rotation has been found by several authors (Fried Illingworth 1994; Emsellem et al. 2004; P09) as well as the presence of a centrally concentrated kinematic twist (McDermid et al. 2006). NGC 2768 is located at the centre of a loose group of which it is the brightest galaxy (Giuricin et al. 2000). No previous metallicity gradient measurement is available, however the measured central metallicity varies from [ Fe/H ] 0.2 to +0.3 (see Howell 2005; Denic olo et al. 2005; Sil'Chenko 2006).

NGC 2768 As can be seen in Fig. fig:NGC2768 , the data for NGC 2768 cover a more extensive radial range than our NGC 1407 data. They also contain more individual measurements than for any other galaxy in our sample and exhibit smaller scatter. The central metallicity value measured by Howell (2005) is consistent with our innermost data points. A visual inspection of Fig. fig:NGC2768 reveals a steep average metallicity gradient in the inner parts that steepens slightly before becoming shallower at (/r_e) 0.1 around a metallicity of [ Fe/H ] =-1.0 .

sources.summary: The paper compares starlight, planetary-nebula, and globular-cluster kinematics in NGC 2768, separating bulge and disk components and arguing for a transformed late-type origin.

quotes.quote: Here we directly compare a sample of planetary nebulae (PNe), globular clusters (GCs) and galaxy starlight velocities out to 4 effective radii, in the S0 galaxy NGC 2768. Using a bulge-to-disk decomposition of a K-band image we assign PNe and starlight to either the disk or the bulge. We show that the bulge PNe and bulge starlight follow the same radial density distribution as the red subpopulation of GCs, whereas the disk PNe and disk starlight are distinct components.

After separating the tracers into bulge and disk components we find the bulge to be a slowly rotating pressure-supported system, whereas the disk reveals a rapidly rising rotation curve with a declining velocity dispersion profile. The resulting V _ rot / ratio for the disk resembles that of a spiral galaxy and hints at an origin for NGC 2768 as a transformed late-type galaxy. A two-component kinematic analysis for a sample of S0s will help to elucidate the nature of this class of galaxy.

NGC 2768 is a rare example of an early-type galaxy with detectable CO emission (Wikland et al. 1995) and the host of a Calcium-rich supernova type Ib (Filippenko Chornock 2000). The effective radius of the galaxy is 1.06 arcmin (Proctor et al. 2009; Cappellari et al. 2011).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2768 as an E/S0 galaxy with a young central disk population, bimodal GC colours, 109 confirmed GCs, and red-GC rotation aligned with the stars.

quotes.quote: NGC 2768 is classified as E 6 in Vaucouleurs and as S 0_ 1/2 in Sandage . Spectroscopic studies of the very central regions McDermid have also suggested a young ( 2.5 Gyr) stellar population associated with the disk, as supported by the recent supernova SN2000ds Filippenko . Kundu studied the HST /WFPC2 photometry for 113 GC candidates in this galaxy, finding a statistically significant probability of it having a bimodal colour distribution.

Using KMM we found the colour distribution to be bimodal, with the blue and red peaks at (R_ C - z) = 0.41 0.01 and 0.70 0.02 respectively. Bimodality was also found in the HST /ACS West pointing used to design the DEIMOS masks in the central region. The colour peaks occur at (F435W F814W) =1.77 0.03 and 2.17 0.01 respectively. We find 109 spectroscopically confirmed GCs over 5 DEIMOS masks observed in sub-arcsec seeing conditions.
We find significant rotation only for the red GCs, which rotate roughly along the photometric major axis in agreement with the host galaxy stars.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC2768 as a dusty early-type galaxy with ambiguous E/S0 classification, polar molecular/dust structure, shifted FIR morphology, gridded SED fits, and centrally concentrated young stars.

quotes.quote: NGC 2768 NGC2768 has been classified as an E6 galaxy in the third reference catalogue of bright galaxies RCC3, devaucouleurs91 , but have reported the galaxy as an almost edge-on S0 galaxy in the Carnegie Atlas of Galaxies. The galaxy is part of the SAURON sample emsellem07 , where it is classified as a fast-rotator with a single component in their stellar kinematic classification scheme. NGC 2768 has a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus (AGN) with a low-ionisation nuclear emission-line region (LINER) spectrum heckman80 , a compact radio core nagar05 and an X-ray source consistent with being a point source komossa99 , its X-ray luminosity is about 10 ^ 40 erg/s boroson11 . It is a fairly isolated galaxy wiklind95 but has been associated with the Lyon Group of Galaxies garcia93 .

ht NGC2768 analysis ht At optical-NIR wavelengths the NGC2768 morphology resembles an ellipsoid elongated in the RA direction, whereas in the MID to FIR the elongation migrates towards the DEC direction. The reported difference in the elongation direction is in agreement with observation of a polar (along minor axis) ring/dust lane observed by in B-R color and by in CO line emission. The CO lines show as well that this ring/disk is rotating (toward us in the south and away from us in the north). These results match observation of the [ OIII ] line from showing the same rotation of the ionized gas.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 2768 as an S0/group galaxy whose globular clusters are mainly spheroid-like, with red clusters rotating at small radii and mergers important to its assembly.

quotes.quote: NGC 2768 is a group galaxy classified as an E6 by vauc91 and as an S0 1/2 by san94 , located at a distance of about 22 Mpc from us tully13 in the direction of the constellation of Ursa Major. It is part of the small Lyon Group of Galaxies 167 garcia93 , and has traces of ionised gas and a dust lane along the minor axis kim98 . It is interesting to add that the ionised gas havs been found to have different kinematics than the stars in the inner regions of the galaxy fried94 .

Particularly, for NGC 2768 we find that its red GCs display rotation preferentially at inner radii ( Re<1 ). In the case of the GC system of NGC 3115 we find a group of GCs with similar kinematics that are not likely to belong to either its spheroid nor disc. For NGC 7457 we find that 70 of its GCs are likely to belong to the disc. Overall, our results suggest that these galaxies assembled into S0s through different evolutionary paths. Mergers seem to have been very important for NGC 2768 and NGC 3115 while NGC 7457 is more likely to have experienced secular evolution.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2768 as an aligned E6/S0 group galaxy with dust, misaligned ionized gas, consistent PNe and GC rotation, and a flat merger-shaped kinematic profile.

quotes.quote: NGC 2768 is classified as an E6/S0 group galaxy Sandage1994 . This galaxy is found to be characterized by red colours with a dust lane running along its photometric minor axis Kim1989 . Ionized gas has also been detected within 20 from the central regions of the galaxy Kim1989 , and, interestingly, this gas is found to be kinematically misaligned with respect to the stars due to its rotation along the photometric minor-axis of the galaxy Wiklind1995,Sarzi2006 .

NGC 2768 is characterized by an increasing stellar rotation velocity profile out to 2 R_ e . We find a rotation amplitude offset of 40 50 kms^ 1 between ATLAS ^ 3D and SLUGGS at 0.4 R_ e , with ATLAS ^ 3D showing a steeper increase of the rotation velocity than SLUGGS. On the other hand, the stellar velocity dispersion profile does not show any offset at 0.4 R_ e and is continuously decreasing out to 2 R_ e . The PNe show rotation velocity consistent with the stars out to 2 R_ e along the photometric major-axis of the galaxy, with a peak in the rotation velocity between 1.2 1.4 R_ e and a subsequent small dip between 2 3 R_ e .
Clear rotation is also seen for both the red and blue GC sub-populations between 1 3 R_ e along the photometric major-axis of the galaxy, similarly to the stars and PNe.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 2768 as a dominant galaxy of a small group in a six-galaxy lenticular sample, using planetary nebulae to separate spheroid and disc kinematics out to large radii.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we therefore apply the kinematic bulge--disc decomposition technique developed in Ari to the sample of six S0s with suitable PNe data presented in Ari2 . This sample was selected to span a range of environments in order to see if the evolutionary properties depend systematically on surroundings: NGC 7457 and NGC 3115 are isolated; NGC 1023 and NGC 2768 are the dominant galaxies of two small groups (5-6 members); while NGC 3384 and NGC 3489 are satellite galaxies of the Leo Group (30 members). The Hubble types of these galaxies and the adopted distance moduli are listed in Table tab:galfitinput .

In this paper, we have analysed the kinematics of a sample of six lenticular galaxies using the planetary nebulae data presented in Ari2 to determine their dynamical properties out to large radii. This analysis has emphasised the importance of treating the spheroidal and disc components of these systems as distinct entities both photometrically and kinematically, and doing a careful job of separating them. Through this process, the rather heterogeneous properties of S0s over-all start to be resolved into more consistent sub-components, and a reasonably coherent picture begins to emerge.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2768 as the strongest emission-line case, lists it as a literature LINER, and excludes it from pure-photoionization analysis because of significant Ne III flux.

quotes.quote: As an independent check of our template definition methodology, all seven of the potential template galaxies were subtracted from the galaxy with the strongest emission lines (NGC 2768) and we determined that the three template galaxies selected resulted in the high reported fluxes (i.e. lowest background).

Four of the galaxies listed in Table Xmetal Line Ratios are reported as low ionization nuclear emission-line regions (LINERs) galaxies in the literature: NGC 2768 LINER_Heckman80 , NGC 3115 Luis_LINER_ngc3115_template , and NGC 4125 and NGC 4261 LINER_SPEC_NGC4261 .

The line ratios for the four LINER galaxies are consistent within the photoionization, but the excitation mechanism and thus the valid regions in diagnostic diagrams are still uncertain and being debated for LINERs (for a review of the physical mechanisms behind LINERs see LINER_review_96 and LINER_review_02 ). Although it is not entirely clear what to expect for LINER galaxies, the presence of strong collisionally excited lines casts a cloud of doubt of pure photoionization. The [ Ne III ] 3869 line is an indicator of the importance of collisional excitation because the collisional cross-section is over an order of magnitude larger than the photoionization transition probability for this line. NGC 2768 and NGC 4125 have significant [ Ne III ] 3869 fluxes and thus we conclude that these systems have a more complex physical structure and excitation mechanism than photoionization. These two galaxies are excluded in the subsequent sections where pure photoionization is assumed.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2768 as a polar-ring galaxy whose stars and gas rotate around perpendicular apparent axes, with additional stellar velocity structure likely caused by dust extinction.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:ngc2768_star_gas presents the stellar (left panel) and gas (right panel) velocity fields for the polar-ring galaxy NGC 2768. The stellar component rotates around the apparent short-axis of the galaxy. The gas, however, rotates around the apparent long-axis, perpendicular to the stars. This illustrates how we can separate the stellar and gas properties, using the optimal template fit. There is some additional structure in the upper regions of the stellar velocity field, likely due to dust extinction.

sources.summary: NGC 2768 is presented as a LINER with dusty filaments and knots extending to 2.4 kpc and a compact north-south spiral of ionized gas embedded in a halo.

quotes.quote: NGC 2768 --- Fig. 1() : Another popular LINER, NGC 2768 displays a rich network of dusty filaments and knots extending to a radius of 2.4 kpc ( 22 ), mostly dominant in the northern part of the galaxy. The main axis of the dust distribution is nearly aligned with the minor axis of the galaxy. The ionized gas is concentrated in a beautiful spiral feature whose long axis is oriented north-south. Its total length is 220 pc ( 2 ) and it appears imbedded in a diffuse halo. Each arm of the spiral consists of a small inner clump (at radii 45 pc ( 0 4 )) completed by a brighter curved knot.

sources.summary: NGC 2768 is described as accreting gas and forming a small inner polar disk, with linked H I and CO structures indicating accreted origin.

quotes.quote: For example, NGC 2768 is accreting gas and a small inner polar disc is forming Crocker08 . It is quite possible that this galaxy will evolve into a system similar to NGC 3414.

A very interesting aspect is that the combination of the CO and \ observations of NGC 2768, NGC 3032, NGC 3489 and NGC 4150 gives clear evidence that these inner gas structures form by the accretion of small-gas-rich objects. The inner gas discs that are seen in NGC 2768, NGC 3489 and NGC 4150 connect to large, faint \ plumes that are seen at larger radii, clearly showing that these gas structures formed from accreted gas.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 2768 as elliptical and links its blue NUV color plus strong radio emission to young stars and recent star formation around SN 2000ds.

quotes.quote: NGC 2768 (label 4) is classified as elliptical.
The host galaxy of SN 2000ds (NGC 2768, label 4) shows a NUV color that is significantly bluer than those of most SN Ia hosts, strongly implying the presence of young stars.

The host galaxy of SN 2000ds (NGC 2768) appears to have properties similar to those of the SNe II host galaxies. The relatively strong radio emission of NGC 2768 may be additional evidence to support the connection between SN 2000ds and recent star formation.

sources.summary: The paper attributes NGC 2768's colour/CaT metallicity disagreement to poor photometry and reports no metallicity or colour bimodality among its CaT-measured globular clusters.

quotes.quote: In five of our eleven galaxies (NGC 1400, NGC 1407, NGC 2768, NGC 3377 and NGC 4494) the KS test (Figure fig:zhist ) provides evidence that the CaT-based metallicities are not drawn from the same distribution as the colour-based metallicities while in a sixth (NGC 4278) the KS test says there is a low but not significant probability that the two metallicities are not drawn from the same distribution.
In the case of NGC 2768 the poor agreement between colour-based and CaT-based metallicity is likely due to the poor photometry available for this galaxy.

In NGC 2768 we do not see metallicity bimodality nor is the colour distribution of GCs with CaT measurements bimodal. Due to the poor quality of the photometry for this galaxy it is unclear whether we should expect metallicity bimodality.

sources.summary: NGC 2768 is described as a flattened E6/S0 with sustained rapid cylindrical rotation, anti-correlated V and h3, and metal-rich globular clusters rotating with the stars.

quotes.quote: This is a well studied flattened (E6/S0) galaxy. Its cylindrical rotational field within 40 arcsec Emsellem:2004kr and sustained overall rapid rotation are obvious in our combined maps and have already been well documented Fried:1994dd,Proctor:2009iy . V and h_3 are globally anti-correlated as expected from an S0 with bulge and disk components of similar scale size and relative brightness ( B/T=0.7 ; Forbes:2012bi ). According to Pota:2013en , the metal-rich GC subpopulation rotates along with the stars, while the metal-poor GCs do not show significant rotation.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 2768 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: This S0 is a field galaxy 2004ApJ...607..810M or member of a poor group 1992A&AS...93..211F . NGC 2768 has a cylindrical velocity field 2004MNRAS.352..721E whose amplitude remains constant at around 130 from the innermost regions 2004MNRAS.352..721E to the outskirts see 2009MNRAS.398...91P, 2013MNRAS.428..389P . Our velocity map is in good agreement with that presented by 2014ApJ...791...80A and extends it beyond 6 . There is some discrepancy between the PN V_ rot and the kinemetry fit from in the innermost radial bins where the disk strongly dominates.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 2768 as a polar-ring early-type galaxy with ionised gas perpendicular to the stars, filamentary minor-axis emission, moderately high line ratios, and similarly oriented dust.

quotes.quote: NGC 2768 is a well-known galaxy where the distribution of the ionised-gas appears to be perpendicular to that of its stellar counterpart (i.e. polar-ring, Fried Illingworth 1994; Bertola et al. 1992). The \ \ EW map shows that the gas distribution has a filamentary morphology along the galaxy minor axis.
The ratio is moderately high in NGC 2768, NGC 2974, and NGC 4278, while it is very low in NGC 4526.
The dust morphology is less defined in NGC 2768 and NGC 4278, but still suggests an orientation for the dust that is similar to that of the gas distribution.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2768 as an early-type galaxy with a polar molecular structure and a central molecular disk lacking a corresponding recent-star-formation signature.

quotes.quote: In the obvious merger remnant NGC 3656, the gas disk is roughly perpendicular to the stellar kinematic major axis ( ), and in NGC 2768 the gas forms a polar ring (or disk) structure ( ), but much more commonly the gas disks are aligned with the mean stellar rotation and suggest a contribution from internal mass loss.

In NGC 2768 the central molecular disk (only 200 pc) is located in a prominent drop in the stellar velocity dispersion, just as one would expect if a dynamically cold stellar disk had formed ( ). Curiously, however, there is no corresponding H absorption feature which would suggest current or recent star formation.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 2768 as a polar-ring elliptical with possible tidal-accretion material, mostly old nuclear stars, compact radio/X-ray activity, and weak optical lines.

quotes.quote: NGC 2768 (L2) This polar ring elliptical galaxy is detected in CO, and may have acquired material during a tidal accretion event Crocker08 . Silchenko06 derive a luminosity-weighted mean age of 11 Gyr for the galaxy's nucleus, although Crocker08 and Kuntschner10 find evidence of a star forming episode occurring a few Gyr ago, Zhang08 find a mean age of 6 Gyr, and Serra08 estimate a mean age of roughly 4 Gyr. In any case, there is little evidence of ongoing star formation.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC2768 as a brightest group galaxy with an unusually positive velocity-dispersion slope, confirmed strong rotation, and lower central velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: A further exception, although less unusual, can be seen in the velocity dispersion profile of BGG NGC2768, which shows a clear positive slope in contrast to the rest of our brightest group galaxies (the only BGG above the y = 0 line in Figures fig:Sigma , fig:Sigmaslope , fig:variety ). Our kinematic results confirm the SAURON kinematic results for this galaxy, which also show strong rotation and a lower central velocity dispersion McDermid2006 . As shown in Veale2017 (for their MASSIVE sample), brightest group galaxies with positive slopes do exist, and NGC 2768 is not that unusual, just an outlier in our sample which consists of generally less-massive brightest group galaxies than the MASSIVE sample.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2768 as having a cylindrical velocity field, a central dispersion dip, and significant dust extinction north of its center.

quotes.quote: This galaxy shows a rather cylindrical velocity field, with a shallow gradient across the rotation axis FI97 . There is a pronounced dip in the central dispersion field, also clearly present in the data of SP97b . Significant dust extinction is present north of the center Mic99 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2768 as having a minor-axis 120 pc dust lane, larger irregular dust, and emission-line gas rotating orthogonally to the main stellar motion.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, only kpc-scale dust lanes, and the 120pc dust lane in NGC 2768, are roughly aligned with the galaxy minor axis. NGC 2768 has large-scale irregular dust with an extent of 1.7 kpc roughly along the minor axis of the galaxy, i.e., parallel to its small dust lane. This irregular dust is associated with an extended disk of emission-line gas which rotates around the major axis of the galaxy, i.e., orthogonally to the main motion of the stars (McDermid ; Sarzi ).

gmw_00000444.name: NGC 185
gmw_00000444.overview: NGC 185 is a dwarf elliptical companion galaxy of M31 [arXiv:astro-ph/9912417, arXiv:1409.7065] that has also been classified as a dwarf spheroidal [arXiv:1902.04291, arXiv:1106.5280]. Unlike many similar dwarfs, it contains centrally concentrated gas, dust, and active star formation within the last 30 million years [arXiv:0903.0589, arXiv:2402.06953].

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 185 with NGC 147 and NGC 205, noting its gas, dust, young stars, bright nucleus, similar structure to NGC 147, and lower globular-cluster fraction.

quotes.quote: NGC 185 and 205 contain neutral hydrogen and molecular gas, dust, and young stars (Baade, 1944a; Baade, 1944b; Hodge, 1963; Lee et al., 1993, Butler Mart i nez-Delgado, 2005; Young Lo, 1997; Sage et al., 1998). Both galaxies reveal bright nuclei with a young star-forming region in the first one, and a relatively young star cluster in the other (see e.g. Da Costa Mould 1988; Young Lo, 1997; Sharina et al., 2006b). NGC 185 and 147 have similar photometric and structural properties (Mateo, 1998). van den Bergh (1998) has shown that these galaxies form a binary system. However, there are no indications of a tidal bridge between them (Battinelli Demers, 2004).

They are slightly nearer to our Galaxy than to M31, and are not supposed to have had close passages to M31. The differences in the gas content and properties of stellar populations between NGC 185 and 147 are not well understood.
This value is higher than for NGC 205 ( T=4 ), and NGC 185 ( T=12.5 ) using the mass estimates of Bender et al. (1991).

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 185 as a Local Group dwarf spheroidal with gas, dust, younger central stars, many LPVs, and a possible distance-modulus correction from LPV relations.

quotes.quote: The two target galaxies of our investigation, NGC 147 and NGC 185 , were discovered by J. Herschel in September 1829 and by W. Herschel in November 1787, respectively, and are known to be members of the M 31 subgroup. Together with NGC 205, they are the most luminous dSphs in the Local Group and are located at an angular distance of approximately 12^ from the Andromeda nebula (van den Bergh ; Corradi ). According to van den Bergh ( ), they are separated by only 58 on the sky without any indication of interaction (Battinelli Demers ; Geha et al. ).

In contrast, various authors found a significant amount of gas and dust in the centre of NGC 185 (Young Lo ; Lee et al. ; Martinez-Delgado Aparicio ; Martinez-Delgado et al. ). Butler Martinez-Delgado ( ) obtained an age of about 400 Myr for the youngest, centrally concentrated stars. Kang et al. ( ) speculate that the M-giant population in NGC 185 contains stars with a wide range of ages, possibly representing two different epochs of star formation at log(t_ yr ) 9.0-9.4 and 7.8-8.5 dex. In the outer parts of NGC 185 stars with ages of at least 1 Gyr are found.
We report the detection of 513 LPVs in NGC 185 and 213 LPVs in NGC 147 , showing amplitudes i of up to 2 ^ mag and periods ranging between 90 and 800 days.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC185 as an extended M31 dwarf elliptical with a regular outer shape, a smaller effective radius than NGC147, a revised luminosity, and a measurable metallicity gradient.

quotes.quote: Received 2014 September 21; in original form 2014 July 12 firstpage - lastpage We exploit data from the Pan-Andromeda Archaeological Survey (PAndAS) to study the extended structures of M31's dwarf elliptical companions, NGC147 and NGC185. Our wide-field, homogeneous photometry allows to construct deep colour-magnitude diagrams (CMDs) which reach down to 3 mag below the red giant branch (RGB) tip. We trace the stellar components of the galaxies to surface brightness of _g 32 mag arcsec ^ 2 and show they have much larger extents ( 5 kpc radii) than previously recognised. While NGC185 retains a regular shape in its peripheral regions, NGC147 exhibits pronounced isophotal twisting due to the emergence of symmetric tidal tails.

We fit single Sersic models to composite surface brightness profiles constructed from diffuse light and star counts and find that NGC147 has an effective radius almost 3 times that of NGC185. In both cases, the effective radii that we calculate are larger by a factor of 2 compared to most literature values. We also calculate revised total magnitudes of M_g=-15.36 0.04 for NGC185 and M_g=-16.36 0.04 for NGC147. Using photometric metallicities computed for RGB stars, we find NGC185 to exhibit a metallicity gradient of [ Fe/H ] 0.15 dex/kpc over the radial range 0.125 to 0.5 deg.

sources.summary: The paper contrasts NGC 185 with NGC 147, describing it as an M31 dwarf elliptical with regular outskirts and less evidence for tidal disruption.

quotes.quote: More recently, data from the the PAndAS survey has been used to explore the outer regions of the dE satellites NGC 147 and NGC 185 to extremely faint surface brightness levels using resolved stars. Projected 100 kpc north of M31, and lying at M31-centric distances of 120 kpc and 180 kpc, respectively mcc12 , these systems are sufficiently remote that contamination from M31 itself is minimal and the main complication is the presence of substantial foreground Galactic populations along their sightlines. crn14 trace both systems to _ g 32 mag arcsec ^ 2 and show that they have much greater extents than previously recognized. As can be seen in Figs. fig:outer_map and fig:rod_halo , NGC 185 retains a regular shape in its peripheral regions while NGC 147 exhibits pronounced isophotal twisting due to the emergence of long symmetric tidal tails. Even neglecting these tails, NGC 147 appears more distended with an effective radius almost three times that of NGC 185.

In contrast to NGC 185, it also exhibits no metallicity gradient. These differences in the structure and stellar populations of the dEs suggest that tidal influences have played an important role in governing the evolution of NGC 147, but not NGC 185. On the assumption that NGC 147, NGC 185 and nearby dwarf spheroidal Cass II form a bound subgroup, ari16 show that it is possible to find orbits around M31 which result in substantial tidal disruption to NGC 147 but not the other two systems.

sources.summary: The paper identifies extended unresolved X-ray emission in NGC 185, argues its morphology follows the starlight, and includes it in a Local Group dwarf-elliptical emissivity comparison.

quotes.quote: Dwarf elliptical galaxies We first examined NGC 185, which has the highest S/N. Fig. f:N185image shows the XMM-Newton image in the 0.5-2 keV band, overlaid by 2MASS Ks-band intensity contours tracing the starlight. In Fig. f:sbd we compared the radial intensity profile of the unresolved X-ray emission with the Ks-band profile. Also plotted are the MOS1 point-spread function (PSF) as dotted line and the pn PSF as dashed line (Ghizzardi 2002). It is evident that the X-ray intensity profile differs from a point source, which indicates that the X-ray emission is truly extended, rather than dominated by a central, compact source.
The similarities in the morphology and radial intensity distribution between the X-rays and near-infrared starlight strongly suggest a stellar origin for the unresolved X-ray emission.
For NGC 147, NGC 185 and NGC 205, the contributions from unresolved LMXBs were found to be 2.2 , 0.7 and 3.8 , respectively, which are negligible.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 185 as a likely member of the NGC 147-CassII subgroup, using genetic-orbit and N-body models to explain why it lacks NGC 147-like tidal features.

quotes.quote: NGC 147, NGC 185 and CassII measured positions and velocities strongly suggest that these three satellites of the Andromeda galaxy are a subgroup of the Local Group. In this work, the results of a statistical analysis show that it is highly unlikely that the similar positions and velocities are due to a chance alignment of the satellites, which further reinforces the sub-group hypothesis. Nevertheless, differences in their star formation history and interstellar medium, and the recent discovery of a stellar stream in NGC 147 combined with the lack of tidal features in the other two satellites seemed to contradict the sub-group scenario, since one would expect that the members of a sub-group suffer similar tidal forces and interactions. However, that is not necessarily the case: using the genetic algorithm we find sets of orbits for which NGC 147 had a closer encounter (and therefore stronger tidal forces) with M31 than the other two satellites of the subgroup.

This scenario, proposed by Irwin et al. (in preparation) as a possible explanation for the observed differences, was tested in this work using N-body simulations. We found that the closer encounter always resulted in the formation of a clear stellar stream in NGC 147, whereas the other two satellites had no significant tidal features. We showed that it is therefore possible to have contrasting internal properties for satellites within a subgroup, and that the tidal stream in NGC 147 could be the result of an interaction with M31 during which the other members of the subgroup were further away and suffered no significant tidal disruption.

sources.summary: The paper re-evaluates NGC 185's proposed pair with NGC 147 using updated distances, finding an 84 kpc separation and supporting the conclusion that the pair is unbound.

quotes.quote: The NGC 147/NGC 185 Pair NGC 147 and NGC 185 are two of the brightest satellite galaxies around M31 and in the Local Group. Due to their close proximity on the sky ( 12 kpc, projected), it has long been suspected that NGC 147 and NGC 185 formed a bound galaxy pair e.g., VandenBergh98,Arias16 . Using information from radial velocities, and adopting a separation of 60 kpc, Geha10 suggested that NGC 147 and NGC 185 are indeed gravitationally bound.

More recently, Sohn20 measured based proper motions for these galaxies and, through orbital history modeling, were able to rule out NGC 147/185 as a bound galaxy pair. This conclusion was drawn assuming a separation of 89 kpc between the two galaxies. Using our distances, we find a separation between NGC 147 and NGC 185 of 83.8^ +25.4 _ 24.6  kpc. This is only marginally smaller than previous determinations and the result of Sohn20 is unaffected.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 147 and NGC 185 as a previously suggested bound pair around M31, close enough to be one of two almost indisputably associated Local Group dwarf pairings.

quotes.quote: Besides the previously recognised pairings of the Magellanic Clouds and of NGC 147/NGC 185, other candidate pairs include the Ursa Minor and Draco dwarf spheroidals, as well as the And I/And III satellites of M31. These pairs are much closer than expected by chance if the radial and angular distributions of satellites were uncorrelated; in addition, they have very similar line-of-sight velocities and luminosities that differ by less than three magnitudes.

An obvious pairing is that of the Magellanic Clouds see, e.g., and references therein Kallivayalil2006b . Around M31, there have been suggestions that NGC 147 and NGC 185 also form a bound pair vandenBergh1998 . If these pairs are bound, their vicinity to their primary galaxy suggests that we are observing them just before they are separated by the tidal field of the main galaxy Besla2007,Sales2011 . This implies very recent accretion and indicates that their occurrence should not be uncommon amongst isolated systems. Taken at face value, the existence of these two pairs of dwarfs seems at odds with the expected rarity of such associations.

sources.summary: NGC 185 is treated as a bright M31 dwarf elliptical with intermediate-age stars, gas and dust, pressure support, and one newly discovered remote globular cluster likely bound to it.

quotes.quote: Three of these are associated with NGC 147 and one with NGC 185. All lie beyond the main optical boundaries of the galaxies and are the most remote clusters yet known in these systems. Radial velocities derived from low resolution spectra are used to argue that the GCs are bound to the dwarfs and are not part of the M31 halo population.

Despite their initial similarities, they exhibit some notable differences. NGC 147 is a typical dE composed primarily of old stars and is dust and gas free YoungLo97 . However, NGC 185 has a substantial number of intermediate age stars MartinezDelgado99 , as well as gas and dust Young01 . Bender91 found NGC 147 to be rotating, while NGC 185 to be entirely pressure supported.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 185 with NGC 147 as an M31 dwarf spheroidal/elliptical pair, compiling 54 distances and finding recent values stable except for low TRGB outliers.

quotes.quote: Our exhaustive exploration of these 12,000 publications led to inclusion of a total of 131 newly reported distances to M33 since records began in 1926, as well as 38 to M32 (since 1944), and 37, 54, 43, 46, and 145 distance estimates to, respectively, NGC 147, NGC 185, NGC 205, IC 10, and IC 1613.

The published distance moduli for both NGC 147 and NGC 185 have reached an approximately stable level in recent years. Notable outliers occurred, however (NGC 147: Kang et al. 2009, only provided as an abstract; NGC 185: Sohn et al. 2008). Both articles, published by a subset of the same authors, provided TRGB-based estimates that were systematically lower than the long-term average values. These systematically smaller distance estimates may be owing to a combination of the authors' use of the Yonsei--Yale isochrones for their calibration instead of the more often used Padova isochrones and their NIR JHK calibration instead of the more customary I band (or equivalent) calibration.

For both galaxies, post-1990 independent distance tracers---such as those based on SBFs, horizontal-branch stars (Han et al. 1997; Butler Mart nez-Delgado 2005), ( K band) long-period variables (Lorenz 2011; Lorenz et al. 2011, 2012), and kinematics-based methods (Devereux et al. 2009)---lead to estimated distance moduli that are fully consistent with our weighted means.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 185 as the brighter member of a bound dwarf spheroidal pair near M31, assigning it equal mass and core radius to NGC 147.

quotes.quote: All of the presently available observational data are consistent with the conclusion that NGC 147 and NGC 185 form a gravitationally bound system (see e.g., and references therein). Both NGC 147 and NGC 185 are located in the constellation of Cassiopeia and they are members of the Local group of galaxies and they form a bound pair of satellite galaxies of the Andromeda Galaxy (M31). NGC 147 and NGC 185 are located in the sky about 6 degrees north of M31 and are separated by around 1 degree. NGC 185 is the brighter of the two galaxies (visual magnitude 10.1) and lies at a distance of around 2.05 10^6 ly from us, while NGC 147 has a visual magnitude of 10.5 and lies at a distance of around 2.53 10^6 ly.

In these units the values of the involved parameters, for NGC 147 and NGC 185, are: M_1 = M_2 = 0.1 , c_1 = c_2 = 0.25 , while R = 6 , according to and . For simplicity in the numerical calculations we assume that both dwarf spheroidal galaxies NGC 147 and NGC 185 are identical, thus having the same mass as well as the same core radius.

sources.summary: The paper measures NGC 185's first proper motion, reconstructs its orbit around M31, and links its lack of tidal tails to earlier or more distant pericentric passages.

quotes.quote: We present the first proper motion (PM) measurements for the dwarf elliptical galaxies NGC 147 and NGC 185, two satellite galaxies of M31, using multi-epoch \ imaging data with time baselines of 8 years. For each galaxy, we take an error-weighted average of measurements from \ ACS/WFC and WFC3/UVIS to determine the PMs. Our final results for the PMs are ( , )_ N147 = (-0.0232, 0.0378) (0.0143, 0.0146) for NGC 147, and ( , )_ N185 = (-0.0242, 0.0058) (0.0141, 0.0147) for NGC 185.

The lack of tidal tails around NGC 185 is consistent with the orbital results, which indicate that NGC 185 either passed around M31 at earlier times (1.5--2 Gyr ago) and that these tidal tails are no longer visible today or that NGC 185 passed nearby M31 at very early times ( > 6 Gyr ago) and at larger distances ( >200 kpc) such that no tidal features ever existed. Future N body models will help confirming (or refuting) such claims. The disparities in gas content and SFHs found for NGC 147 and NGC 185 can be attributed to the fact that the two galaxies have different orbital characteristics.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 185 as a dwarf elliptical with declining SFH from gas depletion, yet finds evidence for star formation within the last 30 Myr.

quotes.quote: This characteristic is evident in the SFHs of the dwarf elliptical galaxies NGC 147 and NGC 185 of Figure fig:SFHs_All . Over time, due to the depletion of gas, the SFHs of NGC 147 and NGC 185 have progressively declined, and they now comprise nearly no young stars such as RSGs, as depicted in Figure fig:SFH_N185 . 2017MNRAS.466.1764H investigated the SFHs of NGC 147 and NGC 185 using the method of counting LPVs, with their results displayed in Figure fig:SFHs_All . These LPVs are low and intermediate mass AGBs, which also serve as tracers in our sample.

Therefore, the SFHs prior to 0.3 Gyr measured by 2017MNRAS.466.1764H align with those found in this work. When compared to 2014ApJ...789..147W , as demonstrated in Figure fig:Cumulative_SFHs_All , it becomes apparent that NGC 147 and NGC 185 exhibited star formation activity as recent as 30 Myr ago, evidenced by the relatively young stellar populations in this work restricting the SFHs.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 185 as a dwarf elliptical satellite closely matching NGC 147 structurally, but with recent star formation and substantial gas because M31's disk shields it from UV radiation.

quotes.quote: The two dwarf elliptical satellites of M31, NGC 147 and NGC 185, appear to be almost identical in many respects you97a : they have comparable blue luminosities, Holmberg diameters, B--V colors, average surface brightness, light profile shapes, mean stellar dispersions, and projected distances from M31. Both galaxies show evidence for an intermediate-age stellar component you97a . Despite all these similarities, NGC 147 and NGC 185 are strikingly different in their recent SFHs, and in the present day neutral gas content. NGC 185 formed stars as recently as 20 Myr ago lee93a , and contains substantial amounts of ( 1.1 10^5 M _ ) and H _2 ( 4.1 10^4 M _ ) you01 .
We argue that the differences between NGC 147 and NGC 185 can be explained by different fluxes of the LyC and FUV radiation escaping from M31 at the present locations of the dwarfs.
It appears that NGC 185 is located almost in the plane of the M31 disk, and is therefore shielded from the FUV and LyC electromagnetic radiation by the disk of M31.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 185 as an unusual M31 companion with old stars plus gas, dust, intermediate-age populations, extended star-cluster formation, and a central star-forming gas complex.

quotes.quote: NGC 205 and NGC 185 are very unusual galaxies and do not resemble any galaxy within 10 Mpc. Both have a regular spheroidal shape and are composed mainly of old stars. At the same time, both galaxies have a considerable amount of gas, dust, and a significant intermediate-age stellar component.

Star cluster formation seems to be a more extended process in NGC 185. A star forming region of H + [ NII ] emission with a diameter of 50 pc near the center of NGC 185 may be a probable place of a massive young star cluster birth according to a modern evolutionary scheme (Gallagher Grebel, 2001 and references therein). Young Lo (1997) estimated the mass of the brightest central HI clump in NGC 185 M_ HI =0.7 10^4 M_ and the corresponding virial mass a factor of 100 higher.

sources.summary: The paper treats the carbon stars in NGC 185 as members, finds more carbon stars than predicted, and combines SPLASH measurements with literature FBPS observations for its carbon-star population.

quotes.quote: Using the membership criteria outlined in membership , we find that all carbon stars in the dSph fields are unambiguous members of their respective satellite. The same is true for the carbon stars in NGC 185.
However, in And II, NGC 147, NGC 185 and NGC 205 we observe far more carbon stars than are predicted, despite the fact that our calculated C/M ratios match those in the literature.

In addition to the new fields discussed above, we also compile carbon star populations in And II, NGC 147 and NGC 185 from the literature. We combine our carbon star samples observed by SPLASH with these samples from the literature. The observations of And II Kerschbaum2004 , NGC 147 and NGC 185 Nowotny2003 also use FBPS, but with V and i_0 as the broad-band colors rather than R and I .

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 185 with NGC 147, finding an earlier main star-formation epoch, continued low-level activity until 100 Myr ago, and a larger produced stellar mass.

quotes.quote: The Andromeda satellite pair NGC147/185 show different histories; the main epoch of star formation for NGC 185 occurred 8.3 Gyr ago, followed by a much lower, but relatively constant star formation rate (SFR). In the case of NGC 147, the SFR peaked only 6.9 Gyr ago, staying intense until 3 Gyr ago.

NGC 147 and NGC 185 are two of the Andromeda satellites, which despite their similar mass and morphological type, show different SFHs. Using the catalogue of LPV stars from we estimated that the star formation started earlier in NGC 185 (8.3 Gyr ago) than in NGC 147 (6.9 Gyr ago) and continued over the past 6 Gyr until 100 Myr ago, albeit at a much lower rate, while no star formation is seen for the past 300 Myr in NGC 147 (Fig. fig3 ). We calculated the total stellar masses of M 1.13 10^8 M _ and M 2.3 10^8 M _ in NGC 147 and NGC 185, respectively ( ).

sources.summary: The review notes NGC 185's dwarf-spheroidal and Seyfert-like classifications, argues stellar processes may mimic Seyfert line ratios, and uses planetary-nebula abundances in chemical-evolution modeling.

quotes.quote: The case of the latter is indeed curious, since to NGC 185 it was not only attributed the classification as a dSph, but also as a Seytfert galaxy. Although its emission lines place it in the category of Seyferts, the galaxy was not detected either in radio surveys or in 6 and 21 cm, or X-ray observations, which probably indicates that the Seyfert-like line ratios may be produced by stellar processes. The strong emission-lines from the post-AGB population could perhaps fake a Seyfert galaxy. had, instead, shown that a combination of PNe plus supernova remnants is the responsible for the mimic. (O/H) from PNe are used in the chemical evolution models of this work to predict the supernova rates and to support the idea that supernova remnants should then be present in NGC 185, as in fact observed ( ).

sources.summary: The paper uses the first HST proper motions and abundance-matched halo modeling for NGC 185, deriving an infall halo mass and maximum circular velocity for M31 mass inference.

quotes.quote: The first PMs of NGC 147 and NGC 185 were recently measured using HST sohn20 . To determine the appropriate maximum circular velocity values of these two dwarf elliptical galaxies, we first use the abundance matching relation from moster13 These abundance matched masses are consistent with those from gk17a , despite the well-known discrepancies for different SMHM relations in the regime of low-mass galaxies. to find the infall halo mass for NGC 147 and NGC 185 using the stellar masses reported in mcconnachie18 . From this relation, we determine the following infall halo masses: 5 10^ 10 M_ (NGC 147) and 4.5 10^ 10 M_ (NGC 185).
The best-fit NFW halo profiles result in maximum circular velocities of 61 km s ^ 1 (NGC 147) and 73 km s ^ 1 (NGC 185).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC185 as a gas-poor dwarf companion of M31 with signs of earlier infall and absorber associations entangled with M31 and NGC205.

quotes.quote: Two dwarf elliptical companions of M31, NGC185 and NGC205, have only small amounts of gas relative to their total mass and given their proximity to M31 are identified to be potentially associated with some absorbers. NGC205 lies at a projected distance of only 37 arcmin ( 9 kpc) from M31 and shows evidence of distortion in its outer isophotes that are presumed to be the result of tidal interaction with M31 hodge_ngc205, sato86_ngc205, choi_ngc205 . Another dwarf companion, NGC185, lies at a projected distance of 7 ( 140 kpc) from M31 and shows less evidence for dynamical interactions with M31 held92_ngc185 , but the star formation history of NGC185 implies an earlier infall time into the M31 environment geha15_ngc185 . A recent proper-motion (PM) measurement shows the connection between NGC185 and M31 through alignment of rotation planes paw21_ngc185 .

For example, absorbers along sight lines HS0033+4300 (ID 22), HS0058+4213 (ID 23), IO-AND (ID 24), MRK352 (ID 42), RX-J0043.6+3725 (ID 72), RX-J0050.8+3536 (ID 73), and Zw535.012 (ID 92) are listed to be associated with M31 and either NGC185 or NGC205 or sometimes all three simultaneously. The multiple associations of absorbers in the direction of M31 imply a spatial and kinematic proximity between M31 and its satellites NGC185 and NGC205.

sources.summary: The review discusses NGC 185 carbon-star surveys, noting independent counts, C/M ratios, field coverage, and the concentration of its C-stars in the main body.

quotes.quote: NGC 147 NGC 185 Nowotny et al. (2003) and Battinelli Demers (2004b,) independently present data on these two companions to M31, while Harbeck et al. (2004) observed NGC 147. Nowotny et al. find 146 C-stars and a C/M ratio of 0.15 in NGC 147, and 154 and 0.089 in NGC 185, respectively, over an un vignetted FOV of approximately 33 arcmin ^2 . Battinelli Demers (2004c) find 288 C-stars and a C/M ratio of 0.24 for NGC 147 and Battinieli Demers (2004b) find 145 C-stars and a C/M ratio of 0.17 for NGC 185, both over an area of 1180 arcmin ^2 . Figure 3 in Battinieli Demers (2004b) shows that most of their C-stars are located in the main body of NGC 185, which is still well covered by the smaller FOV of the Nowotny et al. observations. This is consistent with the very similar numbers of C-stars discovered in both surveys.

sources.summary: The paper monitors NGC 185 as one of four Local Group dwarfs, reports no novae, and calculates upper limits on its bulk and luminosity-specific nova rates.

quotes.quote: We report upper limits on the bulk nova rates in NGC 147 of 2 yr ^ 1 and NGC 185 of 1.8 yr ^ 1 and a combined bulk nova rate for the four galaxies of 4^ +4.2 _ 1.4 yr ^ 1 (2 ^ +3.9 _ 1.4 yr ^ 1 without the NGC 205 nova candidate). The bulk rates we report here are based on Monte Carlo simulations using nova maximum magnitudes and decline rates and individual epoch frame limits. From the Monte Carlo rates, integrated and extinction corrected V-band photometry, and (V-K) _0 colors we derive a luminosity specific nova rate for M32 of 12.0 ^ +14.4 _ 6.0 yr ^ 1 [ 10^ 10 L_ ,K ] ^ 1 and for NGC 205 of 29.3 ^ +32.3 _ 14.7 yr ^ 1 [ 10^ 10 L_ ,K ] ^ 1 and for the combined 4 galaxies of 14.1 ^ +14.8 _ 4.9 yr ^ 1 [ 10^ 10 L_ ,K ] ^ 1 (7.0 ^ +13.7 _ 4.9 yr ^ 1 [ 10^ 10 L_ ,K ] ^ 1 without the NGC 205 nova candidate).

For NGC 147 and NGC 185 we can place a limit on the nova rate by saying that it is no greater than the inverse of the survey time. For NGC 147 this gives R < 1 / 0.315 yr = 3.18 yr ^ 1 and for NGC 185 this gives R < 1 / 0.323 yr = 3.10 yr ^ 1 . The Monte Carlo Approach sha01 describe a Monte Carlo technique which uses the maximum magnitudes and decline rates of novae and their survey faint limit to find the most probable nova rate in their survey region.

We could not verify the nova candidate in NGC 205 conclusively and so we also report upper limits on the bulk nova rates based on Monte Carlo simulations for NGC 205 of 1.5 yr ^ 1 , for NGC 147 of 2 yr ^ 1 , and for NGC 185 of 1.8 yr ^ 1 . We also calculated a combined bulk nova rate for the four dEs of 4 ^ +4.2 _ 1.4 yr ^ 1 (2 ^ +3.9 _ 1.4 yr ^ 1 without the NGC 205 nova candidate).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 185 as an Andromeda dwarf elliptical with five globular clusters, fast predicted dynamical decay, low mass-to-light ratio, and a globular-cluster system more extended than its stars.

quotes.quote: Five GCs were identified in the dwarf elliptical satellite of Andromeda, NGC 185 (Hodge 1974). Since the stellar core radius of NGC 185 is only 0.1 kpc, Tremaine (1976) derived a decay rate four times faster than in Fornax. The explanation of an extended dark halo for the problem of the survival of those GCs may be problematic as the value of the mass-to-light ratio for this galaxy of 3 is rather low (Held et al. 1992) and, hence, the interaction with the stars rather than with the dark matter will dominate the drag, with no much room left to dilute the effect of the friction (see the two-component formula, Eq. eq:TwoCompDF ). However, the closest GC to the centre of this galaxy is at a projected distance of 6 _ 0.6 kpc, well outside the core of the galaxy. NGC 185 is a clear case where the globular cluster system is much more extended than the stellar population.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 185 is unlikely to share an orbit with NGC 147, but its orbital parameters closely match Cass II, favoring that association.

quotes.quote: NGC 147 ( a = 117 6 kpc, e = 0.23 0.08 ) and NGC 185 ( a = 180 8 kpc, e = 0.22 0.07 ) are very close together on the sky and a connection between them has long been suspected. However the orbits we find for these two objects in our study do not support such a conclusion. Their eccentricities are similar, but their semi-major axes are quite different, so much so that NGC 147 completes two orbits in the time that NGC 185 takes to complete one.
Our results certainly favour a connection between Cass II and NGC 185 as the orbital parameters we find for them are in close agreement, but not for Cass II and NGC 147.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 185 orbits and test-particle streams around M 31, suggesting that a primeval stellar stream from its outskirts could appear relatively tight.

quotes.quote: The orbits of NGC 185 (black) and NGC 147 (red) are similar in Solutions 1 and 2 and rather different in Solution (3).

Figure Fig:7 shows streams of test particles around M 33 in the left panel, NGC 185 in the central panel, and NGC 147 in the right-hand panel. The test particles plotted as yellow were initially closer to the galaxy than r_g/2 , and red initially at r_g/2 to 3r_g/4 , where r_g 100 kpc in M 33 and 40 kpc in NGC 185 and NGC 147.
A primeval stream of stars drawn from the outskirts of NGC 185 could appear somewhat tighter, and a stream from NGC 147 could be tighter still, though both avoid M 33.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 185 with M31 high-luminosity dSph counterparts, cites its published spectroscopy, and compares its planetary-nebula oxygen abundance to stellar metallicity.

quotes.quote: The dEs of M31 (NGC 147, 185, and 205) seem to be higher luminosity counterparts to LG dSphs. Therefore, we classify those three galaxies as dSphs.

The details of the spectroscopy have been published by NGC 147, 185, and 205 geh06,geh10 , Andromeda I, II, III, VII, and X kal09,kal10 , additional Andromeda II spectra ho12 , Andromeda XIV maj07 , and Andromeda V, IX, XV, and XVIII and additional spectroscopy of Andromeda III and XIV tol12 .
Similarly, the PN abundances for NGC 147 and 185 are O/H = -0.6 gon07 and 0.5 ric95 , respectively, whereas our stellar metallicities are = and .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 185 in the M31 satellite-plane discussion but treats it, with NGC 147, as too massive for the NGC 205 sub-subhalo group.

quotes.quote: Therefore, we have 13 satellite galaxies that are part of the VTC plane (including NGC 147, NGC 185, AND XII, and AND XXVI); another 2 (AND XIII and AND XXVII) that are in the plane, but not co-rotating; M31 and NGC 205, which are expected to be there as the prior of the scenario; and M32 which is assumed to be in a short period orbit of M31 and therefore neglected.

This group excludes NGC 147 and 185 since they are too massive to be sub-subhalos bound to NGC 205, as well as AND XIII XXVII since they are counter rotating. The former two satellites are often considered to be tidally bound to each other (however, see evslin14 ), but are far too bright to have formed in the subhalo of NGC 205, i.e. to be associated with a sub-subhalo. The most logical explanation would be that they are each associated with another, less massive subhalo of M31 and became bound to NGC 205 during their orbit towards M31, similar to the LMC and the SMC and NGC 147 185 themselves.

sources.summary: The paper reports eight globular clusters associated with NGC 185 and says tidal distortion plus central stellar concentration make its derived halo mass uncertain.

quotes.quote: NGC 147 and NGC 185 Several new GCs have been discovered by the PAndAS survey bringing the total number of GCs to 10 associated with NGC 147 and 8 associated with NGC 185. The details of these GCs including their luminosities are given by 2013MNRAS.435.3654V . The two galaxies are likely tidally interacting with one another, with NGC 147 exhibiting pronounced isophotal twists 2014MNRAS.445.3862C . As with NGC 205, this tidal distortion, and the significant central concentration of stars in NGC 147 and NGC 185, leads to large uncertainties on our derived M_ 200 for these dwarfs (see Section 2).

sources.summary: The paper contrasts NGC 185 with NGC 147, finding earlier formation, continued recent star formation, and central gas in NGC 185.

quotes.quote: This new technique was successfully used in M 33 (Javadi \ 2011a,b, 2017), the Magellanic Clouds (Rezaeikh \ 2014), NGC 147 NGC 185 (Golshan \ 2017) and IC 1613 (Hashemi \ 2018). The main results that we found in these galaxies are summarized as below: The disc of M 33 was built > 6 Gyr ago, when most stars in M 33 73 were formed. The second enhanced epoch of star formation in M 33 occurred 250 Myr ago and contributed 6 to M 33's historic star formation. Radial star formation history profiles suggest that the inner disc of M 33 was formed in an inside--out formation scenario. We found a significant difference in the ancient SFH of the LMC and the SMC. For the SMC the bulk of the stars formed a few Gyr later than the LMC. A secondary peak of SFH at 700 Myr ago in the LMC and the SMC is possibly due to the tidal interaction between the Magellanic Clouds and their approach to the Milky Way. In spite of similar mass and morphological type, NGC 147 and NGC 185, which are two of the massive satellites of the Andromeda galaxy reveal completely different SFHs. NGC 185 formed earlier than NGC 147 but its star formation continued until recent times with almost constant rate while the star formation in NGC 147 quenched at least 300 Myr ago. These results are corroborated by strong tidal distortions of NGC 147 and the presence of gas in the centre of NGC 185.

sources.summary: The paper derives a JAGB distance for NGC 185 from published near-infrared photometry of its reddest stars and compares it with five TRGB distances.

quotes.quote: These TRGB moduli average out to < _o (TRGB) > = 20.73 0.02 mag (error on the mean), in good agreement with the estimate using the JAGB method above. NGC 185 JAGB stars: Near-infrared JHK' observations of NGC 185 were obtained by Kang et al. (2005) using the CFHT-IR camera on the CFHT 3.6m at Mauna Kea. Photometry for only the 30 reddest stars was published.

sources.summary: NGC 185 is described as an M31 companion with eight known globular clusters; the observed clusters have similar metallicities and enhanced alpha-element abundances.

quotes.quote: NGC 185 As in NGC 147, the presence of gc in NGC 185 was first noted by Baade1944a . Today, a total of eight gc are known in NGC 185 Veljanoski2013 . Interestingly, the three gc that we have observed in this M31 companion all have nearly identical [ Fe/H ] 1.75 , and the elements (Si, Ca, and Ti) are enhanced by similar amounts as in Milky Way gc at this metallicity. Larger variations are seen in the abundances of Na and Mg, perhaps related to the presence of multiple populations.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 185 as an M31 dwarf elliptical with dedicated proper-motion observations; unlike NGC 147, it lacks a large systematic offset but retains increased uncertainties.

quotes.quote: As additional test cases of the limits of our method at M31 distances, we measure PMs for NGC 147, NGC 185, and Andromeda VII (And VII). NGC 147 and NGC 185 are relatively bright dwarf elliptical (dE) galaxies sitting comfortably within the halo of M31 (107 and 154 kpc from M31; mcconnachie2012,savino+2022 ) with ACS/WFC-based PMs and orbital histories presented in .

For NGC 185, though done with very similar observations, we do not see the same large, obvious systematic offset that we observe for NGC 147. However, we do see an obvious increase in the uncertainties from the uncertainty on the \ positions of these stars -- in fact, for some of the brightest sources, the uncertainties on stellar positions are of the same order as the uncertainties from .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 185 as an M31 dwarf elliptical with multiple stellar populations and derives its TRGB distance from annular photometry.

quotes.quote: NGC 185 NGC 185 ( 0 h  38 m  58.0 s , +48^  20'  15'' ) is another dwarf elliptical companion to M31. found strong evidence for three distinct stellar populations: a well defined RGB, indicating a mean metallicity of Fe/H = -1.23 0.16 , with a large dispersion; a bright AGB population above the TRGB, implying the presence of a strong intermediate age population; and some young stars with blue - yellow colours, suggesting that NGC 185 has Population I stars as well as Population II (see also hodge1963,hodge1973,gallagher1981,johnson1983,wiklind1986 ). The CMD and RGB luminosity functions displayed in Figure  ngc185 are constructed from all the stars located further than 0 1 from the centre of NGC 185, but interior to 0 2. By only sampling stars in a ring around NGC 185, we negate the possible effects of crowding in the inner regions while still leaving a large enough area to act as a reference field.

sources.summary: The paper measures NGC 185 as an M31 dwarf elliptical with extended kinematic profiles, significant rotation, and dynamical models requiring modest dark matter.

quotes.quote: 4 Department of Astronomy, University of Washington, Seattle, WA 98195-1580 5 Hubble Fellow, California Institute of Technology, Department of Astronomy, 1200 E California Blvd, Pasadena, CA 91125 footnote We present kinematic and metallicity profiles for the M 31 dwarf elliptical (dE) satellite galaxies NGC 147 and NGC 185.
We measure a maximum rotational velocity of 17 2 \ for NGC 147 and 15 5 \ for NGC 185.
We construct two-integral axisymmetric dynamical models and find that the observed kinematical profiles cannot be explained without modest amounts of non-baryonic dark matter.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC185 as one of the Local Group field dwarfs whose radius-velocity distribution gives a high likelihood of past passage through the Milky Way.

quotes.quote: Comparison of the radius-velocity distributions of VLII populations and measurements of Local Group galaxies presents a strong likelihood that Tucana, Cetus, NGC3109, SextansA, SextansB, Antlia, NGC6822, Phoenix, LeoT, and NGC185 have passed through the Milky Way.

From Table t4 we expect that the following Local Group Objects have with high likelihood ( > 50 per cent) at some point in time passed through the virial radius of the Milky Way: NGC3109, SextansA, SextansB, Antlia, Cetus, Tucana, NGC6822, Phoenix, LeoT, and NGC185.

The separation between the associated and unassociated populations in the distance-velocity plane in VLII was applied in the Local Group to identify field dwarfs that may be associated with the Milky Way: Tucana, Cetus, Antlia, NGC3109, SextansA, SextansB, NGC6822, Phoenix, LeoT and NGC185.

sources.summary: NGC 185 is described as an M31 dwarf elliptical companion whose neutral gas is centrally concentrated, with CNM and WNM phases and less systematic kinematics than the CHVC comparison objects.

quotes.quote: The dwarf elliptical companions of M31, NGC 185 and NGC 205 (Young and Lo ) are rather different in their gas properties.
