Figure 2 shows the C ^+ spectra, spatially integrated over the blueshifted North galaxy, the redshifted South galaxy, and the total spectrum, spatially integrated over both North+South galaxies together. Both the North and South C ^+ line profiles appear roughly Gaussian, with large linewidths, 300 and 410 , respectively. These are linewidths typical of individual galaxies, or individual, rapidly rotating, compact circumnuclear disks, rather than linewidths expected at opposite ends of a large ( 10 kpc), quiescent, rotating disk.

sources.summary: The paper models 3-586.0 as the foreground z=1.224 elliptical lens near HDF850.1, fitting its light with galfit and adopting an SIE lens model to assess magnification.

quotes.quote: We modelled the foreground lensing galaxy, 3-586.0, with the galfit package (Peng et al. 2010a). Our approach in modelling the lens is to use the smallest number of components possible, i.e. keeping the model as simple as possible. The objective is a purely phenomenological description of this galaxy for the purposes of subtracting its flux. At 814 nm, the lens is modelled as the sum of two S e rsic profiles, though allowing five Fourier distortions of the smaller profile.

Both HDF850.1 and HDF8501.K have been argued to be only moderately lensed by the foreground z=1.224 elliptical galaxy 3-586.0. In this section we critically re-assess these claims. We adopt a Singular Isothermal Ellipsoid (SIE) model for the foreground lens, because in the image plane at the locations of HDF850.1 and HDF850.1K, there are only very small differences between the SIE model and a composite Navarro-Frenk-White profile plus de Vaucouleurs' profile (e.g. Gavazzi et al. 2007) .

sources.summary: The survey detects CO(2-1) from HDF850.1, treats it as a known bright dust-obscured z=5.183 starburst, and notes radio/submillimeter detections and a large molecular gas reservoir.

quotes.quote: COLDz.GN.31 : We also detect CO(2--1) line emission from the bright, dust-obscured starbursting galaxy HDF850.1 ( z =5.183), with a moderate significance of SNR=5.3. We include this line detection here given the known match, but we do not include it in the statistical analysis because it does not reach the significance threshold for detection by the blind line search. The measured flux is compatible with the previously reported flux of 0.17 0.04 Jy km s ^ 1 Walter12 . It is detected by the 5.5 GHz eMERGE survey, with a flux of 14 3 Jy eMerge , but is not detected at 1.4 GHz Morrison10 . This source is also detected at 850 m with a significance of 7.1 and a flux of 5.9 1.3 mJy Geach17 .

With this CO deep field study, we also further demonstrate that blind CO searches are sensitive to ``optically dark", dust-obscured galaxies at very high redshift, such as GN10 and HDF850.1. In particular, the massive molecular gas reservoirs of these galaxies are among the largest in our field (Riechers et al., in prep.).

sources.summary: The paper discusses HDF850.1 as a dust-obscured z~5.2 SMG embedded in a galaxy overdensity, with uncertain B19 but separate AH18 overdensity detection.

quotes.quote: Similarly, the z=5.2 SMG HDF850.1 in our sample is embedded in an overdensity of galaxies with spectroscopic redshifts that agree well with that of HDF850.1 Walter12 . Calvi21 showed none of their 23 spectroscopically confirmed members are SMGs. They are instead in majority LAEs or Lyman break galaxies (LBGs).

Many SMGs are indeed undetected in the optical wavelengths, as for example it is the case for GN10 and HDF850.1, which are completely dust-obscured Daddi09, Walter12,Calvi21 , so that it is difficult to estimate their stellar masses. Out of the four distant SMGs that fall in the z 3.35-6.8 redshift range of AH18, overdensities are detected around HDF850.1 and GN10, separately. In other cases (e.g., HDF850.1, SMM J123634, SMM J123606, SMM J123618, SMM J123707, SMM J123711, and SMM J123712) our detected overdensities have evidence for large-scale filamentary or clumpy structures, as typically found in still-assembling protoclusters such as SSA22 at z=3.09 Steidel98,Umehata2019 , the Spiderweb at z=2.16 Jin21 , and Hyperion at z=2.45 Cucciati2018 .

sources.summary: The paper treats GOODS 850-1 as a z=5.183 SMA source that is optically faint, lacks clean Herschel fluxes, but still has constrained FIR-radio q values.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Submillimeter Properties of the SMA Sample: 850-1 & 14/850.1 & 189.216614 & 62.207165

quotes.quote: In Cowie et al. \ (2009), we presented GOODS 850-1 (GN14 or HDF 850.1), which continues to elude detection at optical/near-infrared (NIR) wavelengths but now has a redshift of z=5.183 from millimeter spectroscopy (Walter et al. \ 2012).

We did not include GOODS 850-1 (GN14/HDF850.1) in our clean SMA sample, because the Herschel fluxes are too low to be confidently measured (see Figure A1). However, at the known z=5.183 redshift of the source (Walter et al. \ 2012), the observed-frame 860 m lies close to the rest-frame 42.5-122.5 m band, which means we can strongly constrain the q value even without the Herschel data. In Figure fig10:8501 , we show fits to our 860 m SMA data and the 1100 m data ( 2.87 1.25 ) from Chapin et al. \ (2009; their Table A3) assuming =1 and T=47 K (red solid curve) or =1 and the range T=42 to 55 K (red dashed curves). The corresponding value of q is 2.34 with a range from 2.22 to 2.50.

sources.summary: HDF 850.1 is modeled as a z=5.183 high-redshift submillimeter galaxy whose CO and [C II] lines favor unvirialized molecular clouds with distinct CO and C+ layers under cosmological mass constraints.

quotes.quote: walt have recently identified the J=6-5, 5-4, and 2-1 CO rotational emission lines, and [ C II ] fine-structure emission line from the star-forming interstellar medium in the high-redshift submillimeter source HDF 850.1, at z = 5.183. We employ large velocity gradient (LVG) modeling to analyze the spectra of this source assuming the [ C II ] and CO emissions originate from (i) separate unvirialized regions, (ii) separate virialized regions, (iii) uniformly mixed unvirialized region, and (iv) uniformly mixed virialized regions.

For HDF 850.1 we find that a model in which the CO and C ^+ are uniformly mixed in gas that is shielded from UV radiation, requires a cosmic-ray or X-ray ionization rate of 10 ^ 13 s ^ 1 , plausibly consistent with the large star-formation rate ( 10 ^3 M _ yr ^ 1 ) observed in this source. Enforcing the cosmological constraint posed by the abundance of dark matter halos in the standard CDM cosmology and taking into account other possible contributions to the total gas mass, we find that three of these four models are less likely at the 2 level. We conclude that modeling HDF 850.1's ISM as a collection of unvirialized molecular clouds with distinct CO and C ^+ layers, for which = 0.6 M _ (K km s ^ 1 pc ^2 ) ^ 1 for the CO to H _2 mass-to-luminosity ratio, (similar to the standard ULIRG value), is most consistent with the CDM cosmology.

sources.summary: The paper identifies HDF850.1 as a z=5.183 SMG galaxy with detected CO(6-5) emission and an expected CO(5-4) line that becomes more significant with directed priors.

quotes.quote: Including the known CO( 5 4 ) and CO( 6 5 ) emission lines associated with the SMG HDF850.1 from these calculations the expected number of detections _ s in the data will be 3-4 .

Identified as the CO( 6 5 ) transition for the SMG galaxy HDF850.1 at z=5.183 . The second expected emission line corresponding to the CO( 5 4 ) transition corresponds to candidate B8 in Table Table:MainResults but falls below the threshold for acceptance in the context of a blind survey. We shall show in section Section:Directed that when including additional prior information in the form of position and redshifts of known sources that this probability increases to be a definite detection in the data.
The second most significant line is associated with the CO( 6 5 ) transition line for the SMG HDF850.1.

sources.summary: The paper treats WFPC2 3-586 as one visible candidate counterpart to HDF850.1, identifying it with source 1247 but judging it an unlikely match because of its properties.

quotes.quote: VLA observations by dwn99 indicate that the galaxy corresponding to HDF850.1 is either WFPC2 3-586 or 3-593 if it is associated with an optically visible galaxy. It could also be an optically obscured galaxy lying between the two galaxies. Examination of the F160W image does not reveal an optically undetected galaxy lying between the sources, however, the images of the two galaxies slightly overlap at a level below the threshold set for positive detection in sec-dr .

WFPC2 3-586 corresponds to source 1247 in Table tab1 . It is an elliptical galaxy (Template 1.2) with no detected extinction at a redshift of 0.88. The luminosity is 1.5 10^ 10 L _ and it is an unlikely candidate for HDF850.1.

sources.summary: The paper treats HDF 850.1 as a difficult SCUBA counterpart case whose IRAM position favored a new faint radio source over the nearby VLA galaxy.

quotes.quote: This sub-mm source, usually called HDF 850.1, has been the subject of intense scrutiny since its discovery in the initial deep sub-mm survey of the HDF by 1998Natur.394..241H . After years of effort, 2004MNRAS.350..769D now claim that the counterpart for 850.1 has been determined. Using the IRAM interferometer, the sub-mm flux was resolved with a positional uncertainty of 0.3 \ 1999A&A...347..809D . However, it was not found coincident with the VLA source mentioned above, but rather a new and very faint radio source found by co-adding VLA and MERLIN data. It cannot be stressed how difficult obtaining this (tentative) counterpart has been.

sources.summary: The paper uses the HDF-N scan to identify CO emission from HDF 850.1, establishing z=5.183 and detecting multiple CO transitions from the SMG.

quotes.quote: In walter12 , we used the dataset presented here to search for CO emission associated with HDF 850.1, the brightest sub--millimeter galaxy in the HDF--N HDF--N, williams96, hughes98 . This search allowed us to unambiguously establish the redshift of this luminous galaxy z=5.183 , walter12 .

These are associated with the SMG HDF 850.1 and they are consistent with being CO(5-4) and CO(6-5) at z 5.18 walter12 . HDF 850.1 is the only source for which we detected more than one line in the molecular line scan.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3-593.1 as a neighbouring foreground galaxy whose modeled light and SED help separate foreground contamination from the HDF850.1 Spitzer/submillimetre emission.

quotes.quote: Galaxies 3-577.0 and 3-593.1 were additionally modelled, the latter incorporating a bending mode.

Furthermore, the centroid of this emission is clearly not consistent with flux solely from the neighbouring foreground galaxy 3-593.1. In section sec:seds we will model the SED of 3-593.1 to assess the likely contribution from this galaxy to the Spitzer flux.

The positions of all the galaxies are known, and given that the data is already inconsistent with flux at only 3-586.0 and 3-593.1, we tried a three-component fit to the 5.8 m and 8 m images, with point sources fixed at the locations of 3-586.0, 3-593.1 and the submm emission.

sources.summary: HDF850.1 is discussed as a well-studied GOODS-N SMG with difficult multiwavelength identification, faint optical/NIR emission, absent catalog coverage, and no false SCUBA-2-beam matches.

quotes.quote: For the other well-studied SMG, HDF850.1, multiwavelength identification has been a long battle , with the current consensus being that the source is extremely faint at optical and NIR wavelengths; indeed, our current GOODS-N catalogue does not contain HDF850.1. Nevertheless, we were able to confirm that no false SMGs were found within the SCUBA-2 beam surrounding HDF850.1. The GOODS-N field has also seen submm interferometric follow-up with with the SMA from cowie2017 .

sources.summary: The paper places HDF850.1, a well-known z=5.2 dusty star-forming galaxy, in the largest GOODS-N overdensity and notes a Balmer-break galaxy 0.6 arcmin from it.

quotes.quote: The powerful combination of deep imaging and wide field slitless spectroscopy (WFSS) provided by JWST/NIRCam have already revealed large-scale galaxy overdensities around the ultraluminous QSOs J0100+2802 at z = 6.3 Kashino:2023 and J0305–3150 at z = 6.6 Wang:2023 , the well-known dusty star-forming galaxy (DSFG) HDF850.1 at z = 5.2 Herard-Demanche:2023, Sun:2023b , and in a blank region of the sky near the Hubble Ultra Deep Field (HUDF) at z = 5.4 Helton:2023 .

This overdensity was discussed previously in Walter:2012 , where 13 spectroscopic sources were found to be associated with an overdensity containing the well-known sub-millimeter galaxy HDF850.1; then in Arrabal-Haro:2018 , where 55 photometric sources were found to be related to the same overdensity, although spectroscopic follow-up was needed to confirm the association of these candidates; then in Calvi:2021 , where an additional 10 newly confirmed members were found; and finally in Herard-Demanche:2023 and Sun:2023b , where 100 and 109 sources were found to be in the vicinity of HDF850.1, respectively (note that these two works adopted different survey areas and definitions for galaxy associations).

This galaxy is part of the largest overdensity identified here in terms of total number of galaxies and inferred halo mass (JADES GN OD 5.191 ), with an angular (physical) separation of 0.6 arcmin ( 1.5 cMpc) from the well-known sub-millimeter galaxy HDF850.1.

gmw_00000451.name: NGC 4958
gmw_00000451.overview: NGC 4958 is an edge-on barred lenticular galaxy located 13.2 Mpc away [arXiv:1904.03095]. Its nucleus is classified as a LINER-like active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1904.03095] that exhibits a double-peaked H-alpha emission line characteristic of a relativistic Keplerian accretion disk [arXiv:1904.03095, arXiv:2304.13899].

sources.summary: The paper reports a double-peaked nuclear H feature in NGC 4958, classifies its nucleus as LINER-like, and models the emission as a relativistic Keplerian disc.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we report the detection of a double-peaked feature in the H line in the nucleus of the galaxy NGC 4958. The narrow line region of this object has an emission that is typical of a LINER galaxy, which is the usual classification for double-peaked emitters. A central broad component, related to the broad line region (BLR) of this object, is seen in H and also in H . We concluded that the double-peaked emission is emitted by a circular relativistic Keplerian disc with an inner radius _ i = 570 83, an outer radius _ o = 860 170 (both in units of GM_ SMBH /^2 ), an inclination to the line of sight i = 27.2 0.7 and a local broadening parameter = 1310 70 km s ^ 1 .

NGC 4958 is an edge-on SB0 galaxy (see Fig. fig:ngc4958 ) with a magnitude m_ B = 11.6 1991trcb.book.....D , located at a distance of 13.2 Mpc 2007A&A...465...71T . 1989A&A...226...23B detected weak nuclear emission lines in this galaxy (mainly H , [ N II ] and [ S II ] ). A compact radio source is seen in the centre of NGC 4958 1998AJ....115.1693C , with a power of 1.9 10 ^ 20 W/Hz at 1.4 GHz 2011ApJ...731L..41B . Both the radio and optical emission are in accordance with a non-thermal emission from a LINER-like AGN 2008ARA&A..46..475H .

sources.summary: The paper uses prior nuclear line-emission results for NGC 4958 because its broad components resemble a relativistic accretion disk, and its ratios require fast-shock models.

quotes.quote: The fitting procedure for the emission lines of NGC 4958 was performed differently by 2019MNRAS.486.1138R , since this galaxy presents broad components that are typical of a relativistic accretion disc. We used their results concerning the nuclear line emission of NGC 4958 in the present work, but, for completeness, we also show the fitted profiles of this object in Fig. B1.

However, the / versus / diagram reveals that six objects (NGC 1411, NGC 1553, NGC 1052, NGC 4958, NGC 4546 and NGC 2217) have / ratios that require fast shock models. It is worth noticing that both n_e and line widths measurements for the NLR of the sample galaxies are in accordance with the shock model limits presented in Fig. fig:bpt_photoionization_models .

gmw_00000452.name: NGC 6090
gmw_00000452.overview: NGC 6090 is a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0411301, arXiv:1801.04328] in the intermediate stages of a major merger [arXiv:1605.05769, arXiv:2001.07807], featuring two closely interacting nuclei [arXiv:1605.05769, arXiv:1810.02371] and large tidal tails [arXiv:1605.05769]. This metal-rich starburst system [arXiv:1605.05769] hosts active star-forming regions along its spiral arms [arXiv:1811.09281] and exhibits a galactic wind outflow [arXiv:1811.09281].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 6090 as a metal-rich merging starburst galaxy, deriving its outflow distance, ionization, metallicity, and mass outflow rate from HST ultraviolet spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: NGC 6090 is a massive star forming galaxy at a distance of 128 Mpc (see tab:ngc6090 ), with no indication of AGN contamination from the optical emission lines (see tab:ngc6090 ) and the WISE colors. The galaxy is metal rich, with a log(O/H)+12 of 8.77 ismmetallicity . The galaxy is in the intermediate stages of a massive merger, as two distinct nuclei and large extend tidal tails are seen in the UV ( fig:image ) and optical images ostlin09 . The Cosmic Origins Spectrograph COS; cos on the Hubble Space Telescope observed NGC 6090 during Cycle 18 Project ID: 12173; claus2012 .

Here we measure a physically motivated mass outflow rate ( ) of the nearby starburst NGC 6090. To calculate , we first fit the optical depth with a Sobolev optical depth, and the covering fraction with a radial power-law (see eq:cfbeta and fig:beta ). We then calculate the ionization corrections and the metallicity of the outflow using a Bayesian analysis, CLOUDY models, and the measured column densities (see tab:ion and fig:ionpdfs ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6090 as efficiently heating dust, with SED and line-ratio models requiring multiple shock velocities and differing NE/SW starburst contributions.

quotes.quote: NGC 6090 The results of Acosta-Pulido et al (1996) show that 84 of the NGC 6090 emission comes from the wavelength regime shortward of 120 and argues for very efficient heating of the dust. It implies temperatures higher than about 25 K which cannot be accounted for by the interstellar radiation field alone. The models are compared with observed emission-line ratios (Veilleux et al. 1995) in Tables 9 and 10 for the SW and NE regions respectively.

With cross-checking considerations, the SED of the continuum which appears in Fig. 12 shows that model M17 (solid lines) with =500 and d/g=1.6 10^ 4 explains some significant data in the MIR from NED, in agreement with Keck data (S01) Reradiation by dust in clouds with \ = 200 \ and d/g = 2.8 10^ 4 (M18) explains the IR data (dash-dot lines) and bremsstrahlung. The fit of the summed optical line ratios shows that other models contribute.

Clouds photoionized by a t=3.3 Myr starburst and with \ = 500, 200, and 100 \ explain the summed line ratios in the NE region, while the low velocity cloud contribution in region SW corresponds to a younger age ( t=2.5 Myr) and appears in the sum with a high relative weight (model M19).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6090 as the closest local spectral match to the K20 composite, describing it as a luminous merging infrared galaxy with star clusters and similar UV absorption lines.

quotes.quote: We have compared the K20 average spectrum with the local starburst galaxies NGC 1705, NGC 1741, NGC 4214, and NGC 6090. The best match is obtained with NGC 6090 which is an interacting system at v 9062 km/s in the process of merging. It is a luminous infrared galaxy (log L _ IR = 11.51; Scoville et al. 2000) with a number of luminous clusters triggered by the galaxy-galaxy interaction.

NICMOS/HST images of NGC 6090 (Scoville et al. 2000) show the inner site of the interaction in more detail, where a less massive galaxy seems to be merging with a disk. The spectrum of NGC 6090 taken by the HUT during the Astro-2 mission (Gonzalez Delgado et al. 1998) is shown in Fig. f2 . It has several absorption lines which are similar in strength to the K20 composite spectrum, such as the photospheric lines SiIII 1295 , CIII 1430 \ and SiII 1485 \ and a marginally weaker FeV 1380 \ line.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 6090 as an interacting system with Ly emission from both components, dusty overlap regions, and mismatched Ly and H emission structures.

quotes.quote: NGC 6090  -- Third column in Fig. images_1 shows that the interacting system NGC 6090 exhibits Ly \ emission from each component, at a distance of about 6 \ from each other. The emission is peaking around low extinction regions, and the overlapping region between the two components, where no emission is seen appears very dusty. The extinction map of NGC 6090 shows dust pattern like a spiral structure and no evident correlation with the ionised gas traced by the H \ emission is seen. The main H \ structures correspond to the Ly \ emission components, although the largest Ly \ emission represents a small region in H , and vice-versa. This discrepancy may be due the large amount of dust in the upper left component that could destroy a part of Ly \ photons.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6090 as a large local star-forming control galaxy with stronger, broader silicon absorption and a modeled outflow line profile.

quotes.quote: In the bottom right panel of Fig. fig:siprofiles , we include the 1260 \ and 1206 \ profiles from NGC 6090, a large local star-forming galaxy from the control sample. The and lines from NGC 6090 are stronger and broader ( and equivalent widths of 1.9 and 2.6 , respectively) than the profiles from the LyC leakers.

Chisholm16b find the line profile from NGC 6090 corresponds to a law with = 0.4 . This line profile is plotted as the black curve in the upper panel of Fig. fig:rat_comp . The law from NGC 6090 produces a / ratio of 2.

sources.summary: NGC 6090 is an unresolved close interaction whose north-eastern source has hard-band diffuse emission, a bright arm region, and overlapping radial profiles from the two nuclei.

quotes.quote: [ 75 ] NGC 6090 : This closely interacting system is completely unresolved in both Herschel and MIPS data. Therefore, we resort to the analysis performed by HatYos2004 to derive the contribution of each component to the IRAS flux, listed in Table table:IRfrac . The north-eastern source shows hard-band diffuse emission corresponding to the optical central region of the galaxy, and a peak 3 '' north of the centre. It corresponds to a particularly bright region, both in optical and infrared, on one of the spiral arms. The sources are interacting so closely that the radial profiles interfere with each other past 4-5 '' from each nucleus, and have therefore been limited to this radius.

sources.summary: NGC 6090 has a companion nucleus and spiral-arm star formation; despite a previously detected galactic outflow, the near-infrared OSIRIS data provide no evidence for shocked gas in the nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 6090 NGC 6090 has a companion nucleus 4 kpc away . The main nuclus that we targeted with OSIRIS features large-scale spiral arms traced primarily by flux as star formation sites. have detected a galactic outflow with mass outflow rate of 2.3 M _ yr ^ 1 using the HST Cosmic Origins Spectrograph. As noted in Paper I, extracting kinematic information for the gas has proven to be challenging given the relative lack of gas in the near-infrared range. The / line ratio map also provides no evidence for the presence of shocked gas in this nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper calls NGC 6090 a complex galaxy merger, says its observed northeast radio component has diffuse 8.44 GHz emission and steep values suggesting a radio AGN, despite starburst mid-infrared classification.

quotes.quote: NGC 6090 This is a beautiful galaxy merger that displays a complex double radio structure at 1.49 GHz. Only the NE radio component has been observed at 8.44 GHz, and shows diffuse radio emission. The map gives a distribution of steep values suggesting a radio-AGN. In the mid-IR it is classified as a SB, and as a Seyfert based on the BPT diagram.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6090 as a major merger whose NE and SW components both show Ly-alpha and X-ray emission, with unresolved nuclear X-ray emission in NGC 6090-SW.

quotes.quote: Also, Haro 11 has two strong ULXs, while only one is a strong LAE prestwich15 ; and NGC 6090 is a major merger with \ and X-ray emission from both the NE and SW components. The component sources in these mergers are listed separately in Table chartable .
For NGC 6090-NE, LARS 03, LARS 08, and LARS 10, the \ and X-ray emission are spatially consistent with a physical association but their morphologies do not correlate strongly.

While XMM data is spatially unresolved, we note that all but one of the 8 galaxies observed with Chandra show nuclear X-ray emission, and in Haro 11-C, NGC 6090-SW, LARS 01, and LARS 12 these nuclear sources are unresolved.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 6090 as a LIRG whose steep 4.5-8.0 micron SED implies an AGN, with a relatively low but literature-consistent AGN fraction.

quotes.quote: NGC 6090 : NGC 6090 is a LIRG. The steep 4.5--8.0 \ SED implies an AGN is present, but the estimated AGN luminosity fraction is 26 , one of the lowest in the late-stage merger sample. This is still higher than the 10 value measured by dal14 .

gmw_00000453.name: CGCG97-125
gmw_00000453.overview: CGCG97-125 is an early-type S0a galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0603826] that exhibits stellar shells [arXiv:astro-ph/0603826], a broad neutral hydrogen tail [arXiv:1801.03601], and highly disturbed stellar and gas kinematics [arXiv:1901.03334, arXiv:2202.03443]. These features, along with strong emission lines and a starburst, indicate a past galaxy merger [arXiv:astro-ph/0603826, arXiv:2202.03443].

sources.summary: CGCG97-125 is presented as BIG's brightest S0a member, with shells, disturbed kinematics, strong emission lines, and a starburst likely tied to a merger event.

quotes.quote: The brightest group member: CGCG97-125 CGCG97-125 is classified as S0a goldmine , consistent with its red B-R color index ( 1.34 mag) and with the shape of the continuum optical spectrum (Fig. spec125 ). However, this system is far from being a normal early type galaxy. The presence of stellar shells around CGCG97-125 (see Fig. R ) clearly indicates a past interaction/merger event.

Velocity plots of CGCG97-125 were extracted from each spectrum by measuring the wavelength of the H line in each pixel along the slits. The three rotation curves so obtained are given in Fig. rotslit . In each diagram the recessional velocity is plotted as a function of position along the slit (the spatial axis runs from E (left) to W (right)). All three spectra show multiple velocity components near the galaxy center (C) where sudden velocity jumps of 100-150 km s^ 1 are detected.

Moreover, CGCG97-125 shows strong emission lines (Fig. spec125 ) which are never seen in galaxies earlier than Sc (see e.g. gav02 ), indicating that this galaxy is still experiencing a strong burst of star formation, likely produced by the merging event -- a kind of rejuvenated early type galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 125 as a post-merger BIG member with disturbed gas and stellar kinematics, shock-like line ratios, and obscured nuclear X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: Their data revealed that 125 is the result of a galaxy merger that could have produced the ionized gas filaments. First, the gas associated with 125 does not show ordered rotation. Two streams of gas with velocities separated by 250 \ can be observed over the stellar continuum of this object. The dwarf star forming objects DW1, DW2, and DW3 exhibit velocities similar to 125 and therefore can have been originated from tidal forces caused by the merging systems from which 125 originated.

On the other hand, the latter exhibits a complex stellar kinematic map. The peaks of the rotation curve are roughly aligned N-S, but elsewhere the iso-velocity contours are mostly aligned E-W . This shape further supports the evidence of a recent merging event occurred for this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes 125 as a highly perturbed BIG member with an offset HI maximum and a broad curving HI tail that appears connected by a tidal bridge toward 114.

quotes.quote: Figure big shows the \ in the easternmost group member in the figure ( 125) is highly perturbed with its \ column density maximum offset 14.4 2.9 arcsec (6 kpc) E of the optical centre. Strikingly 125 has a broad 80 arcsec (33 kpc) black long \ tail curving black to the SE. This tail contains a large \ mass (K2 in the Cortese et al. 2006 figure), near the position of a bright foreground star. The Cortese figure shows the eastern \ tail black 125 has an \ counterpart which extends beyond the \ tail to the eastern side of the blue LTG, 114.

sources.summary: The paper discusses CGCG097-125 as a massive BIG galaxy whose disturbed stellar and gas kinematics likely reflect a past merger rather than the RPS analysis focus.

quotes.quote: The BIG is mainly composed of two galaxies CGCG097-125 and CGCG097-114 with mass of (M_*/ M_ ) 10.32 , (M_*/ M_ ) 9.22 respectively. A third galaxy CGCG097-120 ( (M_*/ M_ ) 10.53 ) is unlikely to be part of the group due to its offset in recessional velocity compared to the other galaxies and filaments in BIG Fossati19 . Furthermore, we find lower mass star-forming systems connected by ionised gas filaments, which have originated from strong tides during the merger of the galaxies that now compose CGCG097-125.
During our analysis in Section sec:emlines , we thus exclude CGCG097-125 as its highly perturbed stellar and gaseous kinematics is likely to arise more from a past merger.

The presence of a companion is usually a clear indication of gravitational interactions, however in the case of CGCG097-125 a companion might not be obviously found as the galaxy appears to be a post-merger remnant.

gmw_00000454.name: CGCG97-120
gmw_00000454.overview: CGCG97-120 is a massive galaxy in the Abell 1367 cluster that likely hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0202431, arXiv:1901.03334]. It is undergoing face-on ram pressure stripping, which has caused a truncated gaseous disk, high neutral hydrogen deficiency, and suppressed star formation [arXiv:1901.03334, arXiv:2202.03443].

sources.summary: The paper argues 120 is a superimposed A1367 satellite, not part of BIG, while showing face-on ram-pressure stripping, disk truncation, and gradual outer-disk quenching.

quotes.quote: Combining spatially resolved kinematics and line ratios, we rule out the association of the most massive galaxy, 120, with the group as this system appears to be decoupled from the intragroup medium and subject to strong ram pressure as it falls into A1367. It is immediately clear from Figure Halpha_map that the ionized gas extends only 5.5 kpc from the galaxy nucleus along the major axis. The stellar disk, instead, is at least twice as extended. This abrupt truncation of the gaseous disk is one of the strongest indications of ram pressure stripping, and typically occurs in all the gas phases (atomic, molecular, ionized) and in the dust distribution Koopmann01, Boselli06, Fumagalli08, Fumagalli09, Cortese10, Fossati13, Boselli14a .

Based on this analysis we find that 120 is undergoing a ram-pressure stripping event that has caused a truncation of the gaseous disk and the suppression of star formation at >5 kpc, finding instead no perturbation on the stellar disk kinematics. Based on new data we conclude that the massive galaxy 120 is not part of BIG but is simply superimposed to the group in the dense cluster environment. Instead, 120 gave us one of the best views of face-on ram pressure stripping in a cluster.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 3860 as having both a central point source and diffuse extension, interpreting the nucleus as a likely low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 3860 This \ observation clearly reveals two X-ray components in NGC 3860, a bright central point source and a diffuse extension. The latter has been discussed in paper I. It may be the thermal halo of NGC 3860 with a temperature of 0.7 keV. The position of the bright X-ray point-like source agrees with the optical nucleus of NGC 3860.

No significant variations in its lightcurve were found. The weak H emission observed by Kennicutt, Bothun Schommer (1984) rules out the possibility of a starburst nucleus. Thus, it is most likely a LLAGN (e.g., Ho 1999).

sources.summary: The paper discusses CGCG97-120 as a lower-velocity galaxy apparently encircled by an H-alpha loop, but argues the association with BIG's ionized trails may be coincidental.

quotes.quote: The loop around CGCG97-120 alone contributes 2.4 10^ 14  erg  cm^ 2  s^ 1 , obtained integrating the H + [ NII ] emission in a circular corona of 10 kpc radius and an annulus of 5 kpc centered on CGCG97-120. The derived line intensity is 2.05 Rayleigh (1 Rayleigh = 10^ 6 /4  photons  cm^ 2  s^ 1  sr^ 1 ), corresponding to an emission measure (EM) of 5.7 cm^ 6  pc . Assuming a torus geometry with a circular section of radius 5 kpc and a filling factor of 1, the resultant plasma density is n_ e 3.3 10^ 2 cm^ 3 and the ionized column density is N_ e 5 10^ 20   cm^ 2 (the inferred densities would be higher if the gas is in clumps or filaments, which is likely).

One of the H trails apparently traces a perfect loop around CGCG97-120 (see Fig. net ). Yet this galaxy seems not to be associated with BIG, having a recessional velocity of 5635 km s^ 1 , thus blue-shifted with respect to A1367 by approximately 800 km s^ 1 . Is this a pure coincidence? The galaxy's morphology and kinematics are completely unperturbed, showing no signs of interaction (see Fig. R and Fig. rot120 ).

sources.summary: The paper presents CGCG097-120 as a massive face-on ram-pressure-stripping galaxy, offset from BIG, with truncated gas, high line ratios, HI deficiency, and shocks.

quotes.quote: A third galaxy CGCG097-120 ( (M_*/ M_ ) 10.53 ) is unlikely to be part of the group due to its offset in recessional velocity compared to the other galaxies and filaments in BIG Fossati19 .
Both CGCG097-120 and CGCG097-114 are galaxies suffering RPS. CGCG097-120, despite not being part of the group, gives us one of the best known example of face-on RPS in the cluster potential.

Conversely, CGCG097-120 and ESO137-001 show a significant truncation of the disk, with values of R_ H /R_ band 0.36 and 0.27 , respectively. We thus conclude that the two galaxies with high [ OIII ] / H appear to be undergoing a starburst at an early phase of the stripping process when they are still rich in cold/warm gas, while CGCG097-120 and ESO137-001 are already at a later stripping stage -- as shown by the significant truncation -- and their activity in the interface region is characterized by low levels of star formation and a more prominent contribution from shocks. Moreover, as shown in Table tab:sample , CGCG097-120 exhibits the highest HI-deficiency of our sample indicating a significant lack of cold HI gas in its disk.

gmw_00000455.name: CGCG97-114
gmw_00000455.overview: CGCG97-114 is a blue, late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1801.03601, arXiv:astro-ph/0603826] that is a member of the Blue Infalling Group [arXiv:1801.03601, arXiv:2202.03443]. It is undergoing ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:1901.03334, arXiv:2202.03443], which has produced a downstream tail of gas and stripped HII regions [arXiv:1901.03334] without truncating its extended gaseous disk [arXiv:2202.03443].

sources.summary: The paper describes 114 as a lower-mass BIG galaxy undergoing ram-pressure stripping, with high-velocity ionized gas, stripped debris, and possible gravitational influence from 125.

quotes.quote: While 125 and 114 have a similar recessional velocity, consistent with that of the group, 120 has a much lower velocity ( v 5620 ), which questions its membership within the group. The gas ionized in and around 114 shows the highest velocities, which are also found in stripped debris in the North side of this galaxy (at coordinates 25, -5 kpc in Figure vel_map ) . Lastly, 114 shows some form of ordered rotation. Due to its lower stellar mass, the dynamic range of the rotation curve is only 50 , but we find no sign of dynamical perturbations on the stellar disk based on current observations.

The role of 114 remains unclear, despite the wealth of data provided by the MUSE observations. There is no doubt that the galaxy is undergoing ram pressure stripping, possibly by the hot halo of A1367. This is supported by the tail downstream of the stellar disk, and the presence of stripped HII regions in the N direction, exhibiting extended filaments pointing in the same direction as the main tail behind the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes 114 as a blue late-type galaxy in BIG with an eastern HI clump, strong H-alpha emission, and velocity evidence linking it to the 125 tidal bridge.

quotes.quote: The Cortese figure shows the eastern \ tail black 125 has an \ counterpart which extends beyond the \ tail to the eastern side of the blue LTG, 114. The \ velocity field shows the \ velocity falling along the \ tail from 8277 18 \ at the 125 column density maximum to black 8100 \ at K2 ( black V_ opt 8075 \ at K2a). An \ clump is detected 10.4 arcsec (4 kpc) E of the 114 optical centre with a black V_ HI = 8451 20 . 114 emits strongly in \ and has black V_ opt = 8425 . Our \ velocities for 114, 125 and K2 are in good agreement with those from the WSRT sakai02 .

sources.summary: The paper describes CGCG097-114 as a BIG member undergoing ram-pressure stripping, with shock-like line ratios, extended gas, rich HI content, and an intermediate stripping stage.

quotes.quote: The BIG is mainly composed of two galaxies CGCG097-125 and CGCG097-114 with mass of (M_*/ M_ ) 10.32 , (M_*/ M_ ) 9.22 respectively. A third galaxy CGCG097-120 ( (M_*/ M_ ) 10.53 ) is unlikely to be part of the group due to its offset in recessional velocity compared to the other galaxies and filaments in BIG Fossati19 . Furthermore, we find lower mass star-forming systems connected by ionised gas filaments, which have originated from strong tides during the merger of the galaxies that now compose CGCG097-125. Both CGCG097-120 and CGCG097-114 are galaxies suffering RPS.
An intermediate phase is shown by CGCG097-114, which does not show a starburst while still having an extended gaseous disk and a rich HI-content.

A disk truncation is not observed in CGCG097-114, even if it shows shock-like emission-line properties, making this object possibly in an intermediate stage between the gas compression phase and the truncation and quenching phase.

sources.summary: CGCG97-114 is treated as a bright late-type spiral member of BIG, with Balmer absorption, HI content, normal metallicity for its luminosity, and association with emitting-line objects.

quotes.quote: The two bright galaxies CGCG97-125 and CGCG97-114 show evidence for underlying Balmer absorption.
The two brightest galaxies in the group (CGCG97-114 and CGCG97-125) have a normal metal content for their luminosity.

Originally BIG was a normal compact group of galaxies with a typical velocity dispersion of 150-200 km s^ 1 , composed of at least three galaxies: a massive evolved early type galaxy (CGCG97-125), a less massive late type spiral (CGCG97-114) and a gas rich dwarf galaxy (the satellite presently merged with CGCG97-125) with a stellar mass 10^ 9 M_ .

gmw_00000456.name: Canes Venatici I
aliases_external: Canes Venatici Dwarf / CVn I dSph
Canes Venatici I dwarf spheroidal galaxy
Canes Venatici I dSph / Canes Venatici Dwarf
gmw_00000456.overview: Canes Venatici I is a distant, diffuse dwarf spheroidal galaxy and a satellite of the Milky Way [arXiv:astro-ph/0604354, arXiv:1911.10185]. It is dominated by an old, metal-poor stellar population [arXiv:0709.3365, arXiv:1911.10185], but it also hosts a younger, more metal-rich population [arXiv:0709.3365, arXiv:0705.4622] and numerous RR Lyrae variable stars [arXiv:0709.3281, arXiv:1802.06811].

sources.summary: The paper finds CVnI has dominant old stars plus a compact, offset younger metal-richer population, implying distinct unmixed components and a complex star formation history.

quotes.quote: Although the Canes Venatici I dwarf galaxy (CVnI; _0=13^ h 28^ m 03.5^ s , _0=33 33'21.0'' ) is not much less luminous than previously known satellites of the Milky Way ( M_V=-7.9 0.5 ), its large distance ( 224 21 ) kept it hidden until it was recently discovered by zucker06a in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS). Unlike most other Galactic satellites at more than 200 from the MW, CVnI seems dominated by old stellar populations. However, applying an automated color-magnitude diagram (CMD) fitting technique to SDSS data, dejong07 show tentative evidence for the presence of a much younger population of only 3 Gyr. A spectroscopic survey of CVnI stars ibata06,martin07a also revealed the presence of two kinematically distinct populations.

From deep B- and V-band photometry of the Canes Venatici I galaxy reaching to the old main sequence turnoff, we found evidence for: A (known) dominant old population that represents 95 percent of the mass of the galaxy, with an age 10 Gyr and 2.0 ; A new, much younger population revealed by a blue plume that corresponds to 1.4-2.0 Gyr stars that are also slightly more metal-rich ( 1.5 ). We show that the old population is extended and mainly shapes the structure of CVnI. In contrast the young population is very compact as well as offset from the dwarf center by 1.0_ 0.3 ^ +0.6 arcmin or 64_ 20 ^ +40 . This suggests that it is not yet dynamically relaxed although this is not surprising given that the typical relaxation time for a dwarf galaxy is higher than a Hubble time (e.g. gilmore07 ).

sources.summary: The paper models Canes Venatici I as the sample's most massive, extended dwarf, requiring higher star-formation efficiency, higher infall mass, and a longer star-formation history.

quotes.quote: We test the integrated galactic initial mass function (IGIMF) on the chemical evolution of 16 ultra-faint dwarf (UFD) galaxies discussing in detail the results obtained for three of them: Bo otes I, Bo otes II and Canes Venatici I, taken as prototypes of the smallest and the largest UFDs. These objects have very small stellar masses ( 10^3-10^4 M_ ) and quite low metallicities ( [ Fe/H ] <-1.0 dex). We consider four observational constraints: the present-day stellar mass, the [ /Fe ] vs. [ Fe/H ] relation, the stellar metallicity distribution function and the cumulative star formation history.

Canes Venatici I is the most massive and most extended galaxy in our sample, and it is sometimes considered more as a dSph rather than an UFD galaxy. The derived mass of its dark matter halo is M_ DM =1.9 10^7 \ M_ collins2014 , with a half-light radius of r_L=564 pc martin2008 .

In order to reproduce the observed present-day stellar mass of CVn I we need to assume a higher SFE and a higher infall mass with respect to the other UFDs. For this galaxy the models with a short infall timescale, typically associated to UFDs, do not reproduce the cumulative SFH derived from observations. In order to fit the SFH we have then increased _ infall .

sources.summary: The paper models Canes Venatici I as a possibly dark-matter-free, tidally disrupting dwarf spheroidal, deriving an orbit and progenitor that reproduce its observed mass, size, brightness, and velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: Located near the North Galactic Pole at a distance of 224 kpc from the Earth zucker_2006 , the Canes Venatici I dwarf spheroidal galaxy (CVn I dSph, _0 =13^h28^m03.5^s, _0 = 33^ 33'21.0'' ) was, at the moment of its discovery, one of the most remote satellites to the MW zucker_2006 . Being a relatively luminous companion to the MW ( M_v = -7.9 0.5 mag ), its large distance allowed it to remain undetected until 2006. With a half-light radius of 564 pc zucker_2006 , it is one of the largest satellites of the MW mcconnachie_2012,zucker_2006 . It possesses an old ( 12 Gyr, ), metal-poor ( [ Fe/H ] 2 ( , ) population of stars that represents 95 of the luminous mass of the galaxy, and a younger ( 1.4-2.0 Gyr) more metal-rich ( [ Fe/H ] 1.5 , ) population of stars, revealed by a blue plume in the galaxy's colour-magnitude diagramme martin_2008 , accounting for the remaining 5 of the stars.

We were able to find a pair of initial parameters of a Plummer sphere that reproduces the current observational properties of CVn I dSph (Tab. tab:test ). With a initial mass of 2.47 10^7 M _ and Plummer radius of 653 pc, we obtain a remnant with a final mass of 2.45 10^5 M _ , central surface brightness of 26.9 mag arcsec ^ 2 , an effective radius of 545.7 pc, and a velocity dispersion with the value 7.58 km s ^ 1 , properties that matches the observational parameters within the measurement errors. To achieve this, first we had to find an orbit for this dwarf galaxy. Under the assumption that Canes Venatici I is a DM-free object, its elongation, as observed by okamoto_2012 , would be a consequence of the tidal disruption that this galaxy is suffering under the influence of the MW's gravitational potential and, therefore, this dwarf's major axis would be aligned with it's orbital path.

sources.summary: The paper presents Keck spectroscopy of Canes Venatici, finding distinct metal-poor and metal-rich stellar components with different sizes and velocity dispersions, relevant to its dark matter distribution.

quotes.quote: We present a spectroscopic survey of the newly-discovered Canes Venatici dwarf galaxy using the Keck/DEIMOS spectrograph. Two stellar populations of distinct kinematics are found to be present in this galaxy: an extended, metal-poor component, of half-light radius 7 8^ +2.4 _ 2.1 , which has a velocity dispersion of 13.9^ +3.2 _ 2.5 , and a more concentrated (half-light radius 3 6^ +1.1 _ 0.8 ) metal-rich component of extremely low velocity dispersion. At 99 confidence, the upper limit to the central velocity dispersion of the metal-rich population is 1.9 . This is the lowest velocity dispersion ever measured in a galaxy. We perform a Jeans analysis on the two components, and find that the dynamics of the structures can only be consistent if we adopt extreme (and unlikely) values for the scale length and velocity dispersion of the metal-poor population.

We have uncovered two stellar components in the Canes Venatici dwarf spheroidal galaxy with distinct structure and kinematics. Below a metallicity of Fe/H = -2 this galaxy has a velocity dispersion of 10 , typical of other dSph galaxies. At metallicities richer than this limit, an intriguing additional component is detected, possessing a velocity dispersion that is barely resolved with our measurement accuracy, and has an upper limit of 1.9 .

sources.summary: The paper says Canes Venatici I has the most nearly normal MDF, little need for the Extra Gas model, and the strongest preference for Pre-Enriched modeling.

quotes.quote: Canes Venatici I The MDF of Canes Venatici I resembles a normal distribution more than any of the other seven dwarfs. Although it has a slight metal-poor tail, it is nearly symmetric. Canes Venatici I shows the least preference for the Extra Gas Model of all the dSphs in Fig. fig:fehhists except for Sculptor. The most likely extra gas parameter is M = _ ^ + , and the preference of the Extra Gas Model over the Pristine Model is slight ( L_ max ( Extra Gas )/L_ max ( Pristine ) = ). Overall, the MDF of Canes Venatici I fits a Leaky Box Model best of all the dSphs shown here.

It is the only dSph in our sample that fits the Pre-Enriched Model significantly better than the Extra Gas Model ( L_ max ( Pre Enriched )/L_ max ( Extra Gas ) = ). The dSph's large distance 210 kpc, kue08 would make it less susceptible to gas accretion from the MW than the other dSphs in its luminosity class, Draco and Ursa Minor.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Canes Venatici I as a dwarf spheroidal galaxy with RR Lyrae variables, an Oosterhoff-intermediate classification, a 210 kpc distance, and mainly old but possibly complex stellar populations.

quotes.quote: We have identified 23 RR Lyrae stars and 3 possible Anomalous Cepheids among 84 candidate variables in the recently discovered Canes Venatici I dwarf spheroidal galaxy. The mean period of 18 RRab type stars is P_ ab = 0.60 0.01 days. This period, and the location of these stars in the period-amplitude diagram, suggest that Canes Venatici I is likely an Oosterhoff-intermediate system. The average apparent magnitude of the RR Lyrae stars, V = 22.17 0.02 mag, is used to obtain a precision distance estimate of 210 ^ +7 _ 5 kpc, for an adopted reddening E(B-V) =0.03 mag.

We present a B,V color-magnitude diagram of Canes Venatici I that reaches V 25 mag, and shows that the galaxy has a mainly old stellar population with a metal abundance near [ Fe/H ] = 2.0 dex. The width of the red giant branch and the location of the candidate Anomalous Cepheids on the color-magnitude diagram may indicate that the galaxy hosts a complex stellar population with stars from 13 Gyr to as young as 0.6 Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper says Canes Venatici I is sparsely sampled but closely matched by the model, with a symmetric MDF, declining alpha trend, and an old dominant population with possible younger stars.

quotes.quote: Of all of our dSph models, that for Canes Venatici I adheres most closely to the observed abundance distributions, in part because of the sparse sampling. The MDF is a perfect match, and the predicted [ /Fe ] line passes through the observed locus of points, except for veering to slightly high [ /Fe ] values at high [ Fe/H ] . Unfortunately, only three stars pass the [ Mg/Fe ] uncertainty cut of 0.3 dex.

Because Canes Venatici I was discovered recently zuc06 , few photometric studies exist. martin08b found that the dSph contains mostly stars older than 10 Gyr, but 5 of the stars could be as young as 1.4 Gyr. Because the young population is much smaller than the old population, our chemical evolution model and its SF duration should be viewed as applicable to the dominant old population.

sources.summary: The paper discusses CVn I as a distant dwarf spheroidal hosting SDSS J1327+3335, whose Group III CEMP-no status may trace an extremely metal-poor population and unusual star-formation history.

quotes.quote: CVn I is a distant, faint dwarf galaxy, with a heliocentric distance of 220 kpc zucker2006 . Its absolute magnitude, M _ V 7.9 , makes it a galaxy similar to the Draco and Ursa Minor dwarf spheroidals, but its half-light radius, 550 pc, is larger than the others. Therefore, various studies have noted that CVn I is likely a dSph, rather than a UFD galaxy e.g., simon2019 .

Thus, the Group III CEMP-no status of SDSS J1327+3335 is intriguing, and may indicate that CVn I might have had an unusual star-formation history compared to other dSphs. Indeed, based on both Keck/DEIMOS spectroscopic and deep LBT photometric observations ibata2006, martin2007,kuehn2008, martin2008 , CVn I has been claimed to host two distinct stellar populations -- an extended metal-poor population ( 2.5 < [ Fe/H ] < -2.0 ) with hot kinematics and a more metal-rich population ( 2.0 < [ Fe/H ] <-1.5 ) with a near-zero velocity dispersion, concentrated on its center, although this dichotomy has been challenged by a kinematic study using more than 200 stars simon2007 . Perhaps SDSS J1327+3335 is a member of the extremely metal-poor population in CVn I, which might had been accreted into its halo.

sources.summary: The paper announces Canes Venatici as a newly discovered Milky Way dwarf spheroidal, estimating its 220 kpc distance, low luminosity, diffuse structure, and distorted outer regions.

quotes.quote: Based on its apparent morphology and on the presence of a distinct stellar population, we conclude that this is most likely a hitherto unknown dSph galaxy. As is customary, we name it after its constellation, Canes Venatici (CVn). The distance of a distinct, metal-poor stellar population can be estimated from the I band magnitude of the tip of the red giant branch TRGB; e.g., moul83,Lee93 .

At the distance of CVn, the approximately 8 5 half-light radius corresponds to 550 pc. The overall extent is 2 kpc along the major axis, making CVn one of the largest of the Milky Way dSphs. Although Plummer and exponential laws provide reasonable fits to the data, there are some discrepant datapoints in Figure fig:cvn_prof cf. Sextans' profile in Ir95 .

sources.summary: The paper presents Canes Venatici I as a distant, bright faint dwarf galaxy with DEIMOS data and two distinct stellar populations, including an extreme cold central component.

quotes.quote: Discovered in the SDSS by zucker06b , Canes Venatici I is the furthest of the newly discovered Milky Way faint dwarf galaxies with a heliocentric distance of 220 . It is also the brightest, with M_V=-7.9 0.5 , making it a galaxy similar to the Draco dSph, but with a bigger physical size (half-light radius r_h 550 ). The populated red giant branch of this satellite (see Figure 2 b ) makes it a perfect target for DEIMOS and we were able to observe 127 stars with two pointings. An analysis of this dwarf galaxy is presented in details in ibata06 and reveals that CVnI hosts two distinct stellar populations: one is metal-poor ( 2.5 2.0 ), kinematically hot ( =13.9_ 2.5 ^ +3.2 ) and extended ( r_ hb =7 8_ 2.1 ^ +2.4 ) while the second one is concentrated at the center of the dwarf ( r_ hb =3 6_ 0.8 ^ +1.1 ), is more metal-rich ( 2.0 1.5 ) and has a near-zero velocity dispersion (with a dispersion lower than 1.9 at the 99 confidence level). Though there is growing evidence that many dwarf galaxies harbor kinematically and structurally distinct populations or at least population gradients (see e.g. harbeck01 ; tolstoy04 for Sculptor; battaglia06 for Fornax), CVnI is to date the most extreme case of such a behaviour.

sources.summary: The paper includes Canes Venatici I among bright Milky Way dwarf spheroidals used to test SIDM subhaloes, finding its large half-light radius inconsistent with the 15 most massive simulated subhaloes.

quotes.quote: The dSphs sample consists of the 9 galaxies used in Boylan2011b : Fornax, Leo I, Sculptor, Leo II, Sextans, Carina, Ursa Minor, Canes Venatici I and Draco, selected with the criterion L_V>10^5 M _ .

Looking at Figure fig:sub_vcirc we see that at least three of the dSphs (Fornax, Sextans and Canes Venatici I, that have a value of r_ 1/2 1 kpc) are inconsistent with the circular velocity profile of the 15 most massive subhaloes in all our SIDM simulations, particularly for the cases with a velocity-dependent cross section. This however, does not mean that there are no subhaloes in these simulations that are a good match to these galaxies. These subhaloes exist but are considerably less massive than the ones we show in Figure fig:sub_vcirc and are more affected by the limited resolution of our simulations.

sources.summary: The paper treats Canes Venatici I as a studied ultra-faint dwarf, describing its discovery, color-magnitude work, distance estimates, and stellar abundance measurements.

quotes.quote: This paper reports on the detailed abundance determination of stars belonging to the UfDSphs Bootes II, Canes Venatici I, Canes Venatici II, Hercules and Leo IV. Canes Venatici I Canes Venatici I was discovered in 2006 by zucker2006 as a stellar over density in the north Galactic cap using the Sloan Digital Sky Survey Data Release 5. The first deep color-magnitude diagrams of the Canes Venatici I (CVn I) dwarf galaxy were provided by from observations with the wide-field Large Binocular Camera on the Large Binocular Telescope. However, the claim of a young population in Canes Venatici I has not been supported by more recent studies ural2010, okamoto2012 . derived a distance modulus of (m - M)_0 = 21.69 0.10 or D= 218 10 kpc . okamoto2012 confirmed the distance modulus using deep images taken with the Subaru/Suprime-Cam imager obtaining a distance modulus of (m - M)_0 = 21.68 0.08 (216 8 kpc) . kirby2010 determine the abundances of Fe and several elements in a sample of 171 stars using medium resolution spectra (R 7000) obtained with Keck/DEIMOS and found metallicities ranging from 1.0 dex to 3.3 dex.

sources.summary: The paper revises the Canes Venatici I spectroscopic membership sample, removes five RR Lyrae with variable velocities, and says the removals have little impact because the affected stars have large errors.

quotes.quote: We have made slight membership changes to the Simon2007ApJ...670..313S data for the following dSphs: Canes Venatici I, Coma Berenices, Leo IV, Ursa Major I, and Ursa Major II. We have removed RR Lyrae from the following (identified after the original spectroscopic analysis): Canes Venatici I 5 RR Lyrae identified in Kuehn2008ApJ...674L..81K , Coma Berenices 1 star; Musella2009ApJ...695L..83M , Leo IV 1 star; Moretti2009ApJ...699L.125M , and Ursa Major I 3 stars; Garofalo2013ApJ...767...62G . RR Lyrae are pulsating stars with variable velocity; these stars are dSph members, however, without additional phase information we cannot determine the star's bulk velocity. In Ursa Major II, a foreground dwarf has been removed that was identified in follow-up high-resolution spectroscopy analysis Frebel2010ApJ...708..560F . These removals do not have a large impact as the removed stars tend to have large error bars.

sources.summary: The paper studies Canes Venatici I stellar velocities, finding no clear kinematically distinct subpopulations and deriving a mass estimate consistent with prior data.

quotes.quote: We present new spectroscopic data for twenty six stars in the recently-discovered Canes Venatici I (CVnI) dwarf spheroidal galaxy. We use these data to investigate the recent claim of the presence of two dynamically inconsistent stellar populations in this system  Ibata2006 . We do not find evidence for kinematically distinct populations in our sample and we are able to obtain a mass estimate for CVnI that is consistent with all available data, including previously published data.

sources.summary: The paper uses Martin and Simon catalogs for Canes Venatici I, prioritizes Martin classifications, and regards the 57-star Gaia DR2 motion as robust.

quotes.quote: Canes Venatici I We use the catalogs of Martin_07 and Simon_07 and the binary probabilities there-in. We give the classifications of Martin_07 higher priority. Since 57 stars pass all our cuts, the derived motion is robust.

gmw_00000457.name: PKS J0334-3900
gmw_00000457.overview: PKS J0334-3900 is a tailed radio galaxy located at the center of the galaxy cluster Abell 3135 [arXiv:1610.02400, arXiv:1501.00498]. Its complex morphology is shaped by intracluster medium winds, orbital motion from a companion galaxy, and black hole precession [arXiv:1501.00498].

sources.summary: The paper reconstructs ATCA observations of the tailed radio galaxy PKS J0334-39, whose polarimetric structure probes Abell 3135; PURIFY recovers fine details without uniform weighting.

quotes.quote: PKS J0334-39 The observation of PKS J0334-39 was first presented in the work of pra13 , where the tailed radio galaxy's polametric structure was used to probe the environment of the galaxy cluster Abell 3135. The observation was also reprocessed using self calibration in pra16 , where it was used as an example of applying Generalised Complex CLEAN pra16 to a observation. The observation was performed using the ATCA (with the pre-CABB correlator) in 2001 is centered on RA = 03h 34m 07.18s DEC = -39 ^ 00 ^ 03.19 ^ (J2000), at a central frequency of 1.384 GHz. There are 12 channels, each with a width of 8 MHz.

Reconstructions of the source PKS J0334-39 are shown in Figure fig:0332-391 , for a pixel width and height of 2 arcseconds. The PURIFY reconstruction was performed using = 7 and = 1.08 , and ran for 347 iterations. The step size was adapted for the first i_ adapt = 200 iterations.
However, PURIFY has the ability to reconstruct the fine details of PKS J0334-39 and PKS J0116-473 without uniform weighting.

sources.summary: The paper models radio galaxy PKS J0334-3900 in Abell 3135, linking its morphology to ICM wind, orbital motion, black-hole precession, and rotation-measure constraints.

quotes.quote: We present a multi-wavelength study of the radio galaxy PKS J0334-3900, which resides at the centre of Abell 3135. Using Australia Telescope Compact Array (ATCA) observations at 1.4, 2.5, 4.6 8.6 GHz, we performed a detailed analysis of PKS J0334-3900. The morphology and spectral indices give physical parameters that constrain the dynamical history of the galaxy, which we use to produce a simulation of PKS J0334-3900. This simulation shows that the morphology can be generated by a wind in the intracluster medium (ICM), orbital motion caused by a companion galaxy, and precession of the black hole (BH). Additionally, ATCA polarisation data was used to obtain rotation measure values along the line of sight to PKS J0334-3900.

PKS J0334-3900 is located at the centre of the galaxy cluster Abell 3135, as shown in Fig. fig1 . Abell 3135 is found at the northern edge of the Horologium-Reticulum supercluster, and has a redshift of z=0.06228 0.00015 . PKS J0334-3900 is located at the same redshift with z=0.062310 0.000097 .

gmw_00000458.name: NGC5055
gmw_00000458.overview: NGC 5055 is a nearby, unbarred flocculent spiral galaxy [arXiv:2302.14800, arXiv:1702.04820] classified as an Sbc-type [arXiv:1202.2233] with a LINER nucleus [arXiv:2107.14281]. It features an extremely extended ultraviolet disk [arXiv:1406.0310] and a strongly warped neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:2302.14800, arXiv:astro-ph/0610114] that exhibits tidal interactions with surrounding satellite companions [arXiv:2404.09422, arXiv:2210.09383].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5055 as a regular non-active spiral with a warped outer disk, tidal interaction evidence, inner dual gas components, and asymmetric stellar continuum.

quotes.quote: NGC 5055 is a spiral non-active galaxy, control for NGC 5194. Previous radio and optical studies show an overall remarkable regularity and symmetry in its morphology and kinematics 5055_1, 5055_2 . There are evidence for a tidal interaction with a companion (UGC 8313), and the outer disk of NGC 5055 is warped. studied the \ emission of NGC 5055 with a Fabry-Perot: they found two velocity components in the inner 8 of this galaxy. One component is consistent with the global disk kinematics while the second one exhibits a pattern compatible with a bipolar outflow or a counter-rotating disk.

Our \ stellar continuum map presents an asymmetry towards the North-West (Fig.  pair6 ). The stellar velocity field shows very regular rotation pattern with the kinematic major-axis aligned with the photometric one at a PA of 100 . The velocity dispersion rises in the very centre ( < 2 ). The ionised gas \ and \ are mainly concentrated in the inner 5 .

sources.summary: The paper studies M63/NGC5055 as a nearby SA(rs)bc spiral with an extreme extended UV disk, detecting CO along the disk and in an outer UV region to probe molecular gas and star formation.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: center of the galaxy located at (J2000) RA = 13h 15m 49.3s , Dec = +42^ 01m 45.4s

quotes.quote: M63 is a remarkable example of a spiral galaxy with one of the most extended UV disks, so it offers the opportunity to search for the molecular gas and characterize the star formation in outer disk regions as revealed by the UV emission. We obtained deep CO(1--0) and CO(2--1) observations on the IRAM 30 m telescope along the major axis of the M63 disk from the center out to the galactocentric radius r_ gal = 1.6 r_ 25 and over a bright UV region at r_ gal = 1.36 r_ 25 . CO(1--0) is detected all along the M63 major axis out to r_ 25 , and CO(2--1) is confined to r_ gal = 0.68 r_ 25 , which may betray lower excitation temperatures in the outer disk. CO(1--0) is also detected in the external bright UV region of M63.

Classified as SA(rs)bc and located at 10.1 Mpc leroy2009 , M63 (or NGC5055) looks like a typical spiral galaxy, representative of a large class of local spirals, with no immediate neighbour, which excludes the potentiality of a galaxy in interaction. However, M63 is not very ordinary, since it is a remarkable example of a nearby spiral with a bright and XUV disk. In Fig. fig:M63image we show the GALEX NUV and FUV color-composite image of M63, where an extensive population of UV-bright star-forming regions and stellar clusters (tracing the O and B stars) is revealed. The M63 NUV and FUV surface-brightness profiles show a smooth decrease in the UV emission out to 700 in the equivalent radius (defined as the square root of the product of the half-minor axis times the half-major axis), namely out to 2.5 times the optical equivalent radius gildepaz2007 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5055 as a high-surface-brightness spiral test case, modeling its stellar disk and MOND rotation curve under an external field.

quotes.quote: For the purpose of illustration (rather than precise modeling) of the prediction of our numerical results on the RCs of real galaxies, we consider simple mass models of two prototype galaxies under relatively strong external fields: the high surface brightness (HSB) galaxy NGC 5055 and the low surface brightness (LSB) galaxy F568-V1 from the SPARC database Lel2016 . NGC 5055 (the ``sunflower'' galaxy) is one of the best examples of giant spiral galaxies with relatively well-measured extended RCs. This galaxy was also modeled by using their QUMOND simulation results.

The baryonic mass distribution of this galaxy is dominated by the stellar disk. Here we model this galaxy using an approximate exponential disk composed of three MN models (`MN3' model in Figure MN_expdisk ) ignoring the subdominant gas disk. As in , we assume a total baryonic mass of M_ bar =5.48 10^ 10 M _ for which the MOND radius is r_ M = GM_ bar /a_0 =7.98 kpc for a_0=1.20 10^ 10 m s ^ 2 .

The numerical prediction is then compared with the predictions of some models in Table tab:formulae . The outer part of the predicted RC of NGC 5055 agrees well with the AQUAL fitting function and starts to deviate from the algebraic MOND (red) flat line near R 40 kpc somewhat earlier than the QUMOND deviation occurring near R 65 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 5055 as a warped nearby spiral whose extended H i disk, declining then flat rotation curve, mild lopsidedness, and offset dark halo imply distinct inner and outer dynamical regimes.

quotes.quote: We present a study of the H i distribution and dynamics of the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 5055 based on observations with the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope. The gaseous disk of NGC 5055 extends out to about 40 kpc, equal to 3.5 R_ 25 , and shows a pronounced warp that starts at the end of the bright optical disk ( R_ 25 = 11.6 kpc). This very extended warp has large-scale symmetry, which along with the rotation period of its outer parts ( 1.5 Gyr at 40 kpc), suggests a long-lived phenomenon.

NGC 5055 shows remarkable overall regularity and symmetry. A mild lopsidedness is noticeable, however, both in the distribution and kinematics of the gas. The tilted ring analysis of the velocity field led us to adopt different values for the kinematical centre and for the systemic velocity for the inner and the outer parts of the system. This has produced a remarkable result: the kinematical and geometrical asymmetries disappear, both at the same time. These results point at two different dynamical regimes: an inner region dominated by the stellar disk and an outer one, dominated by a dark matter halo offset with respect to the disk.

sources.summary: The paper presents deep photometry of M63, finding a giant stellar tidal stream likely from a disrupted dwarf satellite, alongside a warped H I disk and extended UV disk.

quotes.quote: We present deep surface photometry of a very faint, giant arc-loop feature in the halo of the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 5055 (M63) that is consistent with being a part of a stellar stream resulting from the disruption of a dwarf satellite galaxy. This faint feature was first detected in early photographic studies by vanderKruit1979 ; more recently by martinezDelgado2010 and as presented in this work, the loop has been realized to be the result of a recent minor merger through evidence obtained by wide-field, deep images taken with a telescope of only 0.16 m aperture. The stellar stream is clearly confirmed in additional deep images taken with the 0.5 m telescope of the BlackBird Remote Observatory and the 0.8 m telescope of the McDonald Observatory. This low surface brightness ( _ R 26 mag arcsec ^ 2 ), arc-like structure around the disk of the galaxy extends 14.0 ( 29 kpc projected) from its center, with a projected width of 1.6 ( 3.3 kpc). The stream's morphology is consistent with that of the visible part of a giant, ``great-circle" type stellar stream originating from the recent accretion of a 10 ^ 8 M _ dwarf satellite in the last few Gyr.

Similar to discoveries made for NGC 5907 and NGC 4013, a recent HI study of M63 by battaglia2006 revealed a very pronounced warp ( 20 ^ ) in its gaseous disk that extends out 40 kpc from its center. Additionally, M63 has recently been found through a GALEX study by thilker2007 to host a Type 1 extended UV (XUV) disk that is characterized by significant star formation well beyond the anticipated star formation threshold (i.e., the H edge; see martin2001 ) at a galactocentric distance of up to 20 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5055 (M63) as a nearby flocculent spiral whose low-frequency emission extends beyond the optical galaxy and steepens toward the outer regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 5055 (M63) is a nearby flocculent spiral which has short multiple arms. Although this galaxy lacks organized spiral arms in the optical, polarization observation at 10.55 GHz shows regular, spiral magnetic fields with radial component due to dynamo action knapi00 . Radio continuum spectral index maps between 610 MHz and 1417 MHz by showed spectral index of about 0.6 in the central regions of the galaxy, which steepens to 1 towards the outer parts. The galaxy appears featureless in the above study as well as in the high frequency 10.7 GHz map by .

NGC 5055 (M63) : The 333 MHz observation (see Figure figure1 ) has an angular resolution of 17 10 corresponding to a spatial scale of 0.7 kpc at a distance of 9 Mpc. As is evident from the figure, the extent of the galaxy is larger at 333 MHz than the optical. In an earlier study by at 610 MHz and 1410 MHz at the much lower angular resolution of 58 87 , the galaxy appears featureless which is also the case in our higher resolution 333 MHz map. However, 10.55 GHz polarization observations by show regular spiral structure. The overall radio emission at 333 MHz at a level of 4 is 8.6 5.3 in extent, which has an integrated flux density of 2.3 0.13 Jy.

sources.summary: The paper presents deep outer-disk photometry of M63, interpreting its low-surface-brightness arc and red stellar stream as evidence for a recent minor accretion event.

quotes.quote: Here, we present deep follow-up surface photometry of the outer regions of M63, a galaxy that displays clear evidence of a recent minor merger . We aim to determine the basic properties of what remains of the progenitor satellite galaxy and study the effects of the accretion event on the parent system. M63 is an isolated, flocculent SA(rs)bc spiral.

The most striking low surface brightness feature we observe is the coherent arc structure that reaches R 29 kpc (in projection) from the center of M63 (see Figure fig1 ). The arc is reminiscent of a ``great-circle'' stellar tidal stream that would result from a tidally disrupted satellite galaxy . An ellipse fit to the light distribution displays a 14.8^ tilt with respect to the inner disk isophotes.
We have presented data that make a strong case for the faint features around M63 being the result of an ongoing minor accretion event involving a satellite galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5055 Brackett gamma and Herschel data to calibrate resolved infrared star-formation tracers, noting its AGN center, higher extinction, and elevated 70 micron calibration.

quotes.quote: For our first experiment, we use archival CFHT Br and Ks images of two nearby galaxies: NGC 5055 and NGC 6946, which are also part of the Herschel program KINGFISH (Key Insights on Nearby Galaxies: a Far-Infrared Survey with Herschel). We use the extinction corrected Br emission to derive the SFR(70) calibration for H ii regions in these two galaxies.

The central region of NGC 5055 is identified to have an AGN by moustakas2010 , and it shows one single bright source in the center covering up a region of about 10 ^ radius ( 400pc). This region is also avoided when selecting apertures. We selected a total of 73 regions for NGC 5055 and 94 regions for NGC 6946 (Fig. fig:6_7 fig:5_7 ).

In fact, H ii regions in NGC 5055 show higher extinction values than in NGC 6946 (Figure fig:HavB ), which may imply higher self-shielding for the dust surrounding the H ii regions, resulting in cooler mean dust temperatures e.g. calzetti2010 . Both effects are likely to contribute to the 50 higher C _ 70,200pc in NGC 5055.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5055 as a high-resolution and low-resolution test case for 3D tilted-ring modeling, noting its prominent outer warp and improved rotation-curve recovery.

quotes.quote: Here we focus on the automatic modeling of the well-known spiral galaxy NGC 5055. This galaxy is a difficult case because it has a prominent warp in the outer disc Bosma78 . We used THINGS natural-weighted data-cube at a spatial resolution of 10" (about 0.5 , assuming a distance of 10 Mpc) and we run \ by supplying only initial guesses for the inclination and the position angle. \ automatically identifies the galaxy emission in the data-cube, builds the mask, estimates the initial guesses for all the other parameters, performs the first fitting step for each radius with all parameters free and the second fitting step with only and free, after the regularization of the other parameters by Bezier functions (inclination and position angle) or constants. The comparison between the observations and the final best-fit model is shown in fig:chanmap through seven representative channel maps (a standard output of ).

In fig:n5055 we show the resulting values of the fitted parameters in both steps and we compare them with those of derived from a tilted-ring fit of the velocity field. Note how the regularization of the geometrical parameters improves the rotation curve, removing for instance the unphysical discontinuities around 28 and 47 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 5055 THINGS/VLA data alongside NGC 4214, noting different flux-profile behavior, flatter negative bowls, and distance-related suppression of missing-spacing artifacts.

quotes.quote: NGC 4214 and NGC 5055 observations and Data In the following, we present different data sets of NGC 4214 and NGC 5055 observations. The reason for this selection is that these galaxies are located at different distances and they have substantial differences in their morphology, extent, and physical parameters 2009AJ....137.4670L . Table tb:n4214_n5055_obs_par presents the physical and observational parameters of these two galaxies.

Figure fig:n5055_annflux shows the result of flux profiles for NGC 5055. The curves are quite different for the NGC 5055 data sets compared to those of NGC 4214 data sets (panel (a) of Fig. fig:n4214_annflux_psd ). The most significant difference is the strong increase of the measured total flux values for naturally (robust 5) weighted data sets, whereas the corresponding values are constant for the uniformly (robust 0.5) weighted data. This result suggest that the interferometric array yields more information regarding different scales and therefore, the negative bowls are flatter compared to those of NGC 4214.

sources.summary: NGC 5055 is used to compare two HI rotation-curve data sets and their MOND-RAR, dark matter, and RCFM fits, illustrating underconstrained galaxy mass modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 5055 In this section we compare two different RC data, both from HI, and their resulting dark matter, MOND-RAR and RCFM fits. The first RC is from Batt as reported in SPARC. Their luminous model has no stellar bulge. The RC data and luminous mass model are reported at the reliable distance of 9.83 0.30 Mpc, from the Tip of The Red Giant Branch method.

The second RC for NGC 5055 is from the THINGS Blok at a distance of 10.1 .0 Mpc from the Hubble Flow, including a stellar bulge component. RCFM fits to both sets of RC data can be seen in Fig. fit5055 , at reported distances, using the reported luminous mass models. This galaxy serves as an example of the underconstrained nature of galaxy mass modeling Conroy .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5055 as a luminous spiral whose light profile, rotation-curve bump, outer decline, and maximum-disk decomposition closely correspond.

quotes.quote: Fig. 2 shows the results for the luminous spiral galaxy NGC 5055 recently obtained by Battaglia et al. (2003). The plot shows the observed HI rotation curve and the standard decomposition with maximum disk model (derived from the F-band photometric profile shown in the top panel) and isothermal halo. The rotation curve has the steep rise characteristic of very luminous HSBs and shows a bump in the inner regions, around 2 kpc. The bump is present on both galaxy sides (receding and approaching) and completely symmetrical. The photometric profile shows a bump at the same radius and indeed the maximum disk rotation curve calculated from it reproduces the bump in the observed rotation curve perfectly.

In the outer parts, around the Holmberg radius R(Ho), the rotation curve of NGC 5055 declines by about 25 km/s and remains flat out to the last measured point at 40 kpc. The decomposition of the rotation curve shows that the maximum disk model (with isothermal or NFW profile) reproduces the shape of the rotation curve also in the outer parts.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5055 as an Sbc spiral with a warped HI disk and finds the model diverges from its roughly flat rotation curve.

quotes.quote: NGC 5055. This Sbc galaxy ( D = 10.1 \ Mpc , M_B = -21.12 ) has an extended and warped tenuous outer HI disc which originates the wiggle visible for large R values in its rotation curve. The THINGS rotation curve agrees well with the previous determination B06 and extends further out thus being able to probe the warp for the first time. Position angle (P.A. = 102^o ) and inclination (i = 59^o) are also in agreement.

NGC 5055. The striking feature of this fit is the opposite behaviour of the theoretical and observational rotation curve in the very inner regions.
