Although such a strong disagreement for a best fit model could be surprising, it is worth noting that actually only two points are missed so that their weight in the overall ^2 is quite small compared to the other ones probing the intermediate and outer regions. What we have suggested for NGC 3621 clearly takes place for this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 5055 with a two-component stellar decomposition, uses THINGS and hybrid HERACLES/THINGS curves, and finds ISO halo fits reasonable while NFW fits fail.

quotes.quote: NGC 5055 In Figure fig:N5055-ISO we plot the results of the mass modelling for NGC 5055. For NGC 5055 we use a 2-component stellar decomposition to determine the predicted stellar rotation curves. For NGC 5055 2g=5.3 , which is very close to the Milky Way value.

However, the radial profiles for 2g shows a depression in the central region, which leads to a substantially different 2g rotation curve in the inner 5 , as compared to using a single value for the conversion. The HERACLES rotation curve is steeper in the inner 5 . We therefore consider two observed rotation curves in our fits --- the THINGS rotation curve and a HERACLES/THINGS rotation curve assembled by using the HERACLES rotation curve within 5 and switching over to the THINGS rotation curve for larger radii.

sources.summary: The paper presents deep imaging of M 63, identifying halo stellar streams and five low-surface-brightness satellite candidates, deriving a low orbital halo mass, and emphasizing its declining rotation curve.

quotes.quote: We present a deep (50 hours exposed) image of the nearby spiral galaxy M 63 (NGC 5055), taken with a 0.14-m aperture telescope. The galaxy halo exhibits the known very faint system of stellar streams extending across 110 kpc. We found 5 very low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxies around M 63.

Assuming they are satellites of M 63, their median parameters are: absolute B magnitude --8.8 mag, linear diameter 1.3 kpc, surface brightness 27.8 mag/sq. arcsec and linear projected separation 93 kpc. Based on four brighter satellites with measured radial velocities, we derived a low orbital mass estimate of M 63 to be (5.1 1.8) 10^ 11 M_ on a scale of 216 kpc. The specific property of M 63 is its declining rotation curve.

sources.summary: The paper treats M 63 as one of the massive spiral-group nodes in the nearby filament, giving its group context, peculiar velocity, and shared low mass-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: Muller2017 and Karachentsev2020 supposed that the galaxy groups around massive spiral galaxies M 63 (NGC 5055), M 51 (NGC 5194) and M 101 (NGC 5457) are part of a filament that extends towards the Local Void, including possibly the NGC 6503 galaxy too. Karachentsev2020 and Karachentsev2021 reviewed recently the structure and kinematics of the galaxy groups around M 63 and M 51. In this work, we discuss new data about the M 101 group population in the context of its membership in the M 63/M 51/M 101 filament.

According to a "Distance - Velocity" diagram in the Extragalactic Distance Database http://edd.ifa.hawaii.edu/NAMcalculator Kourkchi2020 , the galaxy M 63 at the far end of the filament has a peculiar radial velocity of 50 km s ^ 1 relative to the unperturbed Hubble flow with H=73 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , whereas, the galaxy NGC 6503 at the close end of the chain has a peculiar velocity of 115 km s ^ 1 . This difference in peculiar velocities results in a steeper observed slope, H=96 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 instead of 73 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 . A general shape of the chain of considered groups in supergalactic coordinates is shown in Fig. figure3 . The early-type and late-type galaxies depicted by red and blue symbols, respectively. The host galaxies in groups are indicated by squares. Small and large circles around them correspond to the virial radius of group, R_ vir , and the radius of zero-velocity sphere, R_0 3.5 R_ vir , which separates the volume of the group from the general expanding cosmic space. Six new dwarf galaxies found by us are indicated by asterisks. They can be identified by their supergalactic coordinates presented in Table 1. In addition to the alleged members of specified groups, we show by crosses in Fig. figure3 other galaxies from the UNGC catalog with their distance estimate in the interval (5 -- 10) Mpc. Almost all of them (9 out of 10) lie near the Local supergalactic plane (SGB < 15^ ). General summary of data on 72 galaxies in the region SGL = [ 30^ 80^ ] , SGB = [ 10^ 37^ ] with the distance estimates D = (5-10) Mpc is presented in Appendix appendix . The columns of this table contain: (1) --- the galaxy name; (2,3) --- supergalactic coordinates in degrees; (4) --- K band integral luminosity in the Sun luminosity units, taken from UNGC; (5) --- morphological type from UNGC in de Vaucouleurs digital scale; (6) --- radial velocity relative to the Local group centroid, in km s ^ 1 ; (7) --- distance in Mpc; (8) method, by which the distance estimation is made; here ``SN'' denotes the distance estimated from the luminosity of supernovae, ``TF'' is the estimate by Tully-Fisher relation between the galaxy rotation amplitude and luminosity, ``NAM'' is a kinematic estimate taking into account the local velocity field Shaya2017 , and ``txt'' is the rough estimate by a galaxy texture. The galaxies in each group are ranked according to their SGL. In Appendix appendix and in Table table2 , we distinguish between the dwarf galaxies that we discovered and known from the literature as "Dw" and "dw", respectively. We examined in DESI Legacy Imaging Surveys all sky region outlined in Fig. figure3 of a total area of about 1200 square degrees and found only one dwarf galaxy with supposed distance of D < 10 Mpc. The galaxy is shown in last row of Table table1 . This fact tell us that the main body of filament M 63/M 51/M 101/ NGC 6503 is indeed surrounded by almost empty space, being part of the Local Void, which extends towards the northern supergalactic pole. The M 63 group as the farthest side of filament from us is the closest one to the Local Sheet plane. Wherein, we did not find any massive group in the Local Sheet itself which could serve as a southward continuation of the filament under consideration. As seen from Fig. figure3 , the zones of gravitational influence of the massive spiral galaxies M 63, M 51, and M 101, outlined by the radius R_0 , intersect with each other. This means that in the future these groups will merge into a single system. The galaxy NGC 6503 with its companion stand apart from the main body of the filament. The dwarf spheroidal galaxies without obvious sings of star formation are located inside the R_0 zones, demonstrating the well-known segregation effect of the early type galaxies ( T < 0 ) against the late type ones in dependence on the density of environment. From the data of Table table3 follows that the average weighted ratio of the total mass-to- K band luminosity for the M 63, M 51, and M 101 groups is (12 4) M_ /L_ .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5055 as an unbarred flocculent disc galaxy with two discs, a minor rotation-curve fall, and reduced dark matter within four outer-disc scale lengths.

quotes.quote: NGC 5055 (M63, The Sunflower Galaxy) is a disc galaxy in the constellation Canes Venatici. It has a flocculent structure, no visible bar, and its photometric profile shows two discs. The rotation curve of NGC 5055 extends to 900 arcsec ( 39  kpc) and has a maximal rotation speed of V_ max 210  km/s at 13  kpc. It shows a minor fall by V = 20  km/s. A stronger fall down in the rotation curve was observed in the data by deBlok+2008 but DiTeodoro_Peek2021 , who took into account the warp of this galaxy, did not confirm the falling character of the rotation curve.

When the parameter (M/L)_ d is fixed at the value of 0.46 , regardless of the type of halo, the galaxy black has reduced content of the dark matter within four exponential scales of the more extended disc ( h_2=12.9  kpc).

sources.summary: The paper reports low-level streaming motions in NGC 5055, only tight upper limits on radial flows in the inner disk, and clear H I warp domination beyond R>200.

quotes.quote: A strong negative slope in the s_1 s_3 plot for the CO data [ Fig. fig:s13:5055 () ] suggests the presence of low-level streaming motions centered around R =30 . Although inspection of the K band image does not reveal clear evidence of a bar, the isophotes do become ``boxier'' around this radius, leading to a dip in the ellipticity _ morph . In spite of this localized deviation, the smallness of the s_1 term places an upper limit of v_R 5 \ (3 of the circular speed) on the magnitude of any radial flows in the region R =15 -70 .

The \ kinematics are clearly dominated by a warp for R>200 (0.5 R_ 25 ). Inside of this radius, a possible region of gas inflow is seen, highlighted by the small box in Fig. fig:s13:5055 (d). We interpret this weak feature (again amounting to radial motions of no more than 3 of the circular speed) to spiral streaming motions, since one finds CO and \ spiral arms crossing the galaxy's minor axis at approximately these radii, where their effects are more likely to have an impact on the observed kinematics.

sources.summary: The paper derives center estimates and harmonic-decomposition results for NGC 5055, describing its disk morphology, non-circular motions, and whether the inferred potential is consistent with being round.

quotes.quote: NGC 5055 NGC 5055 (Fig. fig:ngc-5055 ) a) Center estimates [ 1.5ex ] NGC 5055 is classified as an Sbc galaxy and has a flocculent structure in the 3.6 m IRAC image. The IRAC image shows a compact nucleus with a well-defined center, whose position coincides to within 1 with a faint central radio continuum source. We use the position of the latter ( =8 8 , =0 4 ) as the input center for a \ run with all parameter left free. We derive the dynamical center by averaging the , \ values for radii smaller than 450 \ to exclude the parts with sparsely filled tilted-rings beyond 450 .

sources.summary: The paper detects diffuse polarized emission on both sides of NGC 5055's minor axis, strongest near the warped southwestern gaseous disk, with magnetic fields following spiral structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 5055 (M63) Diffuse polarized emission is detected from the disk of this inclined galaxy on both sides of the minor axis. The highest brightnesses are associated with the zone of strong warping of the gaseous disk in the southwest at the edge of the star-forming disk. A fainter counterpart is seen in the northeast. The minimum in polarized intensity occurs at the PA of the receding major axis (PA = 102 ^ , as tabulated in Table table:summary ). The polarized fraction of the brightest feature is mainly in the range of 5--10 , but at the southernmost end (where the contribution from the bright inner disk is significantly fainter) the fraction increases to 15--25 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5055 as a flocculent spiral with regular magnetic fields, the weakest total field in the sample, and no distinct spiral structure in the field map.

quotes.quote: NGC 5055 is a flocculent spiral galaxy and lacks organized spiral structure when seen in optical. Polarization observations revealed regular spiral magnetic fields believed to have been generated from turbulent dynamo action knapi00 . They estimated a mean equipartition magnetic field of 9.2 G close to our value of 9.5 G. Of all the galaxies in the sample, NGC 5055 has the weakest total magnetic field of 14.5 G in the center and falls off to about 10 G in the disk and 8 G towards the edge. No distinct spiral structure has been seen in the map of total magnetic field.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5055 as the largest sample galaxy and reports high disk attenuation plus a strong SFR-map peak.

quotes.quote: We have therefore selected eight galaxies in our sample: NGC 628, NGC 925, NGC 1097, NGC 3351, NGC 4321, NGC 4736, NGC 5055, and NGC 7793. Properties As we can see in Table tab:sample , the sample is made of fairly regular star--forming galaxies in the nearby universe, spanning the Hubble sequence from Sab to Sd. This ensures different levels of star-forming activity (from sSFR=-10.76 yr ^ 1 for NGC 4736 to sSFR=-9.59 yr ^ 1 for NGC 7793) and different levels of diffuse dust emission sauvage1992a .
At the opposite end, NGC 5055 exhibits high values of the attenuation on a large fraction of the disk.
A strong peak is also present in NGC 4321 and NGC 5055.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5055 (M63) as a massive SAbc galaxy whose neutral gas follows the stellar light but shows outer position-angle distortions and spiral-arm streaming.

quotes.quote: NGC 5055 NGC 5055 (M63) is a well-studied, massive SAbc galaxy. Archival VLA observations originally published in are presented in Fig.  fig:n5055sum . The distribution of neutral gas follows the distribution of the stellar light, but appears to change position angle slightly beyond the stellar disc. This is more apparent in the HI velocity field where the isovelocity contours become distorted in the outer disc, especially along the major axis. Deviations from circular motion within the stellar disc are likely due to streaming motions along the spiral arms ( n5055hipub ).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 5055 as a nearby flocculent late-type spiral, using archival gas and star-formation measurements alongside NGC 3627 and M 83 to study KS-relation variation.

quotes.quote: All three galaxies are nearby late-type galaxies with grand design (NGC 3627 and M 83) or flocculent (NGC 5055) spiral structures Elmegreen:1987kq . They belong to a similar population of galaxies in terms of stellar mass and SFR plane (Figure fig:MSFR ) but NGC 3627 seems to have lower metallicity than the other two galaxies (Figure fig:MZ ). We use archival data to estimate gas and SFR surface densities. The spatial resolutions of the data are 15 arcsec for NGC 3627 (700 pc at the distance of the galaxy) and NGC 5055 (570 pc), and 25 arcsec for M 83 (550 pc). The inclination corrected spatial resolutions are 892 pc for NGC 3627, 819 pc for NGC 5055, and 575 pc for M 83, which are larger than the boundary spatial resolutions at which the KS relation breaks 80-250 pc, Momose:2010fj,Verley:2010rt,Onodera:2010uq .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5055 as a difficult warped system whose restricted EPG model fits poorly and likely underestimates anomalous HI mass.

quotes.quote: NGC 5055. This system is well known to host a prominent warp in its outskirt e.g. Battaglia+06 , which can be traced accurately with \ see Fig. 4 in Barolo . Modelling the EPG of the whole dataset resulted in a very poor fit, and we decided to focus on the innermost 20 where the INC and PA are relatively stable. In fact, anomalous \ flux may spuriously arise at R>20 due to the overlap of consecutive annuli along the line of sight, given the rapid variation of the INC and PA in these regions. Despite this treatment, a significant fraction of the anomalous \ emission, including a filament visible along the major axis, does not seem to be reproduced by our model: it is likely that the EPG mass of this galaxy is underestimated. We also note that the background noise in the datacube is not constant and is difficult to treat properly.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5055 as an unbarred spiral with a warped H I disc and visible spiral structure, whose resolved star-formation-law indices are unusually sublinear and well correlated.

quotes.quote: NGC 5055 NGC 5055 (Messier 63), shown in Figure fig:ngc5055 , is a moderately inclined unbarred spiral galaxy with a very large, warped H i disc battaglia2006 , in which massive stars have recently formed thilker2007 . The spiral structure of the galaxy is clearly visible in our SCUBA-2 observations. HARP ^ 12 CO J=3 2 observations of NGC 5055 were presented by wilson2012 .
Uniquely in our sample, NGC 5055 shows an index which is significantly <1 in both the SED-fit and 250 m-derived cases, and has a very high correlation coefficient between _ H_ 2 and _ sfr .
The Herschel 250 m-derived star formation law index for NGC 5055 is similar to that found in M31 by ford2013 , suggesting a commonality between the two galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5055 as a face-on, moderately interacting galaxy with significant diffuse HI, satellite-linked bridges, inner-disk rotational lag, and tidal-bridge turbulence.

quotes.quote: The galaxies with a significant f_ diffuse ( 40 ) include NGC628, NGC 5055, NGC 5194, and NGC 5457. These four are the most face-on galaxies in the sample, with inclinations below 60 .

For moderately interacting galaxies (NGC 5055 and NGC 5457), there is clear evidence of a large amount of diffuse linking to the surrounding satellites. The bridges have no counterparts in stellar light (Figure fig:atlas , also see mid-infrared and violet images in Leroy09 ).
But it is limited to a relatively small radial range from the center in the relatively face-on and moderately highly interacting galaxies NGC 5055 and NGC 5457.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5055 has ordered spiral magnetic fields, central polarized emission, rudimentary spiral armlets with nuclear star formation, and unperturbed interarm fields.

quotes.quote: To check this hypothesis we observed two flocculent spirals, NGC 3521 and NGC 5055, in the radio continuum (total power and polarization) at 10.55 GHz with a resolution of 1 13 . A search for traces of optical spiral patterns has also been made by observing them in the H line and by filtering their available blue images. NGC 3521 and NGC 5055 were found to possess a mean degree of magnetic field ordering similar to that in grand-design spirals. However, the polarized emission fills the central region of NGC 5055 while a minimum of polarized intensity was observed in the inner disk of NGC 3521. This can be explained by a more uniform star formation distribution in the centre of NGC 3521, while a higher concentration of star-forming activity in the nuclear region and in the rudimentary spiral ``armlets'' of NGC 5055 leaves broader interarm regions with unperturbed regular magnetic fields.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5055 to show how Fabry-Perot data resolve the inner rotation curve, avoid 21-cm beam smearing, and reveal central double emission lines.

quotes.quote: Figure ngc5055_h1_ha shows a 21-cm PV diagram of NGC 5055 taken from 1986A&AS...66..505W ( 1986A&AS...66..505W ). From the 21-cm data alone, one can calculate that the maximum velocity gradient at the center of the galaxy is of the order of 45 kms^ 1 kpc^ 1 . The red line shows the rotation curve derived from the data, which has a maximum gradient of 300 kms^ 1 kpc^ 1 . This shows how the 21-cm data are affected by beam smearing.
NGC 5055 harbors double emission lines in its center, as noted by 2004A&A...420..147B ( 2004A&A...420..147B ).

sources.summary: The paper argues NGC 5055 may host an AGN, but its observed nuclear UV flux is dominated by hot stars rather than the active nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 5055. -- Prominent absorption lines in the UV spectrum of the nucleus of NGC 5055 suggest that hot stars dominate the light maoz98 . This conclusion is supported by the lack of significant UV variability and the extended morphology of the UV source maoz05 . Although an X-ray source with an AGN-like spectrum is detected flohic06 , the equivalent hydrogen column density measured from the X-ray spectrum implies E(B-V)=0.87 , which translates into an attenuation by a factor of 100 at 2500 . Our conclusion is that an AGN may be present in this galaxy, but is not the source of the observed UV flux.

sources.summary: The paper places this galaxy in Group C, where sparse inner rotation-curve data leave the core uncertain and the BDM fit effectively reduces to an NFW-like profile.

quotes.quote: We only present here the conclusions of the last group C, composed of three galaxies (NGC2403, NGC50455 and NGC7331) BDM2011 . These galaxies have fitted values such that r_c/r_s < 10^ 6 for most stellar mass models. The reduced number of data close to galactic center prevents us from finding whether these galaxies actually have or not a core. We conclude in that ISO and Burkert are poorly fitting profiles, meanwhile BDM has a central value for r_c 0 , so BDM reduces to NFW profile and both have equivalent fits.

sources.summary: NGC 5055 is one of the disks with systematic isotope-ratio variations, showing higher ^ 13 CO/C ^ 18 O at larger radii and lower ratios at higher star formation density.

quotes.quote: In the left panel of Figure fig:trends , we see ^ 13 CO/C ^ 18 O increase with increasing radius for NGC 4254, NGC 4321, NGC 5055 and NGC 6946. Again NGC 628 represents the outlier, with a low ratio and perhaps a dropping rather than rising profile.

In NGC 4254, NGC 4321, NGC 5055 and NGC 6946 we do observe systematic internal variations. Here ^ 13 CO/C ^ 18 O increases by 40 with increasing radius. We also find a decreasing ^ 13 CO/C ^ 18 O ratio with increasing surface density of star formation.

sources.summary: The paper notes that NGC5055 has a relatively large reduced chi-squared fit because its H rotation curve and inclination uncertainties were not fully accounted for.

quotes.quote: Note that some of the fits generate relatively large reduced ^2 values, especially for NGC5055. However, it does not mean that the CGG model works poor in these galaxies.

However, for NGC5055, its H rotation curve has not been taken into account Lelli . The resultant data points rely on HI data only, which may generate some systematic errors. Besides, the uncertainties due to the inclination of galaxies have not been considered either Lelli . In particular, the uncertainty of the inclination of NGC5055 is greater than 10 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5055 as the representative unbarred spiral, with mostly circular CO kinematics and smaller noncircular motions than barred NGC 2903.

quotes.quote: Examples of the results of calculations of v_ , , v_r , and for two representative galaxies of SA (NGC 5055) and SB (NGC 2903) types are shown in figure fig:ex , while the rest of the sample is plotted in Appendix. Figure fig:ex shows several important features of these systems: (1) The kinematics is clearly dominated by circular rotation at all radii ( v_r v_ ), especially outside the bar region, and the derived v_ of both galaxies are consistent with the shapes and amplitudes of the rotation curves derived from HI data dB08 . (2) The fraction of noncircular motions is somewhat larger in the inner region of the barred galaxy; within R<50 , we find 0.15 in NGC 5055 and 0.20 in NGC 2903.

While noncircular motions are not entirely absent in NGC 5055, they are a factor of 1.5-2 smaller than in NGC 2903; noncircular motions in this SA galaxy are possibly caused by gravitational torques due to the spiral arms.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5055 as a scaling-relation outlier, suggesting its central stellar velocity dispersion may be abnormal rather than its maximum rotation velocity or black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: Except for NGC 4395, it is apparent from inspection of Figure fig:v-sigma that five galaxies (Circinus, NGC 613, NGC 1300, NGC 4151, and NGC 5055) are mild outliers, noticeably outside the 1 ( 1 _ rms ) scatter band. Of these five galaxies, two galaxies (NGC 1300 and NGC 5055) are >2 outliers. Davis:2018 note that NGC 1300 is an outlier in the M_ BH - M_ *,tot diagram for spiral galaxies, with either an overmassive black hole or a low total stellar mass, which is matched here by either a low v_ max or a high _0 . Davis:2017 pointed out that NGC 5055 is an outlier in the M_ BH - _0 diagram for spiral galaxies, with either an overmassive black hole or a low central stellar velocity dispersion.

Circinus, NGC 4395, and NGC 5055 are not outliers in Figure fig:M_BH-log_v , indicating that they might have abnormal central stellar velocity dispersions, rather than deviant maximum rotational velocities or black hole masses.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5055 as a potential superthin galaxy, deriving a very low H I disk axial ratio but noting it lacks the compact halo inferred for two similar sample galaxies.

quotes.quote: For this galaxy, the axial ratio of the disc is found to be 0.07, whereas we find axial ratios of 0.06, 0.06, and 0.08 for NGC 5055, NGC 6946, and NGC 7331, respectively. Mass-modeling of superthin galaxies also revealed that the superthin galaxies host a compact dark matter halo with R_ C /R_ D < 2 , where R_ C is the core radius of the dark matter halo and R_ D is the scale length of the optical disc see, e.g., banerjee17 . For our three galaxies, R_ C /R_ D are found to be 3.7, 1.6, and 1.4 for NGC 5055, NGC 6946, and NGC 7331, respectively. As can be seen, except NGC 5055, the other two galaxies host compact dark matter halos as well. These results suggest that NGC 5055, NGC 6946, and NGC 7331 could possibly represent superthin galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5055 is not well fit by one flat disk; its H I layer appears warped and is modeled with inner and outer disks.

quotes.quote: NGC 5055 As discussed in sec.results , a flat, axisymmetric disc model was not a good fit to the velocity map of this galaxy, and it seems that the HI layer beyond R_ 25 378 lies in a different plane from that interior to this radius -- \ the HI disc is warped. We have fitted the map inside and outside this radius with two separate flat discs and the fitted circular speeds of these two separate parts are marked in Figure  fig.N5055 by the cyan points with error bars. The global parameters of the fit to the inner disc are given in Table  tab.summary and those for the outer disc are reported in the text. The black points with error bars in Figure  fig.N5055 , many of which are obscured by the cyan points, report a fit that exploits the ability of \ to model the separate parts of the gas layer that have differing projection geometries as an oval flow for the inner optically bright part of the galaxy, although we do not argue that the inner disc is intrinsically oval or that the small ``non-circular'' velocities returned by \ are physically meaningful.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5055 as an Sbc H II/LINER galaxy near UGC 8313 and the M 51 group, with one of the strongest individual SZ-correlation detections.

quotes.quote: NGC 5055 (M 63): This Sbc galaxy is listed as a H II galaxy and with a LINER designation. It has only one significant nearby galaxy, UGC 8313 and may be associated with the M 51 group, lying in the outer parts (SW), with a separation of 1 Mpc.

The six objects with the most significant correlations are NGC 253 (chance probability or p-value of 5.9 ), NGC 3031 (p = 9.2 ), NGC 5236 (p = 1.3 ), and three objects with p < 0.2 : NGC 891, NGC 4826, and NGC 5055.
These are followed by NGC 5055 (1.7 or 2.12 ) and two galaxies with S/N 1.5 : NGC 253 (5.9 ); and NGC 5236 (7.6 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports K-band SRT measurements for NGC 5055, finding its 18.6 GHz flux compatible with existing fits but its 24.6 GHz flux in possible excess pending higher-frequency confirmation.

quotes.quote: For NGC 3627 and NGC 5055, the 18.6 GHz flux density is still compatible with the fit in ; the 24.6 GHz flux density, instead, is in excess of the fit extrapolation and larger than that at 18.6 GHz (see Fig. fig:sed for NGC 3627). With the current data, it is difficult to asses if the 24.6 GHz excess seen in NGC 3627 and NGC 5055 is due to an additional component, such as AME, or if it is just a statistical fluctuation. Observations at larger frequencies, like those that will be possible with the forthcoming Q-band receiver for SRT, are needed to confirm this finding. In fact, a model including synchrotron and free-free only can be fit to the entire 24.6 Ghz range, resulting in minor modifications with respect to the fit for 10 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5055 as a large spiral with two detected companions and a nearly connected gas cloud candidate, with companion velocities compared to disk orientation.

quotes.quote: This is a large spiral galaxy. Besides the target we detect two companions, UGC 8313 and UGC 8365, and a gas cloud candidate which almost connects to the main disk. The cloud candidate and UGC 8365 follow the velocity orientation of the disk, although the velocity difference between NGC 5055 and UGC 8365 is so large that it is doubtful they are in the same dynamical system. UGC 8313's systemic velocity is such that it coincides with the velocities in the receding side of NGC 5055 while its location is on the approaching side.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 5055 as a spiral with moderate disk-geometry variations, similar inner transverse/circular velocities, and coherent large-amplitude anisotropies.

quotes.quote: NGC 5055 NGC 5055 is a spiral galaxy . The TRM measurements of the inclination angle and P.A. show moderate variations across the disk, with fluctuations of less than 15^ (see Fig. NGC5055 ). In the inner disk, for R< R_ 25 350'' , the transversal velocity determined by the VRM is similar to the circular velocity determined by the TRM.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5055 radial profiles to show a flat inner atomic profile, gas near the critical relation, very large extinction corrections, and possible foreground-screen overestimation.

quotes.quote: At first glance, it is impressive how well the relation _ gas _ crit holds for some of the galaxies (NGC 4501, 5033, and 5055). On the other hand, there are two galaxies (NGC 4321 and 4414) where _ gas > _ crit over a significant range of radius, while in NGC 4736 and 5457, _ gas < _ crit across most of the disk.
In NGC 4414, 5033, and 5055, the \ profile remains relatively flat across the inner disk, while in NGC 4321, 4501, 4736, and 5457, the \ profile declines toward the center.

While extinction appears to be negligible for NGC 5457 (M101), a typical value of A_ H 1.5 mag is found for the other six galaxies. After correction for extinction, the global SFRs increase by factors ranging from 1.3 (for NGC 5457) to 140 (for NGC 5055). Comparing the disk-averaged extinction from this model to the radio-derived extinction, we find that for several galaxies, especially NGC 4321 and 5055, this method severely overestimates the global extinction, by up to 4 mag.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 5055 among four galaxies whose rotation curves are fit with the modified acceleration law, reporting good agreement and the galaxy's fitted luminous mass and core radius.

quotes.quote: In Fig. 4, we show fits to the four galaxies NGC 1560, NGC 2903, NGC 4565 and NGC 5055 using the modified acceleration law ( coremodel ) including the galaxy core with =2 . As can be seen from the data fitting, the agreement with the core and extended rotation curves data is good for the two fitting parameters M and the core radius r_c . In Fig. 5, we display a 3-dimensional plot of v versus the range of distance 0.1 kpc < < 10 kpc and the range of total galaxy mass M used in the fitting of rotational velocity data.
For NGC 5055 the luminous mass is M=8.55 10^ 10 M_ and the core radius is r_c=2.04 kpc .

sources.summary: The paper finds clustered nuclear point sources in NGC 5055, argues the bright central source is likely a compact star cluster, and attributes LINER X-rays to stellar processes.

quotes.quote: NGC 5055 (T2, Sb/) has a five point sources in its nucleus, clustered close to the center, and a bright hard source at the very center. The spectrum of this source is well fitted by a two-temperature plasma model, but a power-law plus plasma model gives a very poor fit. We did not attempt more complex models due to the small number of degrees of freedom. Maoz et al. (1995) resolved the central point source in the UV and obtained a radius of 7 pc (at the distance assumed in this paper).

sources.summary: The atlas reports that NGC 5055 has a large, strongly warped H i disk, radio continuum tracing its stellar disk and spiral structure, and diffuse arcs around the bright disk.

quotes.quote: This flocculent spiral galaxy possesses a huge H i disk (of 30 arcmin diameter), which is strongly warped at large radii (Battaglia et al. ). The diffuse outer disk is not accurately represented in our H i image and the reader is referred to Battaglia et al. ( ) for a better representation. The radio continuum closely traces the stellar disk and its spiral structure. Two diffuse arcs are apparent to the North-west and South-east of the high brightness disk.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5055 as a reasonably fitted rotation-curve system despite a large reduced chi-square, attributing inner-region divergences to bulge rather than halo modeling.

quotes.quote: On the contrary, the case of NGC 5055 (shown in the central panel of Fig. fig: rcex ) is an example of a system with a large reduced ^2 ( ^2 = 3.63 ), but with a nevertheless reasonably well fitted rotation curve. Indeed, the best fit model tracks the data almost everywhere but in the very inner regions where the circular velocity is likely to be mainly determined by the bulge component which we hold fixed in our analysis. We have therefore included this galaxy in our sample of well fitted systems since the divergences may be very likely an evidence of a failure in the bulge rather than the halo modeling.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 5055 shows steep star-formation decline, radial cold-dust temperature behavior, and heating-map similarity to dust-temperature maps.

quotes.quote: The most noticeable offsets occur in NGC 1097, NGC 3351, NGC 4736, and NGC 5055; in our sample, these galaxies exhibit some of the steepest declines in star formation from the center to the outskirts.

When both parameters are fitted simultaneously, the cold dust temperature maps for 10 galaxies (NGC 1097, NGC 1512, NGC 3351, NGC 4254, NGC 4321, NGC 4569, NGC 4579, NGC 4736, NGC 5055, and NGC 7331) exhibit a strong radial dependence.

As might be expected, we see a strong similarity between the heating maps and the T_c maps, particularly for NGC 1512, NGC 3351, NGC 4321, NGC 4579, NGC 4736, and NGC 5055, similar to the correlation between resolved 24 m emission and the cold dust temperature found for NGC 891 hughes2014 .

sources.summary: The paper states that NGC 5055 is not well reproduced in its outer rotation curve without the EFE, but an external field improves chi-squared to 1.15.

quotes.quote: (9) NGC 5055 : Although the general form of the MOND rotation curve agrees with the observed curve, the latter is not reproduced well in the outer parts where the predicted rotation curve by MOND without an EFE falls above the observed one. An external filed of about a_e=0.310^ +0.115 _ 0.135 a_0 can improve the quality of the fit such that the ^2 decreases from 3.06 to 1.15. NGC 5194 n5194-p, n3034-md, n3034-lu within 5.315^o angular distance from NGC 5055 which corresponds to the radial distance of about 855 kpc (maximum 7.215 Mpc and minimum 463 kpc) can produce a_e=1.2 10^ 2 a_0 (maximum a_e=2.22 10^ 2 a_0 and minimum a_e=1.43 10^ 3 a_0 ) at the location of NGC 5055 n3198-dm, n5055-lu, ic-md which has a good overlap with the implied a_e by the Milgromian dynamics fit within the 95 confidence interval.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5055 as the resolved global-profile case where the velocity width exceeds the data resolution and the spectrum follows convolved CO more closely than H I.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:globprofs shows one galaxy (NGC 5055, left panel) where the velocity width of the galaxy as measured in is significantly (a factor of 2) larger than the velocity resolution of the data. Despite the coarse velocity resolution, it is still possible to directly compare features in the spectrum with those in the convolved CO and spectra. We see that for this galaxy, the spectrum follows the convolved CO spectrum more closely than the convolved one.

sources.summary: The review cites M63 as an XUV disk case where CO detections imply low star formation efficiency, possibly caused by a warp or turbulence.

quotes.quote: dessauges14 studied a UV-bright region at =1.36 r_ 25 in the XUV disk of M63 (Fig. 5). They detected CO in two out of twelve pointings and concluded that the molecular gas has a low star formation efficiency star formation efficiency (or, equivalently, the molecular gas has a long depletion time) compared to regions within the optical disk. They suggested that the low star formation efficiency may be caused by a warp or by high turbulence.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 5055 has discrepant inner H2 surface densities, distance differences that could affect its rotation curve, and a volume-density gas-SFR conversion illustrated for the galaxy.

quotes.quote: For example, the inner H _2 surface densities in NGC 4736 and NGC 5055 that were calculated by differ from results by one order of magnitude.
For some galaxies (DDO 154, IC 2574, NGC 5055, NGC 6946, and NGC 7793), the difference between the two distances is not negligible and could slightly influence the rotation curve.
Fig. fig:NGC5055colorsgals illustrates the effect of the conversion to volume densities on the correlation between gas and SFR for the galaxy NGC 5055.

sources.summary: The model gives NGC 5055 one of the highest central gas Toomre parameters, a roughly constant mass accretion rate, and an approximately Galactic CO conversion factor.

quotes.quote: The highest central Q_ gas are found in NGC 628 and NGC 5055 ( Q 8 ).
The mass accretion rate is about constant 0.1 M _ yr ^ 1 in NGC 3627 and NGC 5055.
Whereas NGC 628 and NGC 3184 show a CO conversion factor about twice as high as the Galactic value, those of NGC 3627 and NGC 5055 are about Galactic.

sources.summary: The paper calls NGC 5055 a prototypical flocculent spiral whose radio, CO, and ratio structures trace inner spiral arms while maintaining low dispersion.

quotes.quote: NGC 5055 . NGC 5055 is the prototypical flocculent spiral galaxy, which (like NGC 4414) was revealed to have regular spiral structure in K band imaging (Thornley 1996). At 6 \ resolution, the RC and the CO emissions appear nearly featureless, though an inspection of the CO image and especially the \ image (Figure figuraq ) show structures that follow very closely what appear to be inner spiral arms in Thornley's K band image. Nonetheless, the dispersion in \ is only 0.11.

gmw_00000459.name: Scl-MM-dw1
gmw_00000459.overview: Scl-MM-dw1 is a dwarf satellite galaxy of NGC 253 [arXiv:2108.09312]. It contains a small population of possible intermediate-age asymptotic giant branch stars [arXiv:2108.09312], but it has no detected neutral hydrogen gas [arXiv:2108.09312].

sources.summary: Scl-MM-dw1 is confirmed as an NGC 253 dwarf satellite with possible intermediate-age AGB stars, consistent HST/Magellan distances, measured structure, and no detected HI gas.

quotes.quote: In the ground-based Magellan imaging Sand14,Toloba16 , both Scl-MM-dw1 and Scl-MM-dw2 showed some evidence of a younger asymptotic giant branch (AGB) stellar population, which is confirmed by our deeper HST follow-up imaging. Figure  fig:cmd2 shows the AGB phase for isochrones with a range of ages and metallicities for both of these galaxies. Scl-MM-dw1 seems to contain only a handful of such luminous, possibly intermediate--age populations; these stars complicate its TRGB identification, resulting in a larger uncertainty in the TRGB value (F814W _ TRGB = 23.72 0.33  mag, see Section  sec:dist ). This galaxy does not seem to contain populations younger than 6-8  Gyr: the low stellar mass of Scl-MM-dw1 and the inherent stochasticity of the AGB phase make it difficult to constrain the amount of possible intermediate--age star formation.

Our results for Scl-MM-dw1 are in good agreement with those derived from Magellan + Megacam imaging Sand14 : we find r_h=18.8 1.8  arcsec with an ellipticity of 0.20 0.07 while their value is 16.8 2.4  arcsec with an ellipticity of < 0.42 .
Gas Limits Sand14 used data from the  Parkes All-Sky Survey (HIPASS; Barnes2001 ) to constrain the  content of Scl-MM-dw1, with no detection, and a 3-  gas mass upper limit of log(M_ HI /M_ ) 6.5 .

gmw_00000460.name: Scl-MM-dw2
gmw_00000460.overview: Scl-MM-dw2 is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy candidate located near the spiral galaxy NGC 253 [arXiv:1512.03815]. It exhibits a highly elongated shape and is likely undergoing tidal disruption [arXiv:2108.09312, arXiv:1512.03815], hosting both an old, metal-poor red giant branch and a younger asymptotic giant branch population [arXiv:2108.09312, arXiv:1512.03815].

sources.summary: Scl-MM-dw2 is an extended NGC 253 satellite with old RGB stars, a younger AGB population, likely tidal interaction, no detected HI, and unusually low density.

quotes.quote: The old RGB in Scl-MM-dw2 closely follows the isochrone of [ M/H ] =-1.5 (green line), which is what we would expect for its luminosity ( M_V=-12.1 ) based on the luminosity-metallicity relationship Kirby2013 . This implies that the stripping of stars in the satellite suggested by Toloba16 should be moderate. However, we note that the relationship has a lot of scatter; and given its large size and elongation, the object is likely undergoing a tidal interaction (see Section  sec:conclude ).

In Scl-MM-dw2, the presence of bright AGB stars stretching 1 magnitude above the TRGB requires young populations. The best fit stellar models suggest these stars are 1--2 Gyr old, and are somewhat more metal rich than the bulk of stars along the RGB.

However, Scl-MM-dw2 is a slight outlier: it is one of the most extended and least dense objects known at its luminosity. It is similar to Sagittarius in the Local Group and the recently discovered diffuse Virgo galaxies. It is likely undergoing a tidal interaction see discussion in Toloba16 , as it has a high ellipticity ( 0.66) and is elongated in the direction of NGC 253.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 253-dw2 as a newly found dwarf spheroidal candidate near NGC 253, with an old metal-poor RGB, elongated shape, low luminosity, and tidal disruption.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of NGC 253-dw2, a dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxy candidate undergoing tidal disruption around a nearby spiral galaxy, NGC 253 in the Sculptor group: the first such event identified beyond the Local Group. The dwarf was found using small-aperture amateur telescopes, and followed up with Suprime-Cam on the 8 m Subaru Telescope in order to resolve its brightest stars. Using g and R_ band photometry, we detect a red giant branch consistent with an old, metal-poor stellar population at a distance of 3.5 Mpc. From the distribution of likely member stars, we infer a highly elongated shape with a semi-major axis half-light radius of (2 0.4) kpc. Star counts also yield a luminosity estimate of 2 10^6 L_ ,V ( M_V 10.7 ).
The morphological properties of NGC 253-dw2 mark it as distinct from normal dSphs and imply ongoing disruption at a projected distance of 50 kpc from the main galaxy.
We conclude, based on the ellipticity and size of NGC 253-dw2, that it is experiencing tidal disruption.

gmw_00000461.name: Scl-MM-dw3
gmw_00000461.overview: Scl-MM-dw3 is a faint, diffuse, and elongated dwarf satellite galaxy [arXiv:2108.09312]. It is associated with the NGC 253 system and has also been independently identified as Donatiello II [arXiv:2108.09312].

sources.summary: Scl-MM-dw3 is one of three newly reported faint elongated NGC 253 satellites, independently matched to Donatiello II, with measured size, shape, and luminosity.

quotes.quote: Here we report the discoveries of three new faint, diffuse and elongated satellite galaxies, which we dub Scl-MM-dw3 During the preparation of this paper, we learned of an independent discovery of the same object using the Dark Energy Survey data DES, Martinez-Delgado21 . The authors named it Donatiello II. , Scl-MM-dw4 and Scl-MM-dw5, in accordance with our prior work in the Sculptor group. The locations and properties of these dwarfs are given in Figure  fig:position and Table  tab:dwarfs .

Compared to the structural parameters derived with integrated light from DES data r_h=5.5 0.4  arcsec, =0.39 0.04 , Martinez-Delgado21 , our ML analysis for Scl-MM-dw3 suggests a similar size ( r_h=6.6 1.8  arcsec) and a more elongated shape ( =0.57 0.12 ).
For Scl-MM-dw3, our luminosity measurement ( M_V=-7.24^ +0.26 _ 0.21 ) is consistent with the one reported in M_V=-7.04 0.2 Martinez-Delgado21 , within the uncertainties.
